<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Flame Drake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2959)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Flame drakes dwell near volcanoes and magma, but it's not unheard of for one to drift into nearby areas like forests or wooded hills. Their scales are usually some shade of red, occasionally fading to smoky blacks and grays along the edges of their wings and the tips of their tails.

 Rampages of flame drakes often grudgingly live alongside clans of [fire giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=223), rather than be forced from their volcanic homes.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Flame_Drake.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Flame Drake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2959)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=581" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 129

**Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet, smoke vision

**Languages**
[Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** -1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair a flame drake's vision; they ignore [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from smoke.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 22 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +10 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 75

</row>

**Immunities**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10

**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** <actions string="Reaction" /> Fangs only.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +14,
**Damage** 2d8+5 piercing plus 1d6 fire

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +14 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d6+5 bludgeoning

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The flame drake makes two fangs Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

**Fireball Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The flame drake expels a ball of flame to a range of 180 feet that explodes in a 20-foot burst. Creatures in the burst take 6d6 fire damage (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The flame drake can't use Fireball Breath again for 1d6 rounds.

**Speed Surge** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The flame drake Strides or Flies twice.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Jungle Drake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2960)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Believed to be related to [horned dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=401), jungle drakes are dangerous hunters equipped with a debilitating venom delivered by a large barbed stinger or their noxious phlegm. Their wings are equipped with vestigial claws that allow them to deftly maneuver through thick jungle foliage both in flight and on foot. Jungle drakes prefer to ambush their prey using hit-and-run tactics, picking off the weakest members of a group and dragging their victims off to finish their meals as they please. Rampages of jungle drakes will often drag prey in many directions to divide pursuit.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Jungle_Drake.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Jungle Drake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2960)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=581" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 130

**Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** -1

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +1
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 23 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 90

</row>

**Immunities**
[disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Twisting Tail** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature within reach of the jungle drake's stinger uses a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action or leaves a square during a move action they're using; **Effect** The jungle drake Strikes the target with their stinger. If it hits, the jungle drake [disrupts](/Rules.aspx?ID=394) the creature's action.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 50 feet; forest passage

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +17,
**Damage** 2d10+7 piercing plus Predatory Grab

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
stinger +17 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d6+7 piercing plus jungle drake venom

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The jungle drake makes one fangs Strike and two stinger Strikes in any order.

**Forest Passage**   The jungle drake ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) caused by plants, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth. Even plants manipulated by magic don't impede their progress.

**Jungle Drake Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 24 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison and enfeebled 2 (1 round)

**Predatory Grab** <actions string="Single Action" />  As [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45), but the jungle drake's Grab doesn't end if they move away. Instead, they carry the [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) creature with them. A jungle drake can't [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) while grabbing a creature unless that creature can also Fly.

**Speed Surge** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The jungle drake Strides or Flies twice.

**Spit Venom** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) A jungle drake can spit a sticky glob of their venom to a range of 50 feet that explodes in a 10-foot burst. Those in the burst must succeed at a DC 24 Reflex save or be exposed to jungle drake venom. The jungle drake can't use Spit Venom again for 1d6 rounds.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-404" />
name: Jungle Drake

markdown:
A wyvern is a venomous drake with a well-earned reputation for impatience and aggression. As much as 15 feet long and weighing up to 1,000 pounds, a wyvern's resilient body allows them to crash talons-first into large prey without serious risk to themself. A wyvern uses their momentum to stun their target before injecting it with searing venom or carrying it over the side of a nearby cliff. Because a wyvern lacks the strength to haul their prey all the way to their nest intact, they're far more likely to lift and drop their victim over a gully or canyon and let gravity do its work before they descend to pick apart the carcass. Conversation is of little interest to a wyvern, as the creature typically speaks only to taunt their prey, issue territorial claims, or demand tribute. Even so, many wyverns enjoy grim humor and tales of violent acts, particularly if those acts were committed by the storyteller. A wyvern properly appeased with meat, entertainment, and treasure sometimes agrees to provide assistance ranging from giving directions to serving as a mount for a powerful humanoid. However, these arrangements rarely last more than a few weeks before the wyvern's pride, malice, or insolence inspires them to flee or even betray their allies. Only the truly cruel can cow a wyvern into servitude for an extended period, as most wyverns are so self-interested that they go out of their way to avoid helping others. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Wyvern](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2961) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 131 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +12 **Will** +13 **HP** 95 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**Savage** **Trigger** A creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by the wyvern critically fails a skill check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296); **Effect** The wyvern makes a stinger Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** fangs +17, **Damage** 2d12+5 piercing **Melee** claw +17, **Damage** 2d8+5 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** stinger +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+5 piercing plus wyvern venom **Powerful Dive** ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The wyvern Flies up to their fly Speed and must both move forward at least 20 feet and descend at least 10 feet. If they end the movement within melee reach of at least one enemy their size or smaller, they can make a claw Strike against that enemy. If the claw hits, as a free action, the wyvern can either automatically [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target or knock it [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **Punishing Momentum** **Requirements** The wyvern grabbed a creature this turn using Powerful Dive; **Effect** The wyvern can [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) at half Speed while holding the creature in their claws, carrying that creature along with them and dropping it at the end of their movement. Alternatively, the wyvern can Strike the creature with their stinger with a +2 circumstance bonus. **Wyvern Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d6 poison damage (1 round)
Are Wyverns Drakes? Although commonly classified as drakes, wyverns exhibit significant differences from most other types of drakes. While scholars debate the precise relationship between them, none dispute that they exhibit collegial behavior and general deference to one another.
name: Wyvern

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Frost Drake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2962)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Frost drakes pose an immense danger in the frozen reaches they call home, where they roam far and wide to hunt for prey such as caribou, wolves, small bears, tundra-dwelling people, and even lone [frost giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=222). These drakes take advantage of long distances to hide from the repercussions of their actions, as they are usually among the most depraved and openly malicious of the drakes. They are also especially insolent and are less likely to back down from a fight compared to other drakes. Many frost drakes have met their ends trying to enact cruelties beyond their means, such as singly taking on a frost giant clan or well-fortified township.

 Frost drakes hunt alone more often than other drakes, due to a scarcity of prey and simple temperament. Although a single frost drake can wreak much destruction on their own, tales of village-dwelling northerners banding together to defend their homes from these rogue menaces are fairly common. A frost drake's hunting grounds are quite large. Those frost drakes that live upon the permanently frozen peaks of high mountains have been known to swoop down to snatch up prey from the lowlands, leaving frozen swaths of earth from their breath as the only sign of their passing. Their frost-covered scales range from deep royal blue to crisp cyan and sometimes

 feature sporadic patches of violet. Their hides are thinner than most drakes'; when a frost drake is inhaling in preparation to launch their signature frozen breath, the monster's ice-blue blood can be seen beneath their scales.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Frost_Drake.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=581" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 132

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet, snow vision

**Languages**
[Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** -1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Snow Vision** Snow doesn't impair a frost drake's vision; they ignore [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from snowfall.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 115

</row>

**Immunities**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10

**Retaliatory Strike** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature within reach of the frost drake's tail successfully damages the frost drake with a Strike; **Effect** The frost drake attempts to Strike with their tail. If the Strike hits, it deals an additional 1d6 damage.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet; burrow 20 feet (snow only), climb 20 feet (ice only), fly 50 feet; ice stride

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +17,
**Damage** 2d12+8 piercing plus 1d6 cold

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +17 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+8 bludgeoning

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The frost drake makes two fangs Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

**Freezing Mist Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The frost drake spits a ball of liquid up to 60 feet that explodes into a 20-foot-burst cloud of freezing mist. Those in the burst take 8d6 cold damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The frost drake can't use Freezing Mist Breath again for 1d6 rounds, during which the mist cakes all surfaces in the area with a sheet of slippery ice that turns the area into [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453).

**Ice Stride**   A frost drake isn't impeded by [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) caused by snow or ice, nor do they need to attempt [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to keep from falling on slippery ice.

**Speed Surge** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The frost drake Strides or Flies twice.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Desert Drake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2963)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These distant cousins of creatures like [adamantine dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=396)are a pale echo that lacks their relatives' power and intelligence. Desert drakes are nonetheless dangerous ambush predators, preying upon isolated desert travelers and outposts for food and supplies. Desert drakes' scales range in coloration from rust-brown to light tan and ocher shades, mimicking the colors of the dunes they call home.

 Like [frost drakes](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2962), a rampage of desert drakes often claims a huge span of territory, watching over an expanse of desert from a perch in nearby mountains or a nest among weathered ruins. Any creature visible on the surface is liable to be targeted by the drakes, especially humanoid caravans. Rampages along trade routes quickly learn to leave a member watching over each oasis for new prey. During especially lean times, the drakes will even dig out subterranean prey like [ankhravs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=361), [giant scorpions](/Monsters.aspx?ID=356), or entire nests of [giant ants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2824).

 Desert drakes are among the lightest and most compact of the drakes, though this shouldn't be mistaken for frailty. Their swooped-back horns and feather-thin wings are adapted to make burrowing as easy as possible. Indeed, a desert drake's powerful neck makes wriggling through sand and other loose scree as easy as walking for it.

Creatures in the area take 9d6 slashing damage (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The desert drake can't use Sandstorm Breath again for 1d6 rounds, during which the sandstorm lingers in the area. This lingering sandstorm grants [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62)to everything within it and conceals everything outside from them.

**Speed Surge** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The desert drake Strides or Flies twice.

**Surprise Attacker**   On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the desert drake.

</column>

markdown:
Riding on a horse as black as night, the headless hunter known as the dullahan tracks down and takes the heads of those they deem unfit to continue living. When closing in for the kill, the dullahan first whispers their victim's name, then swiftly collects their prize, casting a pall of dread upon all who witness the grim execution. A dullahan manifests when a particularly violent warrior is beheaded and the warrior's soul stubbornly clings to material existence (or is refused entry to the afterlife). Most dullahans return to their former homelands, where they can exact vengeance on those they feel wronged them in life (or their living descendants). A dullahan's concept of justice is swift and merciless, and once they've selected a target, they're unwavering in their cause. Perhaps even more than revenge, a dullahan desires their own rotted head. An individual who wields the head of a dullahan is powerful indeed, for a dullahan will grudgingly serve such a master in the hopes of reclaiming their missing skull. Mighty fiends such as [devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=389) command dullahans to harvest souls or lead armies for them, while a mortal might use such an undead warrior to fulfill a personal vendetta. A dullahan won't hesitate to kill their liege and reclaim their head when the opportunity presents itself. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Dullahan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2964) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 134 **Perception** +14; [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +15 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13), _+1 [hatchet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=382)_, _+1 [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)_ --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +15 **Will** +17 **HP** 95 ([fast healing 5](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62)) **Immunities** bleed, [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 30 feet, DC 23
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _keen longsword_ +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d8+10 slashing **Melee** _keen returning hatchet_ +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 1d6+10 slashing **Melee** fist +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning **Ranged** _keen returning hatchet_ +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing **Head Hunter** Any slashing weapon gains the [_keen_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2843) rune while a dullahan wields it, and any [hatchet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=382) they wield gains the [_returning_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2845) rune as well. If the dullahan kills a creature with a critical hit using a slashing weapon, the target is decapitated as though the dullahan had used Reap on the target. **Reap** The dullahan removes the head of a dead creature within reach. Each creature within the area of the dullahan's frightful presence must attempt a new save, even if it is temporarily immune. **Summon Steed** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dullahan summons a [war horse with elite adjustments](/Monsters.aspx?Elite=true&ID=268) and the [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) and [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) traits. This steed remains until it is slain, the dullahan Dismisses this effect, or the dullahan Summons a Steed again.
Dullahan Legends In Ustalav, residents recite legends of a cadre of dullahans who drive the “Coach of the Silent,” a macabre funeral carriage pulled by a team of spectral horses. To the west, among the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, Ulfen warriors whisper of dullahans who hunt down their victims using packs of hellish ebony hounds. And along the western coasts of Garund, pirates and merchants alike insist on the existence of a black-sailed ship crewed entirely by headless undead sailors.
name: Dullahan

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dwarf Stonecaster](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2966)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

All dwarves share a connection to the earth, but stonecasters have focused and polished that connection. Their years of meditation have granted them the ability to create boulders and shake the very earth. These elemental specialists are often highly respected in their community, which prizes their art of geomancy. Young dwarves who show a stronger than normal connection to the earth might be sent to a stonecaster to train at an early age.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dybbuk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2967)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The discorporated spirits called dybbuks arise from souls denied respite in the afterlife, often because they committed some great transgression in life. These spirits are said to cling to life through possessing victims to escape the punishment awaiting them in the afterlife. Only by trapping, cowing, or destroying such spirits can a mortal hope to drive them back.

 Dybbuks, who are most often the spirits of men, hide within possessed victims. Their targets of possession are usually young women on the eve of their wedding nights, which can be seen as an omen of an ill-fitting match, particularly in arranged marriages. Male and nonbinary victims do exist as well, though in lesser quantities, and also often on the eve of a troubled marriage. No one knows for sure why dybbuks are drawn to such situations, though it may be because the transgressive soul of a dybbuk seeks to bring chaos to the most strictly regimented aspects of society.

  A victim possessed by a dybbuk might be loud and crude, refuse to eat or drink, perform profane acts, or otherwise stir up trouble in the victim's household. The possessed victim has no memory of these events after their possession ends.

 Certain classes of specially trained priests can expel dybbuks from their victims through exorcism. Typically, this uses a methodology combining smoke, the blowing of a horn from a ram, and the recitation of holy verses. Exorcisms are, however, extremely difficult and dangerous, and require great knowledge and skill on the part of the priest due to the power of these malevolent spirits.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 36

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 36

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31
</column>

</column>

([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83))

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95)

**Resistances**
all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), DC 33) 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ghostly hand +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 3d10+14 void plus 2d6 spirit

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 36, attack +30
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)
- **3rd**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (at will)
- **4th**
[Rewrite Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655)
- **5th**
[Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1464) (×2), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524)
- **6th**
[Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [Never Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717) (at will)

**Malevolent Possession** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=671)) The dybbuk attempts to possess an adjacent corporeal creature. This has the same effect as the [_possession_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1638) spell (DC 34) with an unlimited duration, except since the dybbuk doesn't have a physical body, they aren't [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), and aren't [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) when the effect ends, though they take 5d6 spirit damage if the body is knocked unconscious or killed. If the dybbuk took control of the target with Malevolent Possession, when the dybbuk departs, the target has only incoherent memories of the interval it was possessed.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Cruel Puppet Masters</title>

Dybbuks revel in tricking mortals and using their telekinetic abilities to sow fear in the hearts of those around them. They create chaos in the households of their victims using their innate magical abilities. Victims possessed by dybbuks are often controlled indefinitely, until the dybbuk becomes bored with them or until some brave soul finds a way to release the victim from their torment.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Eagle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2969)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

No mere animals, giant eagles have a keen intellect and a strong sense of honor. As guardians of their mountain homes, giant eagles attempt to prevent the encroachment of civilization upon wild land and the predations of wicked humanoid settlements. Giant eagles congregate within aeries holding up to a dozen members and work together to protect their domains.

 Giant eagles have wingspans up to 30 feet across and weigh up to 500 pounds. A giant eagle may allow a trusted friend to ride it, but they invariably resist saddles, harnesses, or other equipment that might suggest they are mere beasts of burden. Long-lived, they take debts and oaths very seriously, often remembering slights for years and remaining slow to forgive.

<column gap="tiny">

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

**Eagle Dive** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The giant eagle [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to double its fly Speed in a straight line, descending at least 10 feet, and then makes a talon Strike.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Allies in the Skies</title>

Aeries of giant eagles can make potent allies to those who respect their territories and approach without malice in their hearts. Giant eagles are just as likely to swoop in and provide unsolicited aid to those in the lowlands of their realm against obvious dangers, but if not offered respect in turn, the eagles may abandon an unpleasant victim to its fate rather than suffer more insults.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Elananx](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2972)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These strange, fey felines resemble large, broad bobcats from a distance, but a closer view reveals something amiss. Their forms ripple and billow with heat, and their eyes glow from within as if they contained tiny, flickering flames. The pungent scent of rotting leaves smoldering in a bonfire clings to their fur. Yet those who have the chance to watch elananxes hunt or attack prey witness the greatest indication that these creatures are something more than mere predators, for they act with cruel and savvy instincts, reveling in the pain they inflict.

 Elananxes typically hunt alone, but sometimes these cunning and malicious hunters of the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35) roam in packs called billows to take down large prey. Like many house cats, elananxes are not content to merely track and devour prey, but prefer to toy with their victims, drawing joy from the fear and pain of those they capture. To this end, elananxes rarely use their cinder dispersal ability to evade their targets, instead opting to foil their quarry just before the end of the hunt—though, as selfish creatures who wish to live to hunt again, elananxes often reserve one use of this ability, just in case.

 Because of their clever and malicious ways, elananxes are favored as hunting companions by [redcaps](/Monsters.aspx?ID=349), who go out of their way to befriend or make deals with these creatures. Redcaps also find great sport in hunts where competing elananxes chase a single creature. Although they're large enough to serve as mounts for redcaps, elananxes despise being ridden and resist such attempts—little is as sure to cause a supposedly friendly elananx to turn on its redcap ally as a foolhardy attempt to treat the fey cat as a horse!

 Elananxes have a strange affinity with forest fires. Because they are immune to the damage caused by flickering flames, they enjoy capering and caterwauling through the smoky, burning ruins of forest infernos. Some have even been known to use their burning bites to deliberately light undergrowth on fire, simply so they might experience the beauty of the flames combined with the inevitable pain such disasters inflict on other creatures.

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

**Cinder Dispersal** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The elananx takes damage from a hostile source; **Effect** The elananx disperses into a cloud of smoke and cinders, filling its space and a 20-foot emanation. While in this form, the elananx can't be attacked or targeted, and it doesn't take up space. Anything inside this cloud is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and any creature ending its turn there takes 2d6 fire damage. At the start of its turn, the elananx returns to its normal form in any square the cloud covered. If the elananx Strikes a creature using its first action after returning to its normal form, the target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) and the Strike deals an extra 1d6 fire damage.

</column>

**Damage** 2d6+8 slashing

**Pack Attack**   The elananx's Strikes deal an extra 1d6 damage to creatures within the reach of at least two of its allies.

**Pounce** <actions string="Single Action" />  The elananx Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If the elananx began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), it remains hidden until after the attack.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Hunting Grounds</title>

Although many elananxes dwell in the strange realm of the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35), some are also natives of [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). Elananxes prefer to dwell in regions where there are ample intelligent creatures to chase, hunt, and eat, and they favor woodlands and hills as their primary hunting grounds.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Phade](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2975)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

On the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=29), and throughout the Inner Sphere, phades serve as spies and assassins for their creators. Even when summoned into the mortal [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), phades are generally called for unsavory and violent purposes, giving the phades that have turned their back on their creator a poor opinion of mortals as well.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Phade](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2975)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=527" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=587" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 140

**Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +6

**Con** +3

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 26 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +18 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 70

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Naturally Invisible** The phade is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) at all times, though when it takes a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) of any kind, it is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) instead of [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) until the start of its next turn, as the vague outline of its humanoid form is faintly visible for a short period of time.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d10+5 bludgeoning

**Hush** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The phade calms the air in a 30-foot emanation until the beginning of its next turn, reducing sounds in it to a whisper that can't be heard outside the emanation. This doesn't prevent casting spells, but a phade attempts to counteract any [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) effect originating in the area with a +17 [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) modifier. If the counteract attempt fails, Hush ends early.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The phade deals 2d6 extra precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-408" />
name: Phade

Elemental hurricanes embody the ferocity of violent windstorms. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Elemental Hurricane](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2976) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 141 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +22 **Str** +6 **Dex** +7 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +24 **Will** +18 **HP** 140 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **High Winds** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 40 feet. Air within the emanation is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) for [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) creatures that don't have the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527) trait.
**Disperse** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527)) **Trigger** The elemental hurricane takes damage from a hostile action; **Effect** The elemental hurricane disperses. Until the end of the current turn, it can't be attacked or targeted, doesn't take up space, and its high winds aura is suppressed. At the end of the turn, the elemental hurricane reforms in any unoccupied space within 100 feet of where it dispersed, and its high winds are restored. --- **Speed** fly 100 feet; swiftness **Melee** gust +24 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+12 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) 10 feet **Ranged** lightning lash +24 ([range increment 75 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d12+6 electricity **Gale Breath** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527)) The elemental exhales a 30-foot cone of air. Creatures in the cone must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save or be [knocked away](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) from the elemental. A creature knocked into a solid object stops moving and takes 10d6 bludgeoning damage (roll the damage once for all creatures). The elemental hurricane can't use Gale Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is pushed 20 feet.
**Failure** The creature is pushed 40 feet.
**Critical Failure** The creature is pushed 40 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **Swiftness** The elemental's movement doesn't trigger reactions.
name: Elemental Hurricane

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sod Hound](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2977)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Sod hounds are mossy extraplanar canines formed of packed dirt and pebbles. On their native plane, they are often tasked with guarding less secure sites and finding mineral deposits. Others live in the comparative lap of luxury as pets to [jabali](/Monsters.aspx?ID=213).

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=585" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=587" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 142

**Perception** +9; crystal sense ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +11,
**Damage** 1d10+6 piercing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46)

**Earth Glide**   The sod hound can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through any earthen matter, including rock. When it does so, the sod hound moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Living Landslide](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2978)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Living landslides resemble humanoids made of earth and gravel. They find moving across the many surfaces of the mortal [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) strangely liberating.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=585" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=587" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 142

**Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** -1

**Con** +4

**Int** -2

**Wis** +1

**Cha** -1
</row>

**Earthbound** When not touching solid ground, the living landslide is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) and can't use reactions.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +10 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 90

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Crumble <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The living landslide takes damage from a hostile source while atop rock or earth** **Effect** The living landslide crumbles into the ground, [Burrowing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310)down 10 feet. This Burrowing does not trigger reactions. The living landslide can't Crumble again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 25 feet; earth glide

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +16 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d8+8 bludgeoning

**Earth Glide**   The elemental can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through any earthen matter, including rock. When it does so, the elemental moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.

**Sliding Earth** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The living landslide Strides up to twice its normal Speed in a straight line, then attempts to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) a creature in its reach. If a creature falls prone from this Trip, it takes 1d4 bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet the living landslide moved.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-409" />
name: Living Landslide

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Cinder Rat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2981)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These oversized rodents are made of smoldering charcoal and elemental fire, and noxious fumes continually bellow from their flaming flesh. Even other fire elementals find cinder rats unpleasant and are glad when they're summoned away from the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=31).

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

bleed, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 5, [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) 5

A creature that enters the aura or begins its turn there must succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91). Everything within the aura, including the cinder rat, is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) by smoke.

Walking conflagrations of unimaginably hot fire, elemental infernos are harbingers of destruction and heedless chaos. More tactical than many elementals, the infernos will set entire cities ablaze to confuse enemies. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Elemental Inferno](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2984) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 145 **Perception** +20; darkvision, smoke vision **Languages** [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21 **Str** +6 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Smoke Vision** The elemental inferno ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +23 **Will** +19 **HP** 210 (explosion) **Immunities** bleed, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 15, [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) 10 **Explosion** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) When the elemental inferno dies, it explodes, dealing 7d6 fire damage to each creature in a 10-foot emanation (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).
**[Intense Heat](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=53)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) 10 feet, 7d6 fire, DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex --- **Speed** 70 feet **Melee** tendril +24 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+12 fire plus 3d8 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** fire mote +24 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10+6 fire **Blue Flames** When the elemental inferno scores a critical hit, its body surges with blue flames, increasing the damage of its intense heat and Inferno Leap by 3d6 until the start of its next turn. **Inferno Leap** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The elemental inferno jumps horizontally and vertically with a maximum height and distance each equal to its Speed. Its intense heat is suppressed until the end of the jump. Instead, at any point during the jump, flames explode from the elemental in a 30-foot emanation, dealing 12d6 fire damage to each creature within the area (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The elemental inferno can't Inferno Leap again for 1d4 rounds.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Earth Scamp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2986)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An earth scamp's rocky exterior could camouflage it well among loose rocks if not for the scamp's large eyes and bat-like wings. Earth scamps can fly, but the act of flight is uncomfortable and unnerving to them—they rarely leave the ground if they can help it. In fact, most earth scamps would rather never visit the surface at all, staying nestled deeply within the rocks they resemble.

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Although arguably quite friendly, fire scamps are considered far more dangerous than their kin. They delight in fire and playing pranks on everyone they befriend. Even given time to understand others' dislike of fire, most fire scamps enjoy the feel of flames enough to constantly test their limits. Compared to the nations of other scamps, the nations on the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=31) are by far the strongest. This backing inspires fire scamps to challenge authority more quickly than the others.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Water Scamp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2988)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Water scamps are marked apart from other scamps by sleek fur that traps a layer of water next to their skin. Although they can fly like their kin and conjure enough water to never dry out, water scamps leave the water only when they must. Although air-breathing scholars consider these scamps quiet and rather skittish, underwater civilizations find their exuberance and playfulness can be overwhelming.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Quatoid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2991)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Quatoids are peculiar elementals native to the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=32) that resemble four-tentacled octopuses with eerily humanoid faces on their mantles. Mysterious even to other elementals, quatoids nevertheless respond to mortal summons. They assist these mortals in combat but seem to prefer offering calm advice, looking for alternate solutions even in the midst of bloody conflict.

 With the return of [their elemental lord](/Deities.aspx?ID=105) from her long imprisonment, quatoids are slowly becoming more active on the Plane of Water. However, much of their activity still seems to be centered on the mortal realm.

<column gap="tiny">

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Mammoth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2994)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Found mostly in colder climates, mammoths are accustomed to dealing with desperate and dangerous predators, trampling and crushing their enemies while using their larger tusks to greater effect. Humanoids dwelling in harsh, frost-bound lands rely on the mammoth's strength to help them survive.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Grothlut](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2997)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Sluglike abominations, grothluts are grotesque dregs of the fleshwarping process. While their head and torso are vaguely humanoid, their arms are rubbery and move awkwardly at their sides. Wretched creatures, they moan piteously when other creatures are near, perhaps as the last remnants of their mind pleads to be free from their horrid warped form.

 Many fleshwarpers consider the grothlut to be a failure of a creation, as the transformation all but stamps out consciousness. Others disagree, arguing that warping the creature's mind makes it all the more useful, since it becomes pliable and easy to herd. Cultists of [Haagenti](/Deities.aspx?ID=260) typically use grothluts as guardians that slowly patrol the edges of their enclaves. Once in position, grothluts can be used as crude shock troops, unleashed to soften enemy forces before more valuable warriors wade in and cut down the enemies who have been nauseated by the grothluts' exploded organs and flesh.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Grothlut.webp" />
</column>

</row>

**Disgusting Demise** ([acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) When the grothlut is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its digestive organs rupture, unleashing alchemical acid and poison upon all creatures in a 30-foot emanation. Each creature in the area must succeed at a DC 19 Fortitude save or take 2d6 acid damage and become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) (double damage and sickened 2 on a critical failure).

**Piteous Moan** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 60 feet. Each non-grothlut creature that enters or starts its turn within the area must succeed at a DC 17 Will saving throw or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The creature then becomes temporarily immune for 1 minute. The grothlut can Dismiss this aura. A grothlut usually does not begin moaning until it senses the presence of a non-grothlut creature, and it usually stops once it doesn't sense any more such creatures.

</column>

Irnakurse are believed to be [elves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2) who have been subjected to particularly cruel and humiliating fleshwarping practices, though they are so corrupted by their transformation it is nearly impossible to tell. The process of crafting an irnakurse twists the unfortunate elf into a mass of misplaced limbs, loose flesh, and bony protrusions—parts that should be internal are often left on full display to the world. These beings periodically crawl to the surface from the deepest reaches of the Darklands, leading some elven scholars to the gruesome suspicion that these are remnants of the elves who traveled underground in order to escape Earthfall. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Irnakurse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2998) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 153 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +20 **Str** +5 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +18 **Will** +16 **HP** 152 --- **Speed** 15 feet **Melee** jaws +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+11 piercing **Melee** tentacle +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+11 slashing plus mind lash **Mind Lash** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) A creature hit by an irnakurse's tentacle is overwhelmed with corrupted images of a ruined life and must succeed at a DC 28 Will save or be [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) (or stunned 4 on a critical failure). After attempting this save, the creature is temporarily immune to mind lash for 24 hours. **Soul Scream** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The irnakurse unleashes an alien shriek of nightmarish horror and pain. All non-[aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=523) creatures within a 10-foot emanation must attempt a DC 28 Will save. The irnakurse can Sustain Soul Scream for up to 6 rounds; each time it does, it repeats the effect without a new save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected, and it's temporarily immune to Soul Scream for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is stupefied 1. Further failed saves against Soul Scream increase the stupefied value by 1, to a maximum of stupefied 4. Each time the character gets a full night's rest, the stupefied condition gained from Soul Scream decreases by 1.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the stupefied value increases by 2 instead of by 1. **Storm of Tentacles** The irnakurse makes up to four tentacle Strikes, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the irnakurse's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all of its attacks.
name: Irnakurse

**Quick Capture** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature hits or touches the flytrap; **Effect** The flytrap makes a leaf Strike against the triggering creature. If it hits, the creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) in that leaf.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
leaf +11 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d8+2 piercing plus 1d6 acid and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66)

**Focused Assault** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The flytrap attacks a single target with both its two leaves. The flytrap makes one leaf Strike. On a success, the flytrap deals the damage from one leaf Strike plus an additional 1d8 damage for every leaf beyond the first. On a failure, the flytrap deals the damage from one leaf Strike, but it can't use Improved Grab. It deals no damage on a critical failure. This counts toward the flytrap's multiple attack penalty as a number of attacks equal to the number of leaves the flytrap has.

**Hungry Flurry** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The flytrap makes two leaf Strikes at a –2 penalty, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the flytrap's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all its attacks.

**Focused Assault** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The flytrap attacks a single target with all four of its leaves. The flytrap makes one leaf Strike. On a success, the flytrap deals the damage from one leaf Strike plus an additional 1d8 damage for every leaf beyond the first. On a failure, the flytrap deals the damage from one leaf Strike, but it can't use Improved Grab. It deals no damage on a critical failure. This counts toward the flytrap's multiple attack penalty as a number of attacks equal to the number of leaves the flytrap has.

**Hungry Flurry** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The flytrap makes four leaf Strikes at a –2 penalty, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the flytrap's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all its attacks.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Gargoyle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3001)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Gargoyles are monstrous hunters made of elemental stone. They use their resemblance to decorative statues to hide in plain sight in cities during the day and descend upon unlucky pedestrians at night. Their most common form is that of a horned humanoid with bat-like wings, but individual gargoyles show a great deal of variation, with some appearing more or less humanoid and others resembling no known creature. A gargoyle's features are not fixed; city-dwelling gargoyles who remain in the same locale long enough slowly morph, day by day, to match the style of the local architecture. These patient monsters can stay disguised for long stretches of time as they patiently await an opportunity to strike.

 Gargoyles tend to be lone hunters, though sometimes they band into fearsome groups called wings for protection or sport. On rare occasions, wings become relatively stable communities, and gargoyles in wings may even ally with other creatures such as [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) and intelligent [aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=523), though these alliances exist on a razor's edge. The majority of gargoyles are treacherous, vindictive, and petty—traits that preclude lasting partnerships. Almost all have some subject they spend days mulling over while in statue form. Some are collectors, focusing on anything from books to grim trophies, while others are ritualistic or overly passionate about niche intellectual subjects or certain artistic motifs. These tendencies often contribute to the dissolution of wings as individuals with conflicting focuses clash. 

<title level="2" right="">Sanctified Roosts</title> In addition to their appearances matching their environment, gargoyles that position themselves on temples or other consecrated ground slowly succumb to the influence of that location. These gargoyles gain the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait, as do their unarmed Strikes. A gargoyle roosting on a temple of [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278), for example, will eventually gain the unholy trait along with changes to their personality that bring it more in line with the god's ethos. Holy gargoyles are also possible, although gargoyles at such locations often abandon their roosts as their changing personality conflicts with their need to hunt.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/Gargoyle.webp" />
</column>

</row>

**Statue** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) Until the next time it acts, the gargoyle appears to be a statue. It has an automatic result of 32 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks and DCs to pass as a statue.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Gargoyle Religion</title>

Although gargoyles roosting at a religious site eventually gravitate towards that god's ethos and frequently become followers, other gargoyle wings reconsecrate crumbling temples to their own gods. Most such wings hold up a demon lord or one of the lords of the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=30) as their creator and patron.
</aside>

All six elements build each jann and fill them with a constant desire to travel, typically jaunting about [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). The eldest among geniekind, they command respect from their distant offspring. Any jann can grant wishes, not just shuyookhs—a vestige of their ancient power. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Jann](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3002) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 156 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +10, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Items** [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (20 arrows), [Scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=38) --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +10 **Will** +13 **HP** 60 **Resistances** elemental resistance 5 **Commanding Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 20 feet. A creature that enters the aura must succeed at a DC 19 Will save or be [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (frightened 2 on a critical failure) and is then temporarily immune for 1 minute. A [genie](/Traits.aspx?ID=752) (with the exception of another jann) takes a –4 circumstance penalty to its save.
**Elemental Resistance** The jann's elemental resistance applies to cold, electricity, and fire damage, as well as all damage from elemental sources (including environmental damage from the elemental planes and damage from anything with the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait). --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 15 feet **Melee** scimitar +14 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 1d6+7 slashing plus All Made One **Melee** fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)), **Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning plus All Made One **Ranged** composite shortbow +12 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+5 piercing plus All Made One **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2) - **4th** [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to [Astral Plane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2832); Elemental Planes; or [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) only) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **All Made One** The jann calls upon all of the elements that make up their being to gain an additional arcane spell they can cast at will and empower their Strikes with the element, dealing an extra 1d4 damage of the listed type. These benefits last until the jann uses this ability again. **Air** [_tailwind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1711), 1d4 electricity; **Earth** [_pummeling rubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1642), 1d4 bludgeoning; **Fire** [_breathe fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457), 1d4 fire; **Metal** [_thunderstrike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1721), 1d4 electricity; **Water** [_hydraulic push_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1561), 1d4 bludgeoning; **Wood** [_summon plant or fungus_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1705), 1d4 piercing. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The jann transforms into any Small or Medium [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531). This doesn't affect their statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes. **Wanderer's Wish** **Frequency** three times per year; **Effect** The jann instantly grants the benefits of a critical success with the [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual to a mortal creature. This has no cost. That creature specifies what they wish for, but the interpretation is up to the jann. A jann typically attempts to grant wishes in a way that encourages growth and exploration. A summoned jann can't use this ability.
Jann Shuyookhs Jann shuyookhs add the following innate spells: **4th** [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2), [_read omens_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647).
name: Jann

Possessing all the subtlety and elegance of air itself, the jaathooms of the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=29) operate within dreams, nightmares, and time. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Jaathoom](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3003) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 157 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111); (can't speak any language); cloud of visions **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Cloud of Visions** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet. A jaathoom has [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 60 feet but can only show images, not speak. **Items** [Scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=38) --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +14 **Will** +11 **HP** 55 **Naturally Invisible** The jaathoom is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) at all times, though when they take a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) of any kind, they are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) instead of [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) until the start of their next turn, as the vague outline of their form is faintly visible for a short period of time.
**Turbulent Skies** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 20 feet. All squares in the emanation are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for Striding and Flying creatures. Creatures with the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527) trait are immune. The jaathoom can activate or deactivate this aura as a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** scimitar +15 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 1d6+10 slashing **Melee** fist +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning **Ranged** crashing wind +16 ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d8+8 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **3rd** [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566), [Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675) - **4th** [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615), [Vapor Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to [Astral Plane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2832); Elemental Planes; or [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) only) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The jaathoom transforms into a Small or Medium [air elemental](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=408) or aerial [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531), such as an owl. This doesn't affect their statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes. **Hurricane Blast** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The jaathoom moves all creatures without the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527) trait in their turbulent skies aura 20 feet directly away, clockwise, or counterclockwise. A creature avoids being moved if it succeeds at a DC 21 Fortitude save. **Ominous Dreams** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674)) The jaathoom sends a prophetic dream to a sleeping creature within 10 feet. An unwilling creature avoids the vision if it succeeds at a DC 23 Will save. The jaathoom chooses the dream's subject, but not its exact events. The target sees a brief vision of its future related to that subject, with the effect of [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445). If the result is bad or mixed, the creature is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) and can't recover from being frightened until it wakes.
Jaathoom Shuyookhs Jaathoom shuyookhs prefer to manifest wishes informed by their visions of the future. They add the following innate spells: **5th** [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [_illusory object_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569), [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615) (×2), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675) (×2); **4th** [_ill omen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566).

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Jabali](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3004)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The proud and brazen jabalis of the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=30) value physical skill and love bargaining, games of chance, and working with metal and stone.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Jabali](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3004)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=585" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=587" />
<trait label="Genie" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=752" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 158

**Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +1

**Con** +4

**Int** +3

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [falchion](/Weapons.aspx?ID=373)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 110

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 45 feet, climb 20 feet; earth glide

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_falchion_ +20 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 1d10+12 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d4+12 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) 10 feet and stone clutch

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 24
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
- **4th**
[Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1667) (at will)
- **5th**
[Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751)
- **7th**
[Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to [Astral Plane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2832); Elemental Planes; or [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) only)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Earth Glide**   The jabali can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through dirt and stone at their full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of their passing.

**Stone Clutch**  ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) When the jabali [Pushes](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) a creature into a stone barrier, the surface grips it with fingers of stone. The target must succeed at a DC 22 Reflex save or become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) by the surface ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 28).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Jabali Shuyookhs</title>

Jabali shuyookhs fulfill wishes as straightforwardly as possible. They add the following innate spells: **8th** [_earthquake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1507); **6th** [_mountain resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610) (at will; self only), [_petrify_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1628); **5th** [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568).
</aside>
