<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Faydhaan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3005)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The faydhaans of the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=32) are more powerful than the other genies dwelling on the elemental planes, but they prefer to forge alliances through diplomacy and flattery.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Faydhaan.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 9">[Faydhaan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3005)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=587" />
<trait label="Genie" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=752" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=732" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 158

**Perception** +16; (18 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=84) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +20, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +18, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +20, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +5

**Con** +2

**Int** +1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=401)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 28 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +18 

**Will** +18 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 145

</row>

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10

**Turbulent Seas** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) 40 feet. Water in the aura that is also in the same body of water as the faydhaan is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for [Swimming](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381)creatures. Creatures with the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) trait are immune.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, swim 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_trident_ +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d8+10 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_trident_ +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 2d8+10 piercing

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 24
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
- **2nd**
[Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476) (at will), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2), [Water Breathing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1755)
- **4th**
[Hydraulic Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=1561) (at will)
- **5th**
[Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473) (at will), [Hydraulic Torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) (at will)
- **7th**
[Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to [Astral Plane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2832); Elemental Planes; or [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) only)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The faydhaan transforms into a Small or Medium [water elemental](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=412), [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=533) [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531), or [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628). This doesn't affect their statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes.

**Gift of Hospitality** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The faydhaan gives another willing creature a magical gift or an agreeable conversation. The creature gains a +2 status bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) and [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks. A creature can't have more than one gift at a time, and a faydhaan can't grant more than one gift at a time. The gift ends if the target acts hostile, or if the faydhaan renounces the recipient (a single action).

**Skewer** <actions string="Single Action" />  The faydhaan makes a trident Strike, dealing an extra 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) on a hit (4d6 on a critical hit).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Faydhaan Shuyookhs</title>

Faydhaan shuyookhs grant wishes in ways that please the most people possible. They add the following innate spells: **7th** [_hydraulic torrent_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562), [_planar palace_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1634), [_summon elemental_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1698); **5th** [_howling blizzard_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559) (at will), [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567) (×2), [_mirage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604), [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) (at will); **4th** [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2), [_vapor form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738); **2nd** [_create water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476) (at will), [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663).
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-417" />
name: Faydhaan

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ifrit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3006)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The fierce and unforgiving ifrits hail from the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=31), where they build metropolises and trade centers that draw extraplanar travelers. Many ifrits are tyrannical or warmongering, and most use their might to accomplish their goals.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Ifrit.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 9">[Ifrit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3006)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=587" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Genie" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=752" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 159

**Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +19, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** +1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Items**
[Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=393)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 28 

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +20 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 175

</row>

**Immunities**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)

**Weaknesses**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10, [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_scimitar_ +21 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 2d6+11 slashing plus 2d6 fire

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d4+11 bludgeoning plus 2d6 fire

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 27, attack +19
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565)
- **4th**
[Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2)
- **7th**
[Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to [Astral Plane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2832); Elemental Planes; or [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) only)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Burning Grasp**  ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) When the ifrit [grabs](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77)or [restrains](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90)a creature that creature takes 2d6 fire damage and takes 2d6 fire damage at the end of each of its turns until freed.

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The ifrit transforms into a Small or Medium [fire elemental](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=410) or reptile, such as a snake. This doesn't affect their statistics but could change the damage type of their Strikes.

**Combat Grab** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The ifrit has a hand free; **Effect** The ifrit makes a melee Strike. If the Strike hits, the target is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) in the ifrit's free hand.

**Wings of Flame** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The ifrit grows flaming wings from their back. They gain a fly Speed of 35 feet for 1 minute. The flames also create an aura in a 5-foot emanation around the ifrit. Any creature that ends its turn in the aura takes 2d6 fire damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Ifrit Shuyookhs</title>

Ifrit shuyookhs twist the phrasing of wishes to maximize suffering. A wisher for a thousand pounds of gemstones might have the gems delivered as a bone-crushing avalanche. Ifrit shuyookhs add the following innate spells: **7th** [_volcanic eruption_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1746); **5th** [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) (at will).
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-417" />
name: Ifrit

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ghost Commoner](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3007)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The ghost commoner is an ordinary person who believes they died unjustly.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 19

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Ghost_Commoner.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Ghost Commoner](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3007)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Ghost" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=753" />
<trait label="Incorporeal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=632" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=737" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 161

**Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Dwelling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -5

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Site Bound** 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +8 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 30
(rejuvenation, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83))

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, unconscious

**Resistances**
all damage 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) Setting right the injustice that led to the commoner's death allows it to move on to the afterlife.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ghostly hand +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d6+2 void

**Frightful Moan** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) DC 21 The ghost laments its fate, forcing each living creature within 30 feet to attempt a Will save. On a failure, a creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 (or frightened 3 on a critical failure). On a success, a creature is temporarily immune to this ghost's frightful moan for 1 minute

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ghost Mage](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3008)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A wizard who died with work incomplete might become a ghost mage.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 27

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 27

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Ghost_Mage.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Ghost Mage](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3008)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Ghost" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=753" />
<trait label="Incorporeal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=632" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=737" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 161

**Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -5

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** +6

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Site Bound** 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +19 

**Will** +22 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 135
(rejuvenation, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83))

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, unconscious

**Resistances**
all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) Completing the ghost mage's project allows it to move on to the afterlife.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ghostly hand +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d8+12 void

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +23
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)
- **1st**
[Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513) (×2)
- **2nd**
[Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717) (×2)
- **3rd**
[Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1453), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Veil of Privacy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739)
- **4th**
[Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693), [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742)
- **5th**
[Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1551), [Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559)

**Frightful Moan** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The ghost laments its fate, forcing each living creature within 30 feet to attempt a DC 29 Will save. On a failure, a creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 (or frightened 3 on a critical failure). On a success, a creature is temporarily immune to this ghost's frightful moan for 1 minute.

**Telekinetic Assault** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The ghost cries out in pain and anguish as small objects and debris fly about in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Creatures in this area take 6d6 bludgeoning damage, with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Building Ghosts</title>

Note that the ghost mage is built from the ground up, rather than by applying the ghost rules to a once-living creature, so its numbers don't exactly match the values listed under Creating a Ghost. This is usually the better way to go if you have the time, as it allows you to hand-craft a ghost for the situation.
</aside>

markdown:
Dwellers of brackish coastal salt marshes and fetid bogs and swamps, marsh giants appear hideous to most airbreathers, with fishlike mouths, slimy gray-green skin, and dark, beady eyes. They prefer to eat the flesh of those they slay in battle, including other marsh giants, and guzzle stagnant water from cauldrons or directly from their swampy homes.
Marsh giant clans are insular, with each clan dedicating itself to zealous worship of a sea-dwelling deity, demon lord, or stranger entity. Whatever forces call to the marsh giants sometimes ensnare boggards and ogres as well, gathering in grim worship. Rather than favor these humanoid hangers-on, marsh giants lavish attention on favored pets: [krooths](/Monsters.aspx?ID=276), [giant octopuses](/Monsters.aspx?ID=315), or primeval creatures like [dinosaurs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=391) and the crocodilian [deinosuchuses](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2888). Barely topping 11 feet tall and 1,000 pounds, marsh giants are small for giants but make up for their stature with their zealotry. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Marsh Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3011) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 164 **Perception** +16; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +15, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [gaff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=169)_ --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 **HP** 150 --- **Speed** 35 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** _gaff_ +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d6+14 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+14 bludgeoning **Ranged** spit +20 ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)), **Damage** 5d6 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 23 - **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606) - **5th** [Mariner's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1593) **Drowning Hook** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Requirements** A creature is [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) within the marsh giant's reach; **Effect** The marsh giant uses its gaff to push the creature down as water bubbles up below it. The target becomes submerged in water until they are no longer prone and must [hold their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) if they cannot breathe water. They take 4d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save) and lose 3 rounds worth of air if they fail the save. **Twist the Hook** The marsh giant makes a melee Strike with its gaff. If it hits, it twists and yanks the gaff to knock the target [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) and create an awful wound, dealing 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to the creature.
Gaffs Many marsh giants fight with an oversized gaff—a length of wood with a metal hook affixed to the tip. The giants use them to drown prey as humanoid fishers use them to land fish. A gaff is a common martial weapon in the club group. It deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage and has 1 Bulk. It requires one hand to use and has the trip and versatile P weapon traits. Gaffs are readily available anywhere fishers live, costing 1 gp.
name: Marsh Giant

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Stone Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3012)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Stone giants are stoic, reclusive herders and artists with a rich history and collection of traditions. They dwell in caves in tall mountains and craggy ranges, where their grayish skin allows them to blend in with their surroundings and go unnoticed, despite being around 12 feet tall. Benign travelers who come across a clan of stone giants need not worry, however, for stone giants do not actively invite confrontation or strife. They are, by and large, peaceful people who seek wisdom through exploration of nature and long meditations on the elements of the natural world. Their elders are among the wisest of giants, using their charisma and druidic magic to lead their clans to prosperity and harmony with nature.

 A stone giant clan will usually raise a number of animals as pets, favoring [cave bears](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2851), [elephants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2993), or [dinosaurs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=391) depending on the environment. Many clans also accept allies, considering [arboreals](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [elementals](/Traits.aspx?ID=587), and even [gargoyles](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3001) as kin through their shared connection with the earth.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Stone_Giant.webp" />
</column>

</row>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 164

**Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** +0

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [greatclub](/Weapons.aspx?ID=377)_

</column>

---

**AC** 27

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

**Swat Projectile** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Requirements** The stone giant must have a free hand but can Release anything as part of this reaction; **Trigger** The giant is targeted by a physical ranged attack; **Effect** The stone giant gains a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the attack misses and the projectile was made of stone, the giant can throw it back at the attacker as a rock ranged Strike.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_greatclub_ +21 ([Backswing](/Traits.aspx?ID=545), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)),
**Damage** 2d10+12 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d6+14 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rock +18 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d6+12 bludgeoning

**Big Swing** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The stone giant makes a greatclub Strike. The target is [Pushed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) up to 10 feet on a hit or up to 20 feet on a critical hit. If the target collides with a solid object, it takes bludgeoning damage as though it had [fallen](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) the distance it moved.

**Create Boulder** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The stone giant molds a boulder from primal earth and throws it as a rock Strike. A creature hit by the Strike must succeed at a DC 26 Reflex save or be knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Frost Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3013)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Frost giants are remorseless marauders who pillage and plunder from those who dare to live near them in desolate, frigid lands. Their clans range from extremely territorial hunters who ferociously defend their expanse of tundra to nomadic families that roam icy slopes in search of settlements to conquer. Frost giant clans are ruled by the family member who exhibits the greatest ferocity and prowess in battle—often a massive bully who demands absolute obedience from the rest.

 Frost giants' appearance is reflective of their icy homes, with flesh ranging from a translucent glacial blue to a slushy gray. A typical frost giant stands about 15 feet tall and weighs approximately 2,800 pounds. They often wear metal armor adorned with the furs, skin, teeth, and tusks of slain beasts and heft weapons as long as dining tables. A well-stocked frost giant clan will raise [mammoth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2994) mounts or press witchwargs into service as hunting companions, but consider their environment too hostile for a soft concept like pets.

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

**Wide Swing** <actions string="Single Action" />  The frost giant makes a single greataxe Strike and compares the attack roll result to the ACs of up to two foes within their reach. This counts as two attacks for the frost giant's multiple attack penalty.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Fire Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3014)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The most militaristic of giants, fire giants focus obsessively on learning combat techniques, mastering the arts of forging weapons and armor, and finding new ways to dominate their enemies. Most fire giant communities are built around elemental rifts, hot springs, or volcanic calderas and cannot be moved easily, inspiring unwavering defense against any intruders. As a result, their social and political structures are grounded firmly in martial hierarchies, with a strict emphasis on following the orders of one's superior.

 As soon as a fire giant can walk, they are fitted with their first suit of forged armor. This armor is constantly remolded and replaced as the giant matures. Beyond what they can forge in their volcanic furnaces, fire giants also look to tame [dinosaurs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=391), [drakes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=404), and [hell hounds](/Monsters.aspx?ID=259) as tools of war.

 Fire giants are usually identified by their powerful stature and bright orange hair that flickers and dances as if aflame. A typical fire giant stands about 14 feet tall, weighs around 7,000 pounds, and lives to be 350 years old.

markdown:
The graceful and regal cloud giants are fractious, with roughly half their clans believing they should rule over humanoids regardless of their size and the rest shying away from any contact with outsiders. Due to the physical and ideological distance between clans, most rely on [giant eagle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2969) or [roc](/Monsters.aspx?ID=352) messengers, to arrange marriages and exchange art without setting foot in each other's territory. Cloud giants' skin color ranges from milky white to powdery blue. They make their homes anywhere veiled by the clouds, generally mountaintops or isolated valleys, but occasionally fog-shrouded swamps or misty rainforests. Legends persist of floating cities ruled by magically gifted cloud giant queens and kings. While most cloud giants plainly state that such claims are pure fantasy, others are mysteriously tight-lipped or evasive about the matter. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Cloud Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3015) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 167 **Perception** +22; cloudsight, [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +21 **Str** +7 **Dex** +0 **Con** +5 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Cloudsight** Cloud giants ignore [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62)from weather conditions, including clouds and rain. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [ranseur](/Weapons.aspx?ID=390)_ --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +18 **Will** +21 **HP** 220 **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _ranseur_ +25 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+13 piercing **Melee** fist +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+13 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 30 - **2nd** [Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606) (at will) - **3rd** [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1584) (at will) - **4th** [Vapor Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) **Crushing Cloud** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The cloud giant solidifies some clouds, including fog or mist, around a creature that's already in a cloud up to 120 feet away. The target takes 3d8 bludgeoning damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). If it fails its save, it treats clouds as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for 1 round. **Wind Strike** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The cloud giant Strikes a creature with their ranseur, surrounded in a roar of rushing air. On a hit, the target takes an additional 4d8 bludgeoning damage and is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 minute. Whether or not the Strike hits, each non-cloud giant within a 20-foot emanation, including the target of the Strike, is buffeted by roaring winds and must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude saving throw.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d8 sonic damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 4d8 sonic damage and is deafened until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but double damage and also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Shadow Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3016)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Shadow giants are natives of [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36), where they have dwelled in perpetual twilight for millennia. They live in familiar groups and uphold a nomadic way of life as they roam across ancestral lands between shadowy forests and misty chasms. These hunter-gatherers pass down lore through oral histories, conduct pilgrimages to unholy ziggurats of black stone, and bathe in the blood of their long-standing foes, including rival shadow giant clans and fiends of the Netherworld intent on enslaving their kind.

 Standing 15 feet tall, with gray skin and hair only a shade lighter, shadow giants are fearsome foes with a well-earned reputation as zealous warmongers and ruthless combatants. They rarely interact with outsiders, though they may treat with proven warriors who show the giants the respect and deference they feel they deserve.

markdown:
Rune giants are tyrants among their own kind, given power to command and magically control other giants. They once served even more powerful masters—potent wizards known as runelords—and in so doing, commanded entire armies of giants in service to the runelords' empires. In the eons since these empires collapsed, rune giants have persisted, though to the outside world they're little more than fabled horrors. Rune giants usually dwell in the most remote and rugged of towering mountain ranges, but they can also be found in immense ruins atop lost islands, glacial valleys, or even more remote or magical regions. Dozens of runes decorate rune giants' striking charcoal flesh. They are towering creatures, averaging at 40 feet in height and weighing 25,000 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Rune Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3017) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 169 **Perception** +28; fly 45 feet **Melee** _greatsword_ +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 3d12+17 slashing **Melee** _longspear_ +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+17 piercing **Melee** fist +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+17 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 35 - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (at will) - **5th** [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501) (×3), [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) - **8th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) - **Constant (7th)** [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) **Command Giants** When a rune giant casts a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) spell against another [giant](/Traits.aspx?ID=616), the DC is 39, rather than 35. **Demand** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) When a rune giant casts their innate [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665) spell, they can also cast [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) on the target. **Flashing Runes** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) **Trigger** The rune giant uses an arcane ability or casts an arcane spell; **Effect** The runes on the giant's body flash with magical energy. Each creature within a 10-foot emanation must attempt a DC 35 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round. **Invoke Rune** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)) The rune giant invokes one of the runes on their body, causing the rune to spray forth a 30-foot cone of sparks that deals 6d12 electricity damage to all creatures in the cone (DC 37 basic Reflex save). The giant can't use Invoke Rune again for 1d4 rounds.
A glowing copy of the invoked rune appears on a single weapon the giant holds, granting the weapon one effect listed below of the giant's choice. The effect on the weapon lasts for 1 minute. If the giant places a new rune on a weapon, any previously placed rune immediately vanishes, ending its effect.
  • **Rune of Destruction** The weapon gains the [deadly](/Traits.aspx?ID=570) trait with three weapon damage dice of the same die size as for the base weapon, and a creature hit with the weapon is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) unless it succeeds at a DC 35 Fortitude save.
  • **Rune of Flames** The weapon deals an additional 3d6 fire damage on all attacks.
  • **Rune of Smiting** When the weapon hits, the giant can [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) the target back 10 feet, or 20 feet on a critical hit.
  • **Rune of Space** During the rune giant's turn, the weapon's [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) is increased to 60 feet.
**Wide Swing** The rune giant makes a single greatsword Strike and compares the attack roll result to the ACs of up to two foes within their reach. This counts as two attacks for the giant's multiple attack penalty.
The First Rune Giants On Golarion, the first rune giants were created by the powerful rulers of the ancient nation of Thassilon. The runelords gifted rune giants their ability to control other giants, using them to command armies of oversized builders and soldiers to create and defend oversized structures.
name: Rune Giant

Gimmerlings are small, shapeshifting fey who stage ambushes to sate their endless hunger and childish greed. These cruelly curious fey obsess over finding and making unusual traps and sadistic weapons, and their favorite amusement is seeing these traps sprung or the weapons wielded. When residing on Golarion, they're frequently found in urban areas, particularly slums or other parts of town, where they can either go unnoticed or be easily forgotten—and have plenty of victims to choose from. A typical gimmerling disguises themself as an endangered child, hoping to draw creatures close enough to rob. The gimmerling puts themself in apparent danger using a trap, construct, a bribed ally, or even a coerced monster. Because gimmerlings sometimes trade obscure smithing or trapping techniques in exchange for gifts that sate their curiosity, their greed, or their hunger, they have at times been worshipped as minor gods of the forge. Some disciplined gimmerlings work as honored artisans, elite guards, or spies for the demigods known as the [Eldest](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=7), who dwell in the depths of the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35). **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Gimmerling](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3018) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 170 **Perception** +21; hungersense ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet, [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +23, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +25 **Str** +4 **Dex** +7 **Con** +4 **Int** +5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Hungersense** The gimmerling senses creatures that require food to live. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [hand crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=427)_ (20 bolts) --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +25 **Will** +19 **HP** 235 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10 **Treacherous Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 15 feet. Tangled roots, jagged divots, sharp rocks and other hazards appear on surfaces in the aura, creating [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453).
**Trip Up** **Trigger** A creature critically fails a melee attack to hit the gimmerling or moves into a space within the gimmerling's treacherous aura; **Effect** The triggering creature must attempt a DC 32 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of its next turn.
**Failure** The target takes 2d10 bludgeoning damage and is off-guard until the start of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the target is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). --- **Speed** 30 feet; trickster's step **Melee** claw +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d8+7 slashing plus Sly Disarm **Melee** jaws +26 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 3d8+7 piercing plus 2d6 poison **Ranged** hand crossbow +28 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 2d6+3 piercing plus 2d6 poison **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The gimmerling takes on the appearance of any [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628). In humanoid form, They lose their treacherous aura, and their equipment appears to be trinkets or toys. If the chosen form lacks claws or fangs, they lose the matching Strike. If they lose their claw Strike, they gain a fist Strike that is identical except that it deals bludgeoning damage. **Sly Disarm** **Requirements** The gimmerling's last action was a successful claw Strike; **Effect** The gimmerling attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) the creature they hit. They gain a +4 status bonus on the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check. This attempt neither applies nor counts toward the gimmerling's multiple attack penalty. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The gimmerling deals 2d6 extra precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures. **Trickster's Step** The gimmerling ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and doesn't trigger traps with their movement.
Gimmerling Keepsakes Gimmerlings collect weapons, traps, mechanical novelties, and dangerous magic items. A gimmerling is likely found with toolkits (like [artisan's toolkits](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2703), [repair toolkits](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2747), and [thieves' toolkits](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758)) and items it can use to appear more vulnerable, such as locks, manacles, and snare kits (which it feigns being trapped by).
Hunting Grounds Gimmerlings are more common in the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35) than in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) proper and favor hunting grounds on the verges of dangerous places where they might encounter protective creatures to mislead with their disguises.
name: Gimmerling

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Globster](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3019)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The tide washes ashore all manner of detritus, from harmless seaweed and shells to the rotting corpses of massive aquatic creatures. The globster is often mistaken for such, and this assumption isn't entirely incorrect—these mindless, oozing masses are composed of decaying sea creatures, half-digested and merged into a revolting, reeking heap of blubbery sludge.

 Though mindless, globsters are predators that seek out living quarry. They often huddle on the seafloor, where their own fetid mass attracts scavengers who swiftly become the ooze's next meal. When the tides wash these monsters ashore, they simply shift to hunting land-bound prey. Coastal communities usually notice the smell of a washed-up globster long before they see it. Those sent to investigate often mistake a globster for the carcass of a beached whale before discovering the presumed corpse is very much alive and hungry.

 Sages once believed globsters were undead, undulating wads of rotting flesh driven to feed, but though mindless, they are very much alive. They are attracted to waterside refuse dumps and floating garbage scows and are dimly aware enough to congregate where food is plentiful.

 Since they consist of so much blubber and oily tissue, globsters can be collected for lamp oil, grease, cooking fat, and more. The goo that remains when they decompose works for this purpose, if one can stand the smell. The firmer fat deteriorates quickly, but many an impromptu goblin beach barbecue has deep-fried slabs of it as a delicacy.

 Globsters consume living creatures but digest only a portion of them. The undigested dross accumulates within the globster as it becomes more and more bloated. They carry this fetid mass within their squelching bodies until instinct or injury provokes them to vomit forth a new globster to help devour everything nearby. A globster with enough dross to create a new globster automatically does so as a free action triggered upon taking damage. Treat any encounter with such a globster as though it were against two level 5 creatures, instead of just one. As far as scholars know, this is the only way these creatures can create more of their kind.

On a failure, the creature becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91). If the creature is already sickened, the condition value increases by 1, to a maximum of sickened 4. Once a creature succeeds at its saving throw, it is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

**Saturated**   A globster can survive for 1 hour out of the water, after which it risks [suffocation](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).

</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Goblin Pyro](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3026)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Some goblins take their people's admiration of fire fully into the realm of deadly obsession. These pyromaniacs can be a great boon to a band of goblin raiders eager to torch their enemies and wreak havoc. More often, however, their presence is a double-edged sword; in the heat of the moment, goblin pyros sometimes lose sight of their tribe's goals and simply set fire to anything that will burn—including their own allies. Goblin squads are also prone to distraction, and more than one goblin raid has failed because its members were too busy watching a massive blaze.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**

While all goblins enjoy singing, goblin war chanters pride themselves on mastering the art of vocal performance. Whether they are actually enjoyable is entirely subjective. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Goblin War Chanter](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3027) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 175 **Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +7, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +4, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6 **Str** +2 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +4 **Items** [dogslicer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1675), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=77) (10 arrows) --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +8 **Will** +5 **HP** 16 **Goblin Scuttle** **Trigger** A goblin ally ends a move action adjacent to the goblin; **Effect** The goblin Steps. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dogslicer +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d6+2 slashing **Ranged** shortbow +8 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6 piercing **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 17, attack +7 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Courageous Anthem](/Spells.aspx?ID=1763), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **1st** [Bless](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1678) (2 slots) **Goblin Song** The war chanter sings annoying goblin songs, distracting foes with silly and repetitive lyrics. The chanter attempts a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check against the Will DCs of up to two enemies within 30 feet. This has the usual traits and restrictions for a Performance check.

markdown:
Goblins' eponymous pets aren't true canines at all but rather large, blunt-nosed rodents with thin bodies and long legs. Often as cowardly as they are ugly, goblin dogs prefer to lurk behind bushes or in deep shadows, only pouncing upon lone or wounded prey. Goblin dogs frequently roam in packs, but they are likely to flee from a fight if injured, even if it means abandoning their packmates. Goblin dogs take their name from a long association with [goblins](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=422), who breed the beasts as guard animals and mounts. Most goblins take issue with the name, as the average goblin is appalled at the suggestion that their favored mounts have anything at all to do with actual dogs. Of course, being goblins, they haven't bothered to come up with their own unique name for the creatures. Even the most pampered goblin dogs have itchy mange and prolific dander that tenaciously affects those who come in contact with them. This “goblin pox” causes itchy hives and festering sores that are as unsightly as they are irritating and distracting. Goblin dog dander causes allergic reactions in nearly all other creatures that don't share goblin dogs' terrible hygiene—with the notable exception being, of course, goblins, who remain entirely immune to the disease regardless of cleanliness. Hunger can drive goblin dogs to bouts of uncharacteristic violence, and crueler goblins sometimes purposefully starve their pets on the eve of battle. Goblin dogs subsist on whatever organic material they can scavenge; they particularly enjoy fresh carrion. Although goblins are far from picky eaters themselves, they value goblin dogs because the noisome animals will consume material that even goblins won't touch. In fact, “Will It Eat?” is one of the most popular games goblins play with their pets, where a wide range of morsels (not always edible or safe to consume) are dangled before a goblin dog's snout. Sadly, the game “Will It Die?” is often played after “Will It Eat?” Goblin dogs that survive the second game earn renown for their digestive prowess and often become favored tribal pets, treated even better than most of the rank-and-file goblins. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Goblin Dog](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3028) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 176 **Perception** +6; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7 **Str** +3 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** -4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +8 **Will** +5 **HP** 17 **Irritating Dander** A creature that hits a goblin dog with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attack, tries to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) it, or otherwise touches it is exposed to goblin pox.
**[Buck](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=54)** DC 17
**Juke** **Requirements** A creature must be mounted on the goblin dog; **Trigger** The rider issues a command to the goblin dog; **Effect** The goblin dog Steps before following the command. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** jaws +9, **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing plus goblin pox **Goblin Pox** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) [Goblins](/Traits.aspx?ID=618) and goblin dogs are immune to goblin pox; **Saving Throw** DC 17 Fortitude; **Stage 1** sickened 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (1 round); **Stage 3** sickened 2 and can't reduce its sickened value below 1 (1 day) **Scratch** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The goblin dog vigorously scratches itself, exposing all adjacent creatures to goblin pox.
Goblin Dog Stories Goblins adore goblin dogs, and crafting stories of their pets' antics is a time-honored tradition among many goblin tribes. Goblins often seek to outdo prior yarns by increasing the audacity, ridiculousness, and surreality of their adventures. Examples include goblin dogs holding fancy dinners in high society among unwitting humans, goblin dogs tainting dwarven ale in unmentionable ways, and goblins who actually transform into something else as a result of a goblin dog bite. This last tale in particular has some truth to it, as credible accounts confirm the existence of goblin dog werecreatures in certain tribes.
name: Goblin Dog

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Gogiteth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3029)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A gogiteth is a slavering nightmare of teeth, eyes, and hairy spiderlike legs, and its appearance is invariably seared into the minds of any who witness it. Hives of these skittering monsters haunt the lowest reaches of the Darklands, competing with [cave worms](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=378) and other subterranean horrors for food and resources. A gogiteth is rarely alone, as they learned that the best means of survival is sticking with others of their own kind. Even the hardiest Darklands residents seek cover when a gogiteth is spotted, for where there's one, a swarm is sure to follow.

 Gogiteths make a clacking sound as they skitter. The joints in their many legs pop and crack with each movement, though they can suppress this voluntarily and hunt silently without alerting prey. The odd creatures can also make a high-pitched whistling sound that echoes throughout the caverns where they live. Some Darklands natives report that groups of gogiteths sometimes join together in eerie, discordant songs.

 Gogiteth anatomy have inspired no shortage of speculation as to their origins. Some believe they are the result of a fleshwarping experiment gone horribly wrong. Others think they may be related in some way to the [Dominion of the Black](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=394)—possibly the distant cousins or some strange exiles of those alien entities. According to this mythos, once they made their way to Golarion, the gogiteths crawled into the depths of the earth, shying away from the blazing sun above.

 Gogiteths are a menace to every other denizen of the Darklands. Those who live in gogiteth-infested regions of Sekamina or Orv frequently call for temporary truces when a gogiteth swarm has been spotted. Since even an average gogiteth hive can host up to two dozen of the horrors, calls to eradicate them are dangerous quests indeed.

**Skittering Assault** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The gogiteth Strides three times. Once per Stride, it can attempt a leg Strike against a creature in its reach at any point during the Stride; it must make each attack against a different creature, but it doesn't apply its multiple attack penalty until after making all its Strikes. If the result of any of the Strikes is a critical failure, Skittering Assault ends.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Great Gogiteths</title>

As deadly as gogiteths are, rumors persist of even more terrifying threats known as great gogiteths. Said to be nearly a hundred feet across and capable of spawning their own hordes of gogiteths in reaction to being attacked, great gogiteths are also reputed to be unusually intelligent. The rumors claim that great gogiteths are largely content to lurk in their deep, remote caverns, spending ages dreaming of sadism and violence.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Mitflit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3031)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Mitflits are self-loathing and pitiful cowards, easily bullied into servitude by other creatures or even slightly more powerful mitflit leaders. They tame insects, spiders, and other small vermin to serve as faithful allies. Mitflits have lost most of their ancestral magic, leaving them to feel incomplete and full of doubt and insecurity. Mitflits find companionship in the other base creatures of the world, and forge strong bonds of friendship with vermin, the only other beings that seem willing to accept them. A social structure, even one in which they are bullied, partially fills the hole within most mitflits' personalities, and they rarely rebel or last out unless their rage hits a breaking point.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pugwampi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3032)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Mean, dog-faced, and craven, pugwampis take disproportionate enjoyment from the accidents and missteps of other creatures— something that happens often due to the supernatural aura of ill fortune these gremlins project. They enjoy preparing pranks involving spikes, excrement, pits full of spiders, and similar twisted torments. Pugwampis are somewhat deaf and thus often yell loudly to each other when not hiding. Many pugwampis worship [kholos](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=54) as gods and aspire to be more like them. Kholos, on the other hand, hate pugwampis because of their sycophantic fawning.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Jinkin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3033)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Jinkins are sadistic tinkers who steal and sabotage items and take great pride in their power to curse precious objects. They hold grudges and create convoluted plans for revenge when they feel slighted, such as when a creature dares to remove one of their curses. Rarely content to wreak simple mayhem, jinkins also take immense pleasure in torture and murder, though they prefer to lead victims into traps designed to capture or incapacitate rather than just kill them outright. Deep pits are a preferred method, since victims who survive their fall face a slow death from starvation and thirst while jinkins gather at the edge of pits to tease and torment them.

markdown:
Griffons are regal beasts revered as symbols of freedom and strength in many cultures. They are physically striking, with the hindquarters of a lion and the head, wings, and forelimbs of a great bird of prey—typically an eagle, but some instead bear the features of a hawk, falcon, or even an osprey or vulture. In rare cases, the griffon's hindquarters may resemble those of a different great cat, such as a leopard or tiger. The variations seem to conform to the griffon's environment—for instance, especially rare griffons of northern Avistan have the hindquarters of a Grungir lynx and the upper body of a snowy owl. Wild griffons rely on their powerful wings to hold them aloft and their keen eyesight to spy out prey. The speed with which they plunge toward the ground and snatch up victims is astonishing. They often tear apart a kill's flesh with razor-sharp beaks but not before alighting to secluded location where they can enjoy their meal without interruption. Griffons hunting to feed their chicks are more cautious, tearing apart prey rather than risking bringing a living creature back to their nests. Skilled animal trainers long ago learned how to raise griffons as mounts for military forces or powerful individuals. Such mounts are known for their strength, bravery, and unfailing loyalty. They are among the smartest of animals, and many griffon variants are considered intelligent beasts instead; it's thought that a griffon chooses its rider as much as a rider chooses the griffon. The process of training a griffon to accept and carry a rider in flight is a long and expensive ordeal. Griffon trainers charge rich sums for their services, and a ruler who can boast of owning a stable of griffons is the subject of great respect and envy. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Griffon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3034) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 182 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** -4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +13 **Will** +7 **HP** 60 --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** beak +14 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570)), **Damage** 2d8+4 piercing **Melee** talon +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d6+4 piercing **Melee** wing +14 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+4 bludgeoning **Flying Strafe** The griffon [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312)up to its fly Speed and makes two talon Strikes at any point during that movement. Each Strike must target a different creature. The attacks take the normal multiple attack penalty. **Pounce** The griffon Strides and makes a talon Strike at the end of that movement. If the griffon began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), it remains hidden until after the attack. **Regal Shriek** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The griffon unleashes a shriek that transitions into a terrifying roar. Each creature in a 60-foot emanation must attempt a DC 20 Will save. Regardless of the result, creatures are temporarily immune to all griffons' Regal Shrieks for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76).
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2. [Animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) are [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for as long as they're frightened.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3. Animals are [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) as long as they're frightened.
Alces Wingless griffons, known as alces, result from a rare mutation. Among a clutch of winged griffons, the alce is typically considered the runt, so alces are rarely seen on their own in the wild, though they're often intentionally bred in captivity as relatively affordable exotic mounts. An alce has a land Speed of 35 feet and loses its fly Speed and Flying Strafe.
name: Griffon

markdown:
Grikkitogs, also known as the “hungry earth,” are strange parasites from the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=30) that infest earth, rock, and stone in order to feed their endless hunger. A young grikkitog is a formless apparition until it corrupts an [earth elemental](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=409)host, forming the grikkitog's core. A grikkitog can then possess the earth and stone nearby with its voracious essence, forming maws and eyes all around it. These creatures can be particularly dangerous to small creatures that lair within gaps among rocks, as well as mountain climbers searching for the perfect handhold. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Grikkitog](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3035) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 183 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), manifold vision, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 **Implant Core** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The grikkitog implants its core into an adjacent section of earth or stone, melding seamlessly and changing its visual appearance to match the surrounding rock. It's [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) but automatically succeeds at its [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) the stone around it; creatures actively searching for it can still attempt Perception checks against its Deception DC as normal. A grikkitog can release its implantation as a free action, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait. A grikkitog's infestation aura and manifold vision are only active while implanted.
**Manifold Vision** While its core is implanted, the grikkitog can see through the eyes it creates throughout the area of its infestation aura, gaining the benefits of [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49). --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +23 **Will** +24 **HP** 200 **Resistances** 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) **Infestation Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 120 feet. While its core is implanted, a grikkitog infests all earth and stone within 120 feet, as long as there is a contiguous physical connection between the earth, including stone objects touching the ground. This effect spreads even if the grikkitog does not have line of effect, though it can affect earth or stone on the surface and exposed to the air only if at least part of its core is exposed as well. Within the aura, it can grow maws and eyes everywhere. It can make jaws attacks against any creature, originating from any earth or stone in the aura adjacent to that creature. Determine [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) from the origin point of the attack, not from the grikkitog's core. --- **Speed** 20 feet; burrow 20 feet, earth glide **Melee** jaws +29 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d12+14 piercing plus barbed maw **Barbed Maw** **Trigger** The grikkitog hits a creature with a jaws Strike; **Effect** The grikkitog sinks its barbed teeth into the target, which must succeed at a DC 34 Reflex save or be [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81). While immobilized, the victim takes 3d8 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and the grikkitog feeds upon its flesh. The creature is immobilized until the grikkitog ends the effect as a free action or the target succeeds at a DC 38 check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296). The grikkitog can immobilize any number of creatures with these maws. **Earth Glide** The grikkitog can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through dirt and stone at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.
Grikkitog Origins Grikkitogs often feature as bogeymen in scary stories told by denizens of the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=30). Those who recall the wars between the elemental lords believe the first grikkitog was created as an experimental weapon by [Ayrzul](/Deities.aspx?ID=102), the Fossilized King of the Elemental Plane of Earth. Yet the evil elemental lord did not realize the raw power of his creation. The grikkitog's hunger grew so ravenous that it escaped containment, infested an earth elemental warden, and began to spread across the planes. Now grikkitogs burrow throughout [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), devouring the unwary.
name: Grikkitog

markdown:
The Grim Reaper is the unflinching personification of death. Silent as the grave and inexorable as time itself, this legendary being hunts down creatures that have evaded death for too long. Sometimes the Grim Reaper comes without warning. Other times it comes to finish the work others could not. The Grim Reaper serves no god, [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), [aeon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=357), or otherworldly being. It's both despised and feared by [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458) and [celestials](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), but few—if any—dare stand in its way. Like some eternal plague, it kills any who try to cure the multiverse of its presence. It stands alone and keeps its own council. The pleas of mortals and immortals alike fall on deaf ears once the Grim Reaper chooses its quarry. Its reasoning is silent and inscrutable to the mortal mind, but no matter its choice, the result is final. While some legends claim that the Grim Reaper appears to everyone when they die, the truth is more disturbing. Such vigils are the providence of psychopomps, immortals charged with the protection and guidance of mortal souls through the afterlife. The Grim Reaper instead enacts the sinister agenda of [Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=37), where the Apocalypse Riders rule. Indeed, there are many similarities in shape and form between the Grim Reaper and [Charon](/Deities.aspx?ID=135), the Rider of Death, but there are no recorded instance of these entities working together. Instead, the Grim Reaper serves as something of a manifestation of Abaddon itself, and in this regard, is believed to be an incarnation of the mysterious First Rider. When the Grim Reaper visits a world, it does so not as an angel of mercy but a relentless harvester of life. Those who fall to the Grim Reaper were not destined to die as much as they were selected, hunted, and murdered. Perhaps the most frightening detail surrounding the Grim Reaper's legend is its nature as a singular entity, for some believe that more than one exists in the Great Beyond. There are whispered rumors of a council of at least nine Grim Reapers, stalking reality and culling the living as in service to true entropy. According to the teachings of some death cults, the final goal of the Grim Reaper is to end the cycle of life and death and serve as a silent lord of an empty universe. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 52 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 50 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 [Grim Reaper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3036) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 184 **Perception** +41; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), see the unseen, status sight, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +43, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +40, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +43, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +39, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +36, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +43 **Str** +8 **Dex** +10 **Con** +8 **Int** +5 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +8 **Status Sight** The Grim Reaper automatically knows the Hit Points, conditions, afflictions, and emotions of all creatures it can see. **Items** [Scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39) --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +37 **Ref** +41 **Will** +38 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 320 (death's grace, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** all damage 15 **Aura of Misfortune** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) 20 feet. Living creatures in the aura must roll twice on all d20 rolls and use the lower result.
**Death's Grace** The Grim Reaper can choose whether or not it counts as [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) for effects that affect undead differently. Even if it does not count as undead, the Grim Reaper still never counts as a living creature.
**Void Healing** The Grim Reaper can choose whether or not it takes vitality damage.
**Lurking Death** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** A creature within 100 feet makes a ranged attack or uses an action that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) trait; **Effect** The Grim Reaper teleports to a square adjacent to the triggering creature and makes a melee Strike against it. If the Strike hits, the Grim Reaper disrupts the triggering action. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 75 feet **Melee** keen scythe +40 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly 3d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 4d10+23 slashing plus death strike and energy drain **Divine Innate Spells** DC 47, attack +37 - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) - **10th** [Execute](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519) (×4) - **Constant (2nd)** [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) - **Constant (3rd)** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553) - **Constant (6th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Death Strike** ([Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571)) A creature critically hit by any of the Grim Reaper's attacks or that critically fails against any of its spells must succeed at a DC 47 Fortitude save or die. **Energy Drain** When the Grim Reaper hits and deals damage with its scythe, it regains 20 Hit Points, and the target must succeed at a DC 43 Fortitude save or become [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67). If the target is already doomed, the doomed value increases by 1 (to a maximum of doomed 3). **Final Death** A creature killed by the Grim Reaper can't be brought back to life by any means short of divine intervention. **Infuse Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) Any scythe gains the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) trait, can't be disarmed, and becomes a _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2843) scythe_ while the Grim Reaper wields it. If the Grim Reaper Strikes a creature with a weakness to any specific type of damage, the scythe's damage counts as that type of damage, in addition to slashing.
name: Grim Reaper

No one is quite sure what lesser deaths are, though some claim that they are avatars of the Grim Reaper. Unlike that strange hunter, however, lesser deaths hunt in packs on rare occasions. More often than not, they manifest from cursed magic items. Other times, they are just the enactors of death, hunting in the same way the Grim Reaper does—silently, with no remorse or quarter. Rarely, multiple lesser deaths work together to cull a large population, their scythes cutting through crowds and leaving entire cities devoid of life, inspiring (hopefully) false rumors of multiple Grim Reapers. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Lesser Death](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3037) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 185 **Perception** +32; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), status sight, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +32, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +35 **Str** +6 **Dex** +9 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 **Status Sight** a lesser death automatically knows the Hit Points, conditions, afflictions, and emotions of all creatures it can see. **Items** [Scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39) --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +33 **Will** +32 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 255 (death's grace, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Aura of Misfortune** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) 20 feet. Living creatures in the aura must roll twice on all d20 rolls and use the lower result.
**Death's Grace** A lesser death can choose whether or not it counts as [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) for effects that affect undead differently. Even if it does not count as undead, the lesser death still never counts as a living creature.
**Void Healing** A lesser death can choose whether or not it takes vitality damage.
**Lurking Death** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet makes a ranged attack or uses an action that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) trait; **Effect** The lesser death teleports to a square adjacent to the triggering creature and makes a melee Strike against it. If the Strike hits, the lesser death disrupts the triggering action. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** keen scythe +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 3d10+14 slashing plus 1d12 void **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38 - **2nd** [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) - **Constant (6th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Infuse Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) Any scythe gains the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) trait, can't be disarmed, and becomes a _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2843) scythe_ while the lesser death wields it.
