markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Grindylow](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3038)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The top half of a grindylow vaguely resembles a [goblin](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=422), but from the waist down, their bodies split into a tangle of suckered, wriggling tentacles. Grindylows mostly dwell in shallow waters, fresh and briny, including lakes, rivers, coastal regions, and coral reefs. They generally organize into groups called schools, which can range from a few individuals to a few hundred. Smaller schools are sometimes taken over by a powerful aquatic creature, though such alliances only last until the school faces a major setback, at which point the surviving grindylows scatter and form smaller schools of their own.

 Grindylows aren't territorial, but they are pragmatic. While they rarely build permanent structures, they will adopt a good hunting ground for generations until driven away by predators. They often lair in mobile shelters, such as a sargasso of seaweed or hull of an abandoned ship. They are skilled scavengers and hunters who eat anything they can sink their teeth into.

 Grindylows respect the power of larger sea predators but have a special hatred for squids (or anything that resembles a squid). Sailors plying grindylow-infested waters often paint the images of squids on the bottoms of their hulls in hopes of warding off potentials raids. While this can keep smaller schools at bay, it can also backfire, potentially inciting larger groups to gather for a coordinated attack; this becomes especially likely if the ship's route is predictable. Their hatred of squids does not extend to other tentacled creatures; grindylows consider [octopuses](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=143)to be the epitome of grace and power.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 14

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Grindylow.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 0">[Grindylow](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3038)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=523" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=529" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 186

**Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** -1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +5 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 14

</row>

**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** <actions string="Reaction" /> A grindylow gains 1 extra reaction at the start of each of their turns that they can use only to make a Reactive Strike with a tentacle. They can't use more than one Reactive Strike triggered by the same action or choice.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, swim 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bite +7 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
spear +5,
**Damage** 1d6+1 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
spear +7 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 piercing

**Clinging Suckers**   When a grindylow successfully Grabs a creature larger than themself, they attach to that creature. The [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) creature is not [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), but if it moves, the grindylow moves with it. If the creature is Medium or smaller, it takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speeds while the grindylow is attached. The grindylow is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) while attached to a creature.

**Jet** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The grindylow moves up to 60 feet in a straight line through the water without triggering reactions.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Giant Grindylows</title>

While most grindylows are Small, a minute percentage of these creatures keep growing throughout their lives. Those that become Large or larger gain the [giant](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=420) trait and often become champions of their schools.
</aside>
name: Grindylow

markdown:
A guthallath is an enormous construct created long ago, probably as a war machine, by a long-forgotten empire. Nearly 100 feet tall, this massive stone statue typically resembles a stalwart warrior wearing only a loincloth and skullcap. Few have seen the entire body of a guthallath, though; most of the time, the relic is buried up to its neck, covered in moss, and stranded in a forgotten place. Yet every so often, one of these harbingers of destruction reactivates in response to some unknown stimulus or rallying call, and when this happens, woe to any who stand in its way. While a guthallath's ancient enemies are likely gone, it's still an engine of pure destruction, designed to rampage for weeks, even months. It's not intelligent enough to enjoy or regret its acts and cannot be reasoned with— it's also unaffected by most magic and unpredictable in how it selects its targets (and the creatures it spares). **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Guthallath](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3039) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 187 **Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +40 **Str** +10 **Dex** +2 **Con** +8 **Int** -4 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +38 **Ref** +32 **Will** +30 **HP** 325 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)), spells 15 (except [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Erosion Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 120 feet. The guthallath erodes away the physical integrity of all around it. Creatures and objects in the emanation other than the guthallath have their Hardness and resistances reduced by 10. At the start of their turn, a creature in the erosion aura's area takes 6d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC 39 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save.
**Immunity to Magic** The guthallath is immune to spells of 6th rank or lower and activations of magic items of 13th level or lower. --- **Speed** 40 feet, burrow 50 feet **Melee** fist +38 ([deadly 3d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+18 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) or [Improved Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) 20 feet **Melee** foot +38 ([deadly 3d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+18 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Innate Primal Spells** DC 37 - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553) - **Constant (8th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Annihilation Beams** A guthallath releases two beams of destruction from its eyes. Each beam is a 120-foot line. Everything in either line takes 13d10 damage with a DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature reduced to 0 HP is reduced to a fine powder as the [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492) spell. There is no additional effect on creatures in any area where the beams overlap. The guthallath can't use this ability again for 1d4 rounds. **Deadly Throw** **Requirements** The guthallath has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** The guthallath throws the creature into the air, 100 feet high and 50 feet away. If the flung creature lands on another creature, the creature it lands on takes the same amount of bludgeoning damage. The creature being landed on can attempt a DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. **Powerful Blows** If a guthallath hits with an attack and rolls a natural 19 on the d20 roll, the attack is a critical hit. This has no effect if the 19 would be a failure. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Huge or smaller, foot, DC 45
Guthallath Slumber Entire civilizations have been erased due to the guthallath's devastating rampage. Yet for those looking to survive its attack, there's one small hope. Usually after some time, the guthallath ends its massacre in a swift yet seemingly random way. The colossus then finds a remote location deep in the wilderness and buries itself, entering a deep slumber for years—if not centuries— before awakening and beginning the destructive cycle once more.
name: Guthallath

markdown:
Sea hags specialize in transformation magic, preying on those who are desperate to change some aspect of their physical appearance. Targets often include those suffering from insecurity about their bodies or those desperate to reside in a different environment, such as aquatic creatures who wish to live on land. These hags are known for tempting desperate victims into tragic and excruciating bargains, though they're also happy to drown and eat mariners who stray too close to their dwellings. A sea hag has the upper half of a humanoid and the lower half of an octopus, with translucent skin and glowing lights visible beneath their flesh. Sea hags can join covens, but their aquatic nature often prevents them from joining mixed covens with other kinds of hags. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Sea Hag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3040) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 188 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +10, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **[Coven](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=58)** A sea hag adds [_humanoid form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1560), [_mariner's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1593), and [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) to their coven's spells. Their spell DC when leading a coven is 20. --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +8 **Will** +10 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 45 **Immunities** [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 3 --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 35 feet **Melee** claw +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d10+4 slashing **Dread Gaze** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The hag gazes upon a creature, afflicting it with a gnawing sense of impending doom, with a result depending on its Will save (DC 20). The target doesn't need to be able to see the sea hag.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76).
**Failure** The creature is frightened 1 and is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 round. If the target was [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), it remains [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) for 1 day. At the end of the day, it must attempt a Fortitude save against the same DC; if it fails, it dies.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is frightened 2 and slowed 1 for 1 minute. **Sea Hag's Bargain** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The sea hag can make a bargain with a willing creature who must be of sound mind. The creature gives away a special or cherished quality—such as its courage, its beauty, or its voice. In exchange, the sea hag spends 1 minute polymorphing the creature into a form the target desires.
This functions as [Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55). It might be a total transformation or just changing one or more aspects of the target's body, and it can't make the creature more than one size smaller or larger. The creature changes its Speeds as appropriate for the new form. It doesn't change the attack and damage bonuses with its Strikes, but it might change the damage type the Strikes deal. This has an unlimited duration, and as long as it's transformed, the creature is [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) and can't reduce its sickened condition below 2. The creature can slowly and carefully eat and drink despite being sickened. The only way to restore the lost quality used as payment is to defeat the sea hag or make another bargain for its return. Ending the bargain in this way also removes the transformation.
name: Sea Hag

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sweet Hag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3041)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Sweet hags use bright colors, pleasant visages, and sugary treats to draw in children and the young at heart. Their favorite prey is impoverished youngsters who are unlikely to be missed—sweet hags offer them food and a veneer of kindness before devouring the hapless child. Sweet hags also enchant their food to magically charm others, often using these bespelled servitors to handle all of their busywork. Though they present a lovely and generous face to the world, they turn vicious when insulted or defied. A sweet hag's true form is a dribbling mass of flesh with gumdrop eyes, often resembling melting taffy.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

+1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)

candy cane claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 1d10+5 piercing plus soporific touch

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 20, attack +14
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598)
- **1st**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (at will), [Cleanse Cuisine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1468) (at will), [Spider Sting](/Spells.aspx?ID=1684)
- **2nd**
[Create Food](/Spells.aspx?ID=1475) (sweets only), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will), [Laughing Fit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583), [Shrink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1672), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Betraying Touch** <actions string="Single Action" />  The sweet hag touches a creature that doesn't realize the hag is an enemy. The betrayed creature is affected by soporific strike with a –4 circumstance penalty to their saving throw.

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The sweet hag can take on the appearance of any Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) woman. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes, but it might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

**Poisoned Candy** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The sweet hag casts an innate spell that can normally target 1 creature on a piece of food, typically a sweet treat. The spell is stored in the food. The first creature that eats any of the food is affected by the spell and takes a –4 circumstance penalty to their saving throw against that effect.

**Soporific Touch**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) A creature damaged by a sweet hag's claw must succeed at a DC 20 Fortitude save or be [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) for 1 day. If the creature critically fails or fails this save while already enfeebled by soporific strike, it falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and dreams of eating delicious sweets; this is a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) effect. If not woken up before 1 minute passes, the creature wakes up automatically.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Iron Hag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3042)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Iron hags are kidnappers, targeting those too young to even remember to fight back. They most often snatch babies from their cradles, though they sometimes target fearful children or youths who suffer from anxiety. They then imprison their new wards in towers or enchanted dungeons, terrifying their victims with stories of the outside world to discourage them from even trying to escape. If these methods fail to be effective, iron hags become more straightforward in their methods of imprisonment, insisting it's for their captives' own good.

 An iron hag's true form has unnaturally long arms. True to their name, they have teeth made of iron, as well as long iron toenails and claws.

Cuckoo hags seek out youths who are unhappy with their lives, disguising themselves as a trusted figure such as a friend or family member. These hags then weave magical, idealized worlds out of illusions and dream stuff to trap their victims. Once they convince a mortal to stay in their magical world forever, the cuckoo hag drains their prey of life force and imprisons their soul. A cuckoo hag's true form is a creature with the cracked pale skin of a broken porcelain doll and an almost impossibly thin neck and limbs. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Cuckoo Hag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3043) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 191 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +18, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +18, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +18, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +18 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +6 **[Coven](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=58)** A cuckoo hag adds [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615), [_outcast's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620), and [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662) to their coven's spells. --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +19 **Will** +18 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 170 **Immunities** [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 10 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10 **Sound Imitation** A cuckoo hag who succeeds at a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) can mimic the sound of any feminine voice they have heard (in cases where this may be unclear, the GM is the final arbiter) and have a +4 circumstance bonus to this check. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing plus 1d6 spirit **Occult Innate Spells** DC 29 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will) - **3rd** [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503) (at will), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675) (at will) - **4th** [Acid Grip](/Spells.aspx?ID=1436) (×3), [Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) - **5th** [Dreaming Potential](/Spells.aspx?ID=1504), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), [Mirage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604), [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615), [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1692) - **9th** [Seize Soul](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664) (at will; see soul covenant) **Rituals** DC 28 - **2nd** [Animate Object](/Rituals.aspx?ID=1) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The cuckoo hag can take on the appearance of any Medium female [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628). This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Cuckoo's Lair** A cuckoo hag is intrinsically connected to a pocket dimension that serves as their home domain. The lair is an extradimensional space that is 40 feet wide, 40 feet deep, and 30 feet tall. It contains whatever mundane food, buildings, landscapes, and objects that the cuckoo hag desires, though anything removed from the cuckoo's lair dissolves into nothingness instantly.
In order to enter or leave the plane, the cuckoo hag must create a door in the physical world as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) and [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) traits. The door is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) by default, though the cuckoo hag can choose a target creature that can perceive the door normally. The cuckoo hag can Dismiss this door from reality, but only if they (and no other creature) are inside of their lair and alone. **Soul Covenant** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirement** The cuckoo hag can cast [_seize soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664) at will, but only to target a creature who has agreed to stay with them forever. They typically do so as soon as the agreement is made but might hold off to gain leverage over a situation. They typically place the soul into a construct, typically a [soulbound doll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=383) if they want a companion or a more powerful construct if they want a guardian.
name: Cuckoo Hag

<row gap="medium">

The target must succeed a DC 17 Reflex saving throw or become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round (or 1 minute on a critical failure).

**Keen Eyes**   The halfling gains a +2 circumstance bonus when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) creatures within 30 feet of it. <title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Harpy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3046)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Harpies are amalgamations of human and bird, resembling feral humans with wings, talons, and sharp teeth. They use their ancestral control of the wind to lure prey or even directly pull in their next meal. They enjoy causing confusion and fear in their prey before they strike, believing it creates a savory flavor in the flesh. Harpies can eat most creatures but strongly prefer sapient prey— [humans](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6) and [elves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2) in particular. Although harpies will eat [goblins](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4) if sufficiently hungry, they dislike their flavor and avoid eating them if possible. This doesn't comfort goblins, of course, who have a particularly strong fear of harpies.

 Because their aeries often reek with the gore of their kills and careless spatters of guano, harpies carry a distinctly vile scent that canny travelers associate with danger. Harpies who roost close to civilization make better efforts to keep clean, though these efforts have mixed results.

 Somewhere among the filth, most aeries have a shrine dedicated to the demon lord [Pazuzu](/Deities.aspx?ID=79). Harpy legends credit him with raising them up from simple air spirits to their current station, and their gratitude typically takes the shape of a sketched figure in a corner piled with offerings. More devout families build portable wooden shrines that are carried from aerie to aerie for generations. Harpies live in family groups or larger clans. Most adults stand 5 feet tall and weigh around 90 pounds. While some use relatively simple weapons, those who master the use of the bow are regarded as heroes among their kind.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Harpy.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Harpy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3046)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=527" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 193

**Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); wind's whispers

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +13, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +4

**Con** +0

**Int** -1

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Wind's Whispers** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) When a harpy speaks, they can choose one creature within 90 feet. That creature can hear the harpy's words over any other sound, but no other creature hears the words at all.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 75

</row>

**[Stench](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=76)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)) 30 feet, DC 21

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 60 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +15 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d8+4 piercing plus putrid plague

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
talon +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d6+4 slashing

**Hungry Winds** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The harpy uses the wind to pull its prey closer. A target within 20 feet must succeed at a DC 21 Fortitude save or be [pulled](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) adjacent to the harpy, where they make a jaws Strike against the target. If the target was pulled off the ground and can't fly, it then [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) as normal.

**Putrid Plague**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) The [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) and [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) conditions from putrid plague can't end or be reduced until the disease is cured; **Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1d4 hours), **Stage 2** sickened 1 (1 day), **Stage 3** sickened 1 and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (1 day), **Stage 4** unconscious (1 day), **Stage 5** dead

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Harpy Exiles</title>

Most harpies are cruel and sadistic, but now and then a harpy manages to escape from its family and becomes exposed to the wider world. Eyes opened, these harpy exiles are almost always more mild-mannered, hygienic, and open to non-combat interactions.
</aside>
name: Harpy

**Hellfire Breath** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The hell hound breathes flames that deal 4d6 fire damage to all creatures in a 15-foot cone (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The hell hound can't use Hellfire Breath again for 1d4 rounds. If the hell hound would take fire damage or be targeted by a [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) effect, their Hellfire Breath recharges.

**Pack Attack**   The hell hound's Strikes deal 1d4 extra damage to creatures within the reach of at least two of the hell hounds' allies.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Herexen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3049)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

When a cleric rebels against their deity and dies in the grip of blasphemous rage, the heresies they committed in life may fuel their transformation into an undead herexen. Herexens seek vengeance against the deity they once worshipped, defiling temples, slaying the faithful, and rallying lesser undead and death cultists to aid them in their unholy quests. Though a herexen's divine gifts have mostly been corrupted into the vileness of undeath, they stubbornly cling to remnants of their former power, still wielding magic and armaments favored by the deity they so greatly despise.

 Groups of herexens that blasphemed against the same deity sometimes combine to form a mockery of a congregation, conducting blasphemous rites with something approaching euphoria. These congregations are often formed from a blasphemous cult whose members practiced their heresy together in life and died together, though some gather independent herexens of the same former faith.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

</column>

</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hippogriff](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3052)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

With the proud bearing of a great raptor and the magnificence of a powerful horse, hippogriffs are thought to be an accidental fusion of creatures or perhaps the creation of a flesh-warping wizard with a keen aesthetic sense. Regardless of their original source, these animals are now a common sight in the skies above their favored plains or hill country.

 Hippogriffs bear the wings, forelegs, and head of a bird of prey, with feather coloration similar to that of a hawk or eagle, though some breeders have managed to produce specimens with stark white or coal-black feathers. Their torso, hindquarters, and tail resemble those of a horse and usually are colored bay, chestnut, or gray, with some coats bearing black, pinto, or even palomino coloration.

 Hippogriffs are similar in size to large horses. Much like their equine cousins, hippogriffs often have to keep wary eyes on the skies above them, as both are preferred meals for hungry griffons and wyverns. Only hippogriffs' superior speed helps protect them from these predators.

 Hippogriffs are exceptionally territorial and fiercely protect the lands under their domain. They typically favor sweeping grasslands, rolling hills, and prairies. Exceptionally hardy hippogriffs make their homes nestled into niches on canyon walls, from which they comb the rocky deserts for coyotes, deer, and the occasional humanoid. Hippogriffs prefer mammalian prey, but they graze after every meal to aid in digestion.

 Since hippogriff hunting habits can be dangerous to both ranchers and their livestock, such communities often set bounties on hippogriffs. As a result, preserved hippogriffs frequently decorate frontier taverns and remote outposts alongside the taxidermied remains of deer, elk, and bears.

 However, other communities train hippogriffs from hatching to be ridden by elite soldiers in combat—the most notable among these groups in the Inner Sea region is the Sable Company Mercenaries in the city-state of Korvosa. Attempts are sometimes made to train adult hippogriffs in the same manner, but this often proves far more difficult. Hippogriff riders must use special saddles and combat techniques that allow them to act in concert with their mount, fighting effectively while avoiding interfering with the movement of their companion's wings.

crossbow +14 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),

**Crossbow Precision**   The first time the archer hits with a crossbow attack in a round, it deals 1d8 extra precision damage.

**Perfect Aim**   The hobgoblin archer ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition. Their targets don't benefit from [lesser cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459), and they reduce the AC bonus from standard cover by 2 against the hobgoblin archer's attack.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hobgoblin General](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3055)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hobgoblin generals serve as leaders of armies and rulers of hobgoblin settlements. A general does not permit the luxuries of rule to soften them. They lead their forces on the field of battle and view this opportunity to excel in a fight at the head of an army as the true reward for a life spent honing one's skills in battle.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22

</column>

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +2
</row>

**General's Cry** When a hobgoblin general rolls initiative, as long as they can perceive at least one foe, they can yell a mighty battle cry. The hobgoblin general attempts an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) a single foe within 60 feet as a free action. If successful, any ally can, as its first action on its first turn of the combat, Stride up to double its speed as a single action.

**Items**
[Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (20 arrows), _+1 [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=375)_, [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12)

</column>

**Ranged**

composite shortbow +15 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+8 piercing

**Polearm Critical Specialization**   On a critical hit, the target of the critical hit is [moved](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 5 feet in a direction of the hobgoblin general's choice.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Homunculus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3056)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A homunculus is a tiny servitor construct created by a crafter to serve as a spy, scout, messenger, or assistant. When a crafter first begins to study the art of creating constructs, they often craft a homunculus first, since the creation process is simple and inexpensive due to a magical shortcut: the use of the creator's own blood. This forges a link between the homunculus and its master, causing the homunculus to gain a spark of the creator's intellect, as well as the same moral values and some of the creator's basic personality traits. Homunculi left to their own devices never stray far from their masters.

 In most cases, a homunculus doesn't survive the death of its master for long. Deprived of its creator, a homunculus loses focus and grows increasingly self-destructive, and some even end up battering themselves to annihilation. Rarely, a homunculus with a slain master survives the trauma with its mind intact, often seeing itself as its deceased creator's child or successor and attempting to further its creator's legacy as best it can. In such cases, and if the homunculus was in close proximity to its master upon that creature's death, a portion of the dead master's soul “infects” the surviving homunculus as they pass on to the afterlife.

 This doesn't result in a truly soulbound homunculus (see sidebar), since only a fragment of the soul is left behind, but this is still enough to grant the homunculus a greater personality, free will of its own, and perhaps most importantly, the ability to speak. Over time, a few of these “awakened” homunculi even go so far as to become convinced that they are the reincarnation of their prior masters, although their actual personalities never quite reach the depth and complexity of a truly living creature. They are, at best, caricatures of the master, and at worst, they become awful, bitter-minded parodies of life itself. Still, a free-willed homunculus might pursue studies in its creator's class, becoming a unique creature with the abilities of that class if time and fortune permit.

 Homunculi are created from a mixture of clay, ash, mandrake root, spring water, and a pint of the creator's own blood. It is possible for a separate donor to provide the blood, but the process is more difficult.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 14

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Homunculus.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 0">[Homunculus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3056)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 200

**Perception** +3; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); (can't speak any language); master link

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** -2
</row>

**Master Link** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) A homunculus can't speak, but it is telepathically linked to its creator. It can share information back and forth, including its master's knowledge and everything the homunculus hears. The range of this link is 1,500 feet. The homunculus typically has a similar attitude to its creator and is utterly faithful. If the homunculus is destroyed, the master takes 2d10 mental damage. If the master is slain, the homunculus becomes [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652), claims its current location as its lair, and instinctively attacks anyone who comes near.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +2 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +3 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 17

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +7 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d4 piercing plus homunculus poison

**Homunculus Poison**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) A homunculus has one dose of poison in a reservoir in its head. It can refill this poison from its reserves with an Interact action; **Saving Throw** DC 15 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Soulbound Homunculi</title>

Most homunculi use a dose of their creator's blood as their spark of life, but it's possible to use a technique similar to that used in the crafting of a [soulbound doll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=383) to give a homunculus a personality and the semblance of life. These homunculi gain the [soulbound](/Traits.aspx?ID=773) trait, lose immunity to [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), can speak, and do not have a special link to a creator, yet the process tends to warp the soul used so that, more often than not, what rises in the new homunculus body is a parody of its prior life. As such, soulbound homunculi are generally created by cruel spellcasters as a method of humiliating and tormenting vanquished enemies.
</aside>
name: Homunculus

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hryngar Sharpshooter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3061)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hryngar sharpshooters serve both as ranged support for caravans and as snipers posted on guard towers overlooking quarries and other areas where workers toil away the hours. Hryngar sharpshooters also specialize in nonlethal methods of ranged combat—tactics they are often called upon to use when tracking down derelict debtors or those attempting to flee from their contractual obligations.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hryngar Bombardier](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3062)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hryngar chemists have a knack for creating alchemical grenades. Their traditions often motivate them to constantly experiment and innovate, though the hostile nature of their environs tends to focus these innovations on weapons or other tools of war. Hryngar bombardiers eagerly steal notes and secrets from their underlings, pushing their students to cut corners to produce results while gleefully pillaging the credit and profits. For those hryngar bombardiers who find themselves pressed into combat service, each thrown grenade represents a new opportunity to observe the  suffering they inflict, before eagerly incorporating that data into their next experiment or innovation to create an even more terrible tool of torment.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

Hryngar priests of the taskmaster god [Droskar](/Deities.aspx?ID=28) often assume leadership roles within their communities, advancing the goals of their deity through coercion and displays of force. Often referred to as hryngar taskmasters, these leaders issue commands to hryngar subordinates and subjugated contractual clients in the same breath, treating the two as if they were interchangeable. As a result, hryngar taskmasters are equally loathed by practically everyone. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Hryngar Taskmaster](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3063) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 203 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +7, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +7, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +5, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +6, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +6 **Str** +2 **Dex** +0 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Chain Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=9), [Pick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=34), [religious symbol](/Armor.aspx?ID=22) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +4 **Will** +8 +2 status to all saves vs. magic, iron mind **HP** 30 **Iron Mind** Hryngar taskmasters automatically disbelieve all [illusions](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) of 1st rank or lower.
**[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=68)** --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** pick +8 ([fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=597)), **Damage** 1d6+6 piercing **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 18 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [Runic Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1658) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 18 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673) - **2nd** [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=1454), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=1623) **Take Them Down!** The hryngar taskmaster smashes their maul into the ground and invokes [Droskar's](/Deities.aspx?ID=28)name to rally their allies to action. All allied hryngars of equal or lower level that are within 20 feet of the hryngar taskmaster gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls until the end of the hryngar taskmaster's next turn.

Hydras are multiheaded, foul-tempered serpentine beasts with voracious appetites, widely feared for their regeneration abilities. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Hydra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3064) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 204 **Perception** +17; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** -3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 23 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +15 **Ref** +12 **Will** +10 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **HP** 90 (body, head regrowth) , regeneration hydra
**HP** (head) 15 **Immunities** area damage **Weaknesses** slashing 5 **Head Regrowth** A hydra ordinarily has five heads. A creature can attempt to sever one of the hydra's heads by specifically targeting it and dealing damage equal to the head's Hit Points. A head that is not completely severed returns to full Hit Points at the end of any creature's turn.
A hydra can regrow a severed head using hydra regeneration. A creature can prevent this regrowth by dealing acid or fire damage to the stump, cauterizing it. Single-target acid or fire effects need to be targeted at a specific stump, but effects that deal [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage or affect areas covering the hydra's whole space cauterize all stumps if they deal acid or fire damage. If the attack that severs a head deals any acid or fire damage, the stump is cauterized instantly. If all five heads are cauterized, the hydra dies.
**Hydra Regeneration** The hydra has [regeneration](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=72) equal to 3 × the number of heads it has. If a hydra's body is missing any heads and the remaining stumps have not been cauterized, the hydra attempts a DC 25 Fortitude save after it regains Hit Points from regeneration. On a success, one uncauterized stump regrows two heads; on a critical success, two uncauterized stumps regrow into two heads each. The hydra can never grow more than double the number of heads it ordinarily has. The hydra's regeneration only fully deactivates if all its heads are severed and all stumps are cauterized, at which point it dies.
**Reactive Heads** A hydra gains an extra reaction per round for each of its heads beyond the first, which it can use only to make Reactive Strikes. It can't use more than 1 reaction on the same triggering action, even if a creature leaves several squares within its reach, and the hydra must use a different head for each Reactive Strike it makes. Whenever one of the hydra's heads is severed, the hydra loses 1 of its extra reactions per round.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** (see reactive heads) --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 25 feet **Melee** fangs +16 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+7 piercing **Focused Assault** The hydra attacks a single target with its heads, overwhelming its foe with multiple attacks and leaving almost nowhere to dodge. The hydra Strikes with its fangs. On a successful attack, the hydra deals damage from its fangs Strike to the target, plus an additional 1d6 damage for every head it has beyond the first. On a failure (but not a critical failure), the hydra deals the damage from one fangs Strike to the target creature. This Strike counts as a number of attacks equal to the number of heads the hydra has toward the hydra's multiple attack penalty. **Storm of Jaws** The hydra makes a number of Strikes up to its number of heads, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the hydra's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the hydra makes all its attacks.
Variant Hydras Scholars of bestial lore can describe several hydra variations. Though rare, hydras with more than five heads live in very isolated areas, sometimes guarding incredibly powerful artifacts. Legendary, 12-headed miasma hydras dwell in horribly polluted and dangerous swamps. Perhaps the most unexpected danger are newly hatched hydra larvae, born hungry in shallow bogs.
name: Hydra

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hyena](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3065)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hyenas are cunning, powerfully built carnivores that bear a heavy resemblance to dogs and other canines, though they are not themselves canines. Though all hyenas are often maligned as cowardly carrion eaters, their tactics depend on their specific breed: spotted hyenas are active pack hunters that kill most of their prey themselves, while striped and brown hyenas are more likely to be loners and scavengers. Their jaws are exceptionally powerful, allowing hyenas to seize a victim and pull it to the rest of the pack.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hyaenodon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3066)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hyaenodons are ferocious primeval creatures that resemble hyenas. Looming, shaggy-furred animals nearly the size of a horse, hyaenodons are truly formidable predators to be reckoned with, due both to their size and to their flesh-shearing jaws. Hyaenodons prey on small horses, camels, and even young rhinoceroses. Kholo clans find these creatures particularly useful as mounts and guardians.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Imp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3067)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Imps are fiendish infiltrators and corrupters who, despite their diminutive stature, are more than capable of subtly influencing a weak-willed individual into performing increasingly evil acts over time. An imp will often agree to serve a mortal and act docile and loyal in a long-term plot to eventually get their master killed or damn their soul. Imps are born directly from the Outer Planes themselves, rather than from mortal souls, and thus they serve outside any fiendish hierarchies, granting them leeway to pursue their specialties.

 Despite standing a mere 2 feet tall, imps can be vicious combatants, flying out of reach and turning invisible to escape should the odds turn against them.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Imp.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Imp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3067)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 206

**Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (touch)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +6, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +7, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -1

**Dex** +4

**Con** +0

**Int** +1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 15

</row>

**Resistances**
[poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 3

**Weaknesses**
[holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 3

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
stinger +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 1d4–1 piercing plus imp venom

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 17
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
- **1st**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463)
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only)
- **4th**
[Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The imp takes on the appearance of a Medium or smaller [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) (page 358). While transformed, the imp loses their normal senses, innate spells, and special actions, but doesn't otherwise change their statistics and can still speak and use telepathy. The imp also gains any special senses of the animal and any Speeds the animal has. This doesn't change the attack and damage modifiers of their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (depending on what kinds of attacks the animal has) and prevents them from exposing creatures to imp venom.

**Fiendish Healing** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [Vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The imp regains 1d6 Hit Points.

**Fiendish Temptation** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The imp offers a non-[fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) within 15 feet a bargain, granting a boon of good luck if the creature accepts voluntarily. The boon lasts for 1 hour once accepted. Once during the hour, the creature can roll an attack roll or saving throw twice and use the higher result. If the creature dies while the boon is in place, the imp decides where the creature's soul travels. This typically makes the soul bound for eternity in the imp's home plane, and the creature unable to be raised or resurrected except by the [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual or similar magic.

**Imp Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 16 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage, clumsy 1, and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (1 round)

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Imps of Many Planes</title>

Like rats infesting cities, imps litter the fiendish Outer Planes—[Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=37), the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45), and most especially [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42). Imps love to learn the tricks of [devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=389), the better to tempt mortals and confine souls. Over centuries spent on the various planes, imps begin to take on the characteristics of the environment they inhabit, often looking flamescorched if they come from Hell, caustic and monstrous if from the Outer Rifts, and red-eyed and fetid if from Abaddon.
</aside>
name: Imp

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Iron Warden](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3068)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Traditionally crafted into the forms of giant suits of armor or powerful animals, iron wardens are products of exquisite artistry and skill. Their articulated joints and sturdy, armored bodies require great care and mathematical precision to craft, and regular cleaning and oiling ensure they don't rust over the ages. With proper care, iron wardens can remain in good shape for thousands of years, being passed down for generations, as long as they aren't destroyed by meddlesome adventurers. In addition to their incredible strength, iron wardens possess a potent toxic breath that is often more than enough to dispatch entire groups of opponents.

 Though their impressive stature prevents them from being inconspicuous, iron wardens are often placed in areas where they are easily mistaken for decorative objects. An iron warden might be hidden among decorative suits of armor or disguised as a statue in a city square. Occasionally, locals are shocked when something they assumed to be a historic landmark animates into an iron warden, called to action by an unknown mystic command. Stories tell of ancient civilizations, such as the Jistka Imperium, that created iron wardens of massive size. Most of these constructs have since been melted down for resources, but magicians and historians still chase down rumors of one of these lost colossi.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/Iron_Warden.webp" />
</column>

</row>

**Shield Arm** The iron warden has a shield built into its arm, that it can use as a steel shield (+2 to AC and Hardness 5). Because it's a part of the iron warden, all damage in excess of its Hardness is dealt only to the iron warden.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 33 

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +19 

**Will** +22 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 190

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Resistances**
physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915)), spells 15 (except [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) and spells that cause rust)

**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)** <actions string="Reaction" /> (see shield arm)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d10+12 bludgeoning

**Breathe Poison** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The iron warden exhales poisonous gas in a 10-foot burst centered on the corner of one of the iron warden's squares. The gas persists until the start of the warden's next turn. Any creature in the area (or that later enters the area) is exposed to the iron warden's poison. The warden can't Breathe Poison again for 1d4 rounds.

**Inexorable March** <actions string="Single Action" />  The iron warden Strides up to its Speed, [pushing](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) back each creature whose space it moves into and damaging them if they try to stop its movement. A creature can attempt to bar the way by succeeding at a DC 37 Fortitude save. On a critical success, the resisting creature takes no damage; otherwise, it's damaged as if hit by the iron warden's fist.

**Iron Warden Poison**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) Any [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) value from this poison is reduced by 1 every hour; **Saving Throw** DC 33 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 4 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison and drained 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 4d6 poison and drained 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 8d6 poison and drained 3 (1 round)

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Iron Scrap</title>

An iron warden can be melted down for scrap or traded to [fire giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3014) to be repurposed into armor for a Large creature.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Kholo Bonekeeper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3070)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Some kholos serve as conduits to the divine and are responsible for placating spirits and gods alike. [Lamashtu](/Deities.aspx?ID=287) is a favored deity of kholo bonekeepers, as many kholos believe their kind were birthed from the Mother of Monsters. While some kholos embrace the Old Mother completely, others see her as a nightmarish power only to be approached if they're willing to pay the price. Other popular kholo deities include [Calistria](/Deities.aspx?ID=279), [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=288), [Mahathallah](/Deities.aspx?ID=170), or [Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=293).

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Kholo Sergeant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3071)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

When kholos band together to form mercenary bands or raiding parties, the strongest or most respected among them is often designated the hunt leader or sergeant. These kholo are responsible for the safety and success of their packmates and train extensively in the art of war. Their skill at arms makes them powerful adversaries. A kholo sergeant might also serve as a mercenary group's leader in other affairs, such as sorting out domestic disputes or negotiating with rival kholo bands.

([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Kobold Warrior](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3072)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The typical kobold trains in tunnel fighting, learning to use simple weapons that are effective in narrow spaces. Kobolds are capable of landing sneaky strikes against unsuspecting foes, but are just as quick to scamper off to safety when they don't outnumber their enemies by at least two to one.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Kobold Scout](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3073)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Most kobolds encountered outside of a well-defended warren or lair are kobold scouts, those trained for stalking and hunting. They're also often in the thick of the fighting when a warren is invaded, buying time for their comrades to set up various traps and deadfalls.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Kobold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=759" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 210

**Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +3, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** +0

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +1

**Items**
[Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (20 bolts), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)

</column>

---

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +6 

+1 circumstance to all defenses vs. [traps](/Traits.aspx?ID=715)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 16

</row>

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
crossbow +9 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Construct Trap** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The kobold scout creates a rudimentary trap on any square adjacent to it. This must be on a surface, such as a floor, wall, or ceiling. The trap activates the next time a creature moves adjacent to it. The creature takes 1d6 piercing damage and 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) with a DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The trap is destroyed when activated or after 1 hour, whichever comes first. The scout typically carries enough raw materials to make one trap.

**Scamper** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The kobold scout is adjacent to at least one enemy; **Effect** The kobold scout Strides up to their Speed plus 5 feet and gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against reactions triggered by this movement. They must end this movement in a space that's not adjacent to any enemy.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The kobold scout deals an extra 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Kobold Cavern Mage](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3074)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Kobold cavern mages are born more than trained, hatching from eggs that absorbed particularly large amounts of primal earth energy. Although there are many elementals and even natural terrain features that can give rise to cavern mages, most are related to the cavern-dwelling earth nymphs known as [lampads](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1249). These lonely and capricious fey are as enthusiastic to receive the company of a kobold tribe as the kobolds are for the protection they receive in return.

<column gap="tiny">

([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Kraken](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3075)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A kraken is an enormous, squid-like leviathan with a cruel intelligence. It hunts ships, whales, and heroes alike. The hatred and envy krakens hold for [alghollthus](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=358), their rivals, has led many krakens to make their lairs in sunken cities, where they can sift through ancient lore and uncover long-lost arcane secrets.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 40

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Kraken.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 18">[Kraken](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3075)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Gargantuan" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=533" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 212

**Perception** +34; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +32, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +33

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +9

**Dex** +4

**Con** +9

**Int** +5

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 42 

**Fort** +35 

**Ref** +28 

**Will** +32 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 360

</row>

**Immunities**
[controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590)

**Resistances**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 20

**Altered Weather** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A kraken reshapes the weather within 2 miles of it, with the effect of the [_control weather_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=116) ritual centered on the kraken and based on its emotional state, at the GM's discretion. If the kraken dies, the weather returns to normal immediately.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, swim 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
arm +37 ([reach 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 4d10+17 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +37 ([reach 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 3d10+17 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +37 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 3d10+17 piercing

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 40
- **7th**
[Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651)
- **8th**
[Punishing Winds](/Spells.aspx?ID=1643)
- **10th**
[Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501) (animals only)

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** <actions string="Single Action" />  2d10+17 bludgeoning, DC 40 (page 358). On a failed save, a creature that is [holding its breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) loses 1d4 rounds worth of air.

**Double Attack** <actions string="Single Action" />  The kraken makes two Strikes with two different arms or tentacles, each limb targeting a different creature. Double Attack counts as two attacks toward the kraken's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks are made. If the kraken subsequently uses the [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) action, it Grabs any number of creatures it hit with Double Attack.

**Ink Cloud** <actions string="Single Action" />  The kraken releases a cloud of black, venomous ink in an 80- foot emanation. This cloud has no effect outside water. Creatures inside the ink cloud are exposed to kraken ink poison and are [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) while inside the cloud. The kraken can't use Ink Cloud again for 2d6 rounds, and the cloud dissipates after 1 minute.

**Jet** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The kraken moves through the water up to 280 feet in a straight line without triggering reactions.

**Kraken Ink**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) Krakens are immune to this poison; **Saving Throw** DC 39 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 poison damage and [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) (1 round); **Stage 2** 5d6 poison damage and sickened 2 (1 round)

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Kraken Locations</title>

A kraken dwells in deep ocean trenches, sunken cities, or caves and reefs near hydrothermal vents. It seeks food near the surface, however, where it can prey on seagoing vessels.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Kraken Treasure</title>

A kraken's hoard includes the plunder of ships lost at sea and the wealth of sunken cities. Virtually anything could be found in a kraken lair, but they especially covet scrolls, spell books, and other tomes of ancient lore, as well as gemstones and rarefied raw materials found only in the ocean depths.
</aside>
name: Kraken

markdown:
Krooths, sometimes called crocodile eaters, are fast and vicious hunters of bogs and wetlands. While they are known to hunt and eat [crocodiles](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2887), alligators, and virtually any creature with flesh, their favorite prey are [lizardfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=441), [boggard](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=373), and [dinosaur](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=391). Krooths are found alone or in packs. Male krooths are solitary and territorial creatures—fierce, bold, and bloodthirsty. Female krooths, on the other hand, are more likely to shy away from potential predators but swiftly turn violent when their brood is threatened. Because their offspring are so vulnerable, female krooths gather in packs to raise their young, sending smaller groups to hunt for food while the rest of the pack watches the brood. Krooths mate only once every 4 or 5 years, and the mating process is a curiously gruesome spectacle. An entire pack of females will hunt as a group for a lone male krooth, claiming their prize in a frenzy that can last for an entire day and night. After the mating has finished, the females slay their mate and devour his nutrient-rich flesh, and his organs in particular. These organs contain a unique chemical compound vital to gestation. Many naturalists will pay handsomely for the fresh remains of a male krooth so they can study the strange properties of the creature's blood and organs. In addition, krooths have poisonous, hollow teeth. When these creatures bite their prey, a tooth breaks off and causes the victim to bleed profusely as their blood pours through the hollow tooth. Whether male or female, krooths seem to be repelled by [goblinoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=618)flesh, especially that of [bugbear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=374). This doesn't mean krooths won't kill goblinoids, especially those threatening their young, but they typically do so using only their claws and tails, and they take great care to clean themselves thoroughly after such a killing. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Krooth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3076) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 213 **Perception** +16; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +6 **Int** -4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +17 **Will** +14 **HP** 150 **Pain Frenzy** Whenever the krooth is damaged by a critical hit, it gains a +2 status bonus to attack and damage rolls until the end of its next turn. It can't use reactions while this frenzy lasts.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 40 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** jaws +20 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+9 piercing plus Poison Tooth **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d8+9 slashing **Melee** tail +20 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+9 piercing **[Aquatic Ambush](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=51)** **Poison Tooth** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Requirements** The krooth damaged a creature with its jaws on its most recent action this turn; **Effect** The krooth snaps off one of its teeth in the creature it hit. The creature takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68). Neither can be healed while the tooth remains. Removing the tooth safely requires a successful DC 26 check to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396). Instead of ending bleeding or stabilizing, this removes the tooth and the drained condition, but it doesn't automatically end the bleed damage.
Krooth Guts In addition to naturalists, the strange enzymes and other chemicals found in the internal organs of male krooths, particularly the liver, pancreas, and kidneys, are of great value to alchemists who seek to concoct elixirs and potions with transmutation effects. A single male krooth's organs, properly harvested and preserved, can be sold to an interested alchemist or naturalist for as much as 80 gp.
