<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Lamia](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3077)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Just as they were cursed long ago, lamias can inflict a curse on those they touch, clouding the victim's mind and pushing conscious thought toward animalistic instincts. Creatures affected by this curse grow reckless, becoming unaware of the consequences of their own actions. This makes the hapless victim all the more susceptible to the lamia's cunning illusions and insidious charms. The lamia's animalistic nature and the effect of their cursed touch have led some scholars to theorize that the original lamias must have, millennia ago, turned away from their own reason and intellect and embraced the life of simple beasts. Whether this change was rewarded as a monstrous gift from [Lamashtu](/Deities.aspx?ID=287) or inflicted as a curse for abandoning their responsibilities by [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290), remains a subject of debate to this day.

 Whatever the source of this ancient transformation, lamias have grown to enjoy the strengths it has granted them. They continue to cling to a hatred of the gods, seeing them as the cause of their eternal exile from the societies they watch with jealous eyes while hidden amid the ruins of lost civilizations. Because lamias blame divine powers for their curse, they take special delight in the downfall of temples, the suffering and death of divine spellcasters, and the spread of dissension within organized religions.

 While they can briefly assume humanoid form with magic, lamias are usually forced to hide from civilization, making their homes in the barren wilderness. There, they attract cults of their own. With the help of these cultists, lamias strive to bring down popular faiths, introduce schisms into flourishing churches, and humiliate or defame high-profile religious leaders. Most lamias have no true religious faith in anything, hearing instead a mystical calling that manifests as sighs on the desert wind or murmurs from the dark places between the stars.

 Lamias are traditionally matriarchal, revering the eldest female among them as leader, mother, and shaman.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Lamia Matriarch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3078)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The brilliant and powerful lamia matriarchs possess boundless ambition, always seeking to bring more people and territory into the clutches of their kind. Consequently, they become rulers of other lamia. For all their cruelty toward other creatures, they're fierce protectors of other lamia, and consequently, they quickly come to rule cults or warbands. Regardless of the lamia's gender, these ascended lamias are always known as matriarchs. A lamia matriarch is set apart from their kindred by the occult power they pursue, and some have even had grand designs to break the animalistic curse that transformed them. However, every attempt so far has led to the matriarch's fall.

If the target fails additional saves against this ability, the condition value increases by 2 (to a maximum of stupefied 4). This condition value decreases by 1 every 24 hours.

**Scimitar Storm** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The lamia matriarch makes a scimitar attack against each enemy within reach. Leaf leshies are diminutive protectors of forests clad in armor and hats made of fruit, flowers, or leaves. They enjoy mock battles but act cautiously in real ones. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Leaf Leshy](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3079) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 216 **Perception** +4; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) (trees only) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +4, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +4 **Str** -1 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** -2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Items** [Longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=6) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +6 **Will** +4 **HP** 15 (Weaknesses fire 2) **Verdant Burst** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) When a leaf leshy dies, a burst of primal energy explodes from their body, restoring 1d4 Hit Points to each [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) creature in a 30-foot emanation. This area is filled with tree saplings, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). If the terrain is not a viable environment for these trees, they wither after 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet; Glide **Melee** longspear +3 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d8–1 piercing **Ranged** seedpod +6 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning plus deafening blow **Primal Innate Spells** DC 14 - **Constant (3rd)** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) (trees only) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The leaf leshy transforms into a Small tree. This ability otherwise uses the effects of [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618). **Deafening Blow** When a leaf leshy hits with their seedpod Strike, the pod explodes loudly. The target must attempt a DC 16 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is deafened for 1 minute. **Glide** ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The leshy glides gently through the air, moving 5 feet toward the ground and up to 25 feet forward. As long as the leshy spends at least 1 action gliding each round, they remain in the air at the end of each turn. For the purpose of determining [damage from falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=402), a leaf leshy always treats falls as if they were 20 feet shorter.

**Keepsake** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The leshy can store an item of light Bulk or less in their head, concealing it as [_veil of privacy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739). If stored for 24 hours, the item benefits from [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

**Verdant Burst** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) When a gourd leshy dies, a burst of primal energy explodes from their body, restoring 1d8 Hit Points to each [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) creature in a 30-foot emanation. This area is filled with gourds, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). If the terrain is not a viable environment for these plants, they wither after 24 hours.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning plus ensnare

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
seed +9 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d6+2 bludgeoning plus ensnare

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 15
- **Constant (3rd)**
[Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) (gourds only)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The leaf leshy transforms into a Small gourd-bearing plant. This ability otherwise uses the effects of [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618).

**Ensnare**   When the gourd leshy damages a creature with a fist or seed Strike, vines lash out from the leshy (or seed) and wrap around the target's limbs. The target must attempt a DC 17 Reflex save. On a failure, the target takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 round; on a critical failure, the target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 round and the penalty to Speed lasts for 1 minute.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Fungus Leshy](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3081)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Fungus leshies guard caves, bogs, and damp, dark places. Their fungus gardens are bizarre by most standards, but fungus leshies are extremely proud of their works.

<column gap="tiny">

([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 16

**Verdant Burst** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) When a fungus leshy dies, a burst of primal energy explodes from their body, restoring 2d8 Hit Points to each [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614) creature in a 30-foot emanation. This area is filled with fungi, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). If the terrain is not a viable environment for these plants, they wither after 24 hours.

This ability otherwise uses the effects of [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618).

**Spore Cloud** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) A fungus leshy can unleash a cloud of spores that irritates the eyes and throats of non-fungus creatures in a 15-foot emanation. Each creature must succeed at a DC 16 Fortitude save or take 1 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). A creature has its vision reduced as long as the persistent damage continues and can see only within 20 feet.

**Spores**   A creature that takes damage from a fungus leshy's spore pod Strike must attempt a saving throw with the same DC and effect as its Spore Cloud ability.

</column>

A wizard whose insatiable desire for arcane power eclipsed their mortal life, the lich is a truly devious and versatile spellcaster. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Lich](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3082) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 219 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +28, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +17, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +20 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Items** [_invisibility potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2944), [_scroll of teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720), [_greater staff of fire_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3041) --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +21 **Will** +23 +1 status to all saves vs. [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) **HP** 190 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83), rejuvenation) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10, physical 10 (except magical bludgeoning) **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet, DC 29
**Counterspell** **Trigger** A creature casts a spell the lich has prepared; **Effect** The lich expends a prepared spell to counter the triggering creature's casting of that same spell. The lich loses their spell slot as if they had cast the triggering spell. The lich then attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** hand +24 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 4d8 void plus siphon life **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 36, attack +26 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **1st** [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513) (×2), [Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [False Vitality](/Spells.aspx?ID=1523), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1453), [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588), [Vampiric Feast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736) - **4th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Fire Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1529), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **5th** [Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559) (×2), [Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722), [Wall of Ice](/Spells.aspx?ID=1750) - **6th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462), [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735) **Drain Soul Cage** 6th rank **Siphon Life** DC 34 **Steady Spellcasting** If a reaction would disrupt the lich's spellcasting action, the lich attempts a DC 15 flat check. On a success, the action isn't disrupted.

Despite being among the weakest linnorms, the crag linnorm is a devastating predator, capable of quickly cooking their foes with their magma breath. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Crag Linnorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3083) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 220 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +24 **Will** +22 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 270 , regeneration 10 (deactivated by [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916)) **Immunities** [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** cold iron 10 **Curse of Fire** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) When a creature slays the crag linnorm, it must succeed at a DC 35 Will save or gain weakness to fire 15 with an unlimited duration.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 60 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** jaws +30 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d12+14 piercing plus crag linnorm venom **Melee** claw +30 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing **Melee** tail +30 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d6+14 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=666) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 33 - **6th** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) - **Constant (6th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 2d6+14 bludgeoning, DC 34 **Crag Linnorm Venom** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 fire damage and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) (1 round); **Stage 2** 6d6 fire damage and drained 2 (1 round) **Magma Breath** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The crag linnorm breathes out a stream of magma in a 120-foot line that deals 12d6 fire damage to creatures within the area (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Any creature that fails its save also takes 4d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The linnorm can't use Magma Breath again for 1d4 rounds. The magma remains until the start of the linnorm's next turn. If the linnorm was on the ground, the magma remains as a burning line on the ground directly under the line of the Magma Breath; if the linnorm was airborne, the magma rains down in a sheet 60 feet high. Any creature that moves across or through the magma takes 6d6 fire damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). At the start of the linnorm's next turn, the magma cools to a thin layer of brittle stone, or the magma rain finishes falling and turns to harmless pebbles. The cooled magma quickly degrades to powder and sand over the course of several hours.
name: Crag Linnorm

The ice linnorm dwells amid glacial crevasses, atop wind-blasted mountain peaks, or within glittering caverns carved from the hearts of the mightiest of icebergs. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Ice Linnorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3084) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 221 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +32 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** -3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 41 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +28 **Will** +27 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 330 , regeneration 10 (deactivated by [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** cold iron 15, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 **Curse of Frost** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) When a creature slays the ice linnorm, it must succeed at a DC 40 Will save or gain weakness to cold 15 with an unlimited duration.
[**Reactive Strike**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48) Tail only. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 40 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** jaws +34 ([reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d12+17 piercing plus ice linnorm venom **Melee** claw +34 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing **Melee** tail +34 ([reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d6+17 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 38 - **7th** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) - **Constant (8th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 2d6+18 bludgeoning, DC 38 **Ice Linnorm Venom** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 38 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 5d6 cold damage and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) (1 round); **Stage 2** 7d6 cold damage and drained 2 (1 round) **Icemire Breath** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The ice linnorm spews out a blast of freezing, viscous ooze in a 60-foot cone that deals 15d6 cold damage to creatures within the area (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The freezing ooze clings to those struck and hardens into thick sheets of ice. A creature that fails the saving throw is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) by the ice until it succeeds at a check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) or it or an ally [Forces Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) the ice (DC 34 for either case). At the start of its turn, a creature still immobilized by the ice takes 4d6 cold damage. Another creature can free a frozen target by dealing a total of 20 fire damage to the frozen target. Left unattended, the ice crumbles away in 1 minute on its own. Creatures with the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) trait can't be frozen in place by Icemire Breath. Flying creatures [fall](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) if frozen, and swimming creatures that are frozen rise toward the surface of the water at a speed of 60 feet per round. The linnorm can't use Icemire Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Ice Linnorm

Although more powerful linnorms exist, the multi-headed tarn linnorm can wreak an awe-inspiring amount of devastation. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Tarn Linnorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3085) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 221 **Perception** +35; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +32, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +34 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +8 **Int** -1 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +8 --- **AC** 46 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +32 **Will** +31 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 400 , regeneration 15 (deactivated by [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916)) **Immunities** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** cold iron 15 **Curse of Death** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) When a creature slays the tarn linnorm, it must succeed at a DC 46 Will save or it can no longer recover Hit Points via any means, such as [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) spells, the [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) skill, or natural healing from rest. This has an unlimited duration.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 80 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** jaws +38 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing plus tarn linnorm venom **Melee** claw +38 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing **Melee** tail +38 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 4d6+18 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 42 - **8th** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) - **Constant (9th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 3d6+18 bludgeoning, DC 44 **Corrosive Breath** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The tarn linnorm can expel either a 120-foot line or a 60-foot cone of acid, dealing 20d6 acid damage to creatures within the area (DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The linnorm can't use Corrosive Breath or Double Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
The acid creates toxic fumes. At the beginning of the linnorm's next turn, those who failed the breath weapon's Reflex save must succeed at a DC 42 Fortitude save or gain [sickened 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) from the poisonous fumes. **Double Bite** The tarn linnorm Strides and then makes a jaws Strike with each of their heads, each against a different target. Both attacks count toward the tarn linnorm's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the tarn linnorm makes all of these attacks. **Double Breath** The tarn linnorm uses Corrosive Breath twice. A creature attempts only one save and can take damage only once. The linnorm can't use Corrosive Breath or Double Breath again for 2d4 rounds. **Tarn Linnorm Venom** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 44 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 7d6 acid damage and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) (1 round); **Stage 2** 11d6 acid damage and drained 2 (1 round)
name: Tarn Linnorm

As long as a character continues to suffer this curse, its slowed condition can never be reduced below slowed 1.
**Lava Affinity** The linnorm can breathe and swim freely while submerged in lava and magma.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 35 feet, climb 35 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 60 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** jaws +40 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 4d12+19 piercing plus tor linnorm venom **Melee** claw +40 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 4d8+19 slashing **Melee** tail +40 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 4d6+19 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 44 - **8th** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) - **Constant (9th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 3d6+21 bludgeoning, DC 46 **Pyroclastic Breath** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The tor linnorm expels a 60-foot cone of flame and ash dealing 20d6 fire damage to creatures within the area (DC 46 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The linnorm can't use Pyroclastic Breath again for 1d4 rounds. At the start of the tor linnorm's next turn, the area of the Pyroclastic Breath is covered in thick, scorching smoke that burns both the lungs and eyes, dealing an additional 10d6 fire damage to all creatures in the area (DC 46 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature that spends an entire round in the smoke with open eyes must succeed at a DC 44 Fortitude save or be [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 minute. The smoke dissipates after 1 minute; in strong winds, the smoke dissipates in 5 rounds, and in more powerful winds, it may clear even more quickly. **Slashing Claws** The tor linnorm makes four Strikes with their claws, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the tor linnorm's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the tor linnorm makes all their attacks. **Tor Linnorm Venom** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 44 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 8d6 fire damage and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) (1 round); **Stage 2** 12d6 fire damage and drained 2 (1 round)

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Gecko](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3087)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These roughly human-sized reptiles have amazing feet capable of clinging tenaciously to any surface. Giant geckos are typically docile and shy but can be aggressive when cornered or defending their territory. These large-eyed lizards are found in a wide variety of habitats, from rain forests and deserts to mountain slopes or even underground caverns. They spend most of their time climbing around in large trees or on rocky cliffs, whether hunting for small animals or hiding from predators. A giant gecko's scales are often quite brightly colored or decorated in intricate patterns. Giant geckos span nearly 8 feet in length from head to tail, and they weigh about 120 pounds.

 Giant geckos are sometimes used by smaller humanoids as guardians or mounts, as their docile nature makes them relatively easy to tame and train. Their superior climbing skills make giant geckos particularly popular within societies that dwell underground or near cliffs, such as [goblins](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=422) and [kobolds](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=435). Their bright coloration also makes them favorite pets in high society.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Monitor Lizard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3088)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Large and seemingly ponderous, a monitor lizard is a deceptively swift and ruthless predator. It ambushes its prey by rushing out from cover and biting the target with its powerful jaws. A giant monitor lizard's saliva is venomous, allowing it to bring down prey larger than it can easily haul away in its jaws. Giant monitor lizards grow up to 14 feet long, including their long tails, and they weigh about 350 pounds. Their bodies are normally dark brown with patches of yellow or green.

 When nesting, a giant monitor lizard digs a deep burrow to hide in. The burrow serves as both a safe haven and a location from which the lizard can ambush larger prey such as deer, boars, or even humanoids. A giant monitor lizard can consume nearly its own body weight in a single meal, and its loosely articulated jaws allow it to swallow surprisingly large prey.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Frilled Lizard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3089)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Found in warm and tropical forests and savannas, these immense lizards are a deadly threat to the unsuspecting traveler. When they aren't sleeping or stalking prey, giant frilled lizards bask in the sunlight for hours at a time. Explorers sometimes mistakenly think they are related to dragons due to their large size, elaborate neck frills (which, when displayed in a show of aggression and dominance, can be mistaken for wings), and ferocious hissing vocalizations used to frighten away creatures too large to eat.

 A giant frilled lizard's primary food sources are oversized arthropods and other invertebrates, but it won't hesitate to augment its diet with vertebrates, including other lizards, monkeys, and just about anything it can fit into its mouth—even humanoids. Although capable of short bursts of speed, a giant frilled lizard normally lies perfectly still while hunting, hoping to ambush its prey.

markdown:
A lizardfolk defender serves as a protector of the young, guardian of the settlement, and when no other options are available, a soldier in times of war. They eagerly rise to the defense of their kin but do not revel in battle. An iruxi defender would rather turn back intruders and allow them to flee with the knowledge they were beaten, in hopes that such word prevents further invasions, but they are not naive. Iruxis understand the need for revenge, and when they allow a foe to escape, they do not forget. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Lizardfolk Defender](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3090) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 226 **Perception** +7 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Iruxi](/Languages.aspx?ID=32) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +5, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +6, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +5 **Str** +3 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** -1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Deep Breath** A lizardfolk defender can [hold their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) for 15 minutes. **Items** [Flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=19), [Javelin](/Weapons.aspx?ID=71) (3), [Wooden Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=2) (Hardness 3, HP 12, BT 6) --- **AC** 16 (18 with shield raised) **Fort** +8 **Ref** +7 **Will** +5 **HP** 21 **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Lizardfolk Scout](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3091)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Iruxi scouts are the lizardfolk most often encountered by adventurers, for these unsentimental  explorers and hunters spend most of their lives on the move, constantly patrolling the territories held by their enclave. If intruders are driven off, it is the responsibility of iruxi scouts to track them, observe them, and learn their ways and weaknesses so as to report back to the community and aid in the defense against potential reprisals.

<column gap="tiny">

([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Lizardfolk Stargazer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3092)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The tradition of astrology and looking to the stars for both navigation and prognostication is well-established in iruxi society, and their wise and observant stargazers are among the people's most respected members. Even the lowliest stargazer, as presented here, has a number of useful primal spells to aid their kin; in larger iruxi settlements, stargazers wield even greater powers.

markdown:
A manticore is a monstrous amalgamation of lion, dragon, and human with a penchant for feasting on human flesh. Its distinctive tail is festooned with large quills like those of a porcupine, which the manticore flings at prey by whipping its tail like a sling. These deadly spikes give the manticore the power to slaughter even well-armed warriors from the safety of the sky. Although the anthropomorphic face of the manticore suggests a keen intellect, most creatures of its kind are single-minded killers. They can be clever liars, however, and their voices have a strangely musical sound that has lured many unwitting travelers into an ambush. A manticore discovered by uneducated or spiteful humanoids can sometimes become the center of adoration and even worship. A manticore in this situation swiftly gives in to its laziness and allows its acolytes to bring it tribute in the form of food and treasure, feasting upon its own followers when offerings become scarce. Stronger humanoids who encounter a manticore can cow it through intimidation and violence, sometimes even forcing it into service as a mount. As long as the manticore is well fed with human flesh and its master leads it to victory, a manticore mount can prove surprisingly loyal. A manticore enjoys taking plunder from the corpses of its prey as trophies that flatter its vanity and testify to its strength. It has little interest in money, but sometimes accepts gold and jewels as tribute. Because manticores prefer lonely places in the wilderness far from humanoids, they remain content to enjoy their spoils alone rather than show them off to visitors. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Manticore](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3093) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 228 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** -2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +12 **Will** +12 **HP** 90 --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** jaws +17, **Damage** 2d8+8 piercing **Melee** claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d6+8 slashing **Ranged** spike +14 ([Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d10+5 piercing **Spike Volley** The manticore flings up to two spikes from its tail, targeting either two different creatures or a single creature. If the manticore targets two different creatures, these creatures must be within 20 feet of one another, and the manticore makes a separate Strike against each; this counts as only one Strike for the manticore's multiple attack penalty, and the penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks. If the manticore flings both spikes at the same creature, it makes a single Strike. If the attack hits, it deals the damage of a single spike, but the target is pinned in place, rendering it [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81). A creature can get free if it [Forces Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) the spike or [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296); either option is DC 23.
A manticore can hurl no more than 12 spikes in 24 hours.
Scorpion Tails A common variety of manticore has a scorpion-like stinger at the end of its tail instead of quills. Remove Spike Volley and replace their ranged Strike with the following.
**Melee** stinger +17 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d8+8 piercing plus manticore venom
**Manticore Venom** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d10 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d10 poison damage and enfeebled 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d10 poison damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)
name: Manticore

</traits>

**Languages**

**Skills**

**AC** 31 

**Fort** +24 

**Ref** +20 

**Will** +18 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 220

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 50 feet, climb 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
mandibles +25 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d12+14 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
leg +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+14 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Fling** <actions string="Single Action" />  The deadly mantis flings a [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) creature into the air, up to 30 feet overhead and up to 30 feet away from the mantis (the creature takes damage from the [fall](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) as normal). If the flung creature lands on another creature, the creature it lands on takes the same amount of bludgeoning damage with a DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

**Leaping Grab** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The mantis Leaps up to 40 feet vertically and 20 feet horizontally. At any point during the jump, it can make a leg Strike. If it hits, it automatically [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target, bringing the creature along until the end of the jump.

**Rending Mandibles** <actions string="Single Action" />  The mantis makes a mandibles Strike against a creature it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77). If that Strike hits and the creature is wearing armor with Hardness 12 or lower, the armor is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60). This Strike doesn't further damage armor that's already broken.

**Sudden Strike**   On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the deadly mantis.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Sacred Insects</title>

Deadly mantises are sacrosanct to followers of [Achaekek](/Deities.aspx?ID=29), the Mantis God. His adherents, including the infamous Red Mantis assassins, invite or lure deadly mantises close to their settlements, seeing the towering creatures as a sign of their god's favor. Achaekek's followers offer sacrifices of livestock or captured enemies to keep the massive insects well fed. Clerics of Achaekek defend a deadly mantis's territory as if it were their own, believing it to be holy ground.
</aside>

markdown:
Monstrous humanoids that resemble humans with snakes instead of hair, medusas are best known for their petrifying gazes that—if lingered upon— can permanently transform mortals to stone. Medusas are shrewd and manipulative adversaries who collect and covet secrets, and who use threats and guile to exploit the fears of weaker creatures. A medusa may seek out powerful magic items, use divining magic to discover secret knowledge and unlock forbidden power, or infiltrate a society to beguile influential politicians. Their ability to worm their way into powerful organizations makes them natural leaders of criminal outfits and thieves' guilds, and their interest in magical phenomena leads some to pursue careers as oracles who offer to help adventurers find what they seek—for a price. Of course, if wit and deception prove insufficient, a medusa can always simply turn rivals into ornate stone decorations with little more than a glare. Many medusas build elaborate lairs to call home, often decorated with the statues of their foes turned into macabre trophies on prominent display. Exceptionally agile and surprisingly hardy, a medusa rarely backs down from a conflict, even when cornered. Many adventurers who thought themselves readied to resist the effects of a medusa's gaze have nevertheless fallen to a medusa, as these creatures are also often deadly archers able to riddle their foes with venom-coated arrows from a distance. Still, a medusa may barter for their life if no alternatives remain, and the secrets carried by these powerful villains often make it more than worth sparing their lives. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Medusa](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3096) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 230 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +16, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16 **Str** +2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 **Items** _+1 [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=435)_ (60 arrows), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43) --- **AC** 25 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +15 **Ref** +16 **Will** +14 **HP** 105 **Petrifying Gaze** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 25 Fortitude save. If the creature fails, it becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 minute. The medusa can deactivate or activate this aura by using a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait.
**Biting Snakes** **Trigger** A creature ends its turn adjacent to the medusa; **Effect** The medusa makes a snake fangs Strike against the creature. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** shortsword +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+8 piercing plus serpent venom **Melee** snake fangs +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d4+8 piercing plus serpent venom **Ranged** _composite shortbow_ +19 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+7 piercing plus serpent venom **Focus Gaze** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The medusa fixes their glare at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Fortitude save against the medusa's petrifying gaze. If the creature was already slowed by petrifying gaze before attempting its save, a failed save causes it to be [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87) permanently. After attempting its save, the creature is then temporarily immune until the start of the medusa's next turn. **Serpent Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)
Medusa Infiltrators Rumors persist of disguised medusas acting as prominent members of criminal organizations such as the Sczarni in Riddleport and the Aspis Consortium in Port Peril, and their kind is known to thrive in metropolises including Absalom and Katapesh. Because they are so widespread, medusas resembling humans of every major ethnicity can be found in Avistan and Garund.
name: Medusa

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Minotaur](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3099)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A minotaur is a large humanoid with bovine features such as horns, hooves, and a layer of hair that covers their entire body. Their head also resembles that of a bull or cow, though with eyes that brim with curiosity or fury, depending on the minotaur's temperament. Though often mistaken for aggressive brutes due to their size and reputation, many minotaurs are skilled artisans who spend much of their lives perfecting their craft. Minotaur communities tend to be insular and are found at the heart of a cunning labyrinth or within a tangle of underground caverns.

 The myth many minotaurs like to tell of their origin involves a stonemason living in ancient Iblydos. After accidentally insulting a hero-god, he was cursed to become the first minotaur. He then retreated into a series of caves beneath a temple he had built, but continued his work, sculpting stone statues for any who dared to brave the subterranean passages.

 Sometimes, a lone minotaur is compelled, exiled, or chooses to live alone within a maze, a warren, or old ruins. This solitude drives them to become a monstrous tormentor who delights in hunting any who stumble across their lair. They slowly close in on their prey, thrilling in the terror of the hunted becoming lost within corridors the minotaur knows all too well. Only then does the minotaur charge in for the kill, cutting foes down with powerful strikes or impaling them on their sharp horns. Unfortunately, the world tends to judge all minotaurs by the stories of these lone, vicious hunters.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Minotaur_Hunter.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>

<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 232

**Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +0

**Con** +3

**Cha** -1
</row>

**Perfect Orienteering** A minotaur automatically critically succeeds at [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) or [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408).

**Items**
[Greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=21)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +13 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +10 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 70

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
greataxe +14 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 1d12+8 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
horn +14,
**Damage** 1d8+8 piercing

**Axe Swipe** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The minotaur swings their axe in a wide arc, making greataxe Strikes against any two foes who are adjacent to each other and within the minotaur's reach. The multiple attack penalty does not increase until after both attacks are resolved.

**Hunted Fear** <actions string="Single Action" />  The minotaur snorts and clomps as they hunt their prey, inspiring terror. The minotaur makes an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) all living creatures within 60 feet that can hear the minotaur but not see them. Roll once and apply the result to all creatures. If the targets are in a maze or similarly difficult-to-navigate structure, the minotaur gains a +4 circumstance bonus to this check. Creatures that become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) as a result also take a –2 circumstance penalty to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to avoid getting lost for 1 minute. Each target is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

**Powerful Charge** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The minotaur Strides twice, then makes a horn Strike. If they moved at least 20 feet from their starting position, the Strike's damage is increased to 2d8+10.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Variant Minotaurs</title>

Most minotaurs are a simple blend of muscular humanoid and raging bull, but unusual variants and unique minotaurs may have different physical features and abilities. Great-horned minotaurs can impale foes if they critically succeed on a charge, but they do not have the Hunted Fear ability. Shaggy minotaurs are covered in thick hair form head to toe, allowing them to live in freezing environments with ease and giving them resistance to cold damage, but their hooves are broad and heavy, making it impossible for them to charge.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-444" />
name: Minotaur Hunter

Fearsome centipede-like creatures, mukradis are three-headed predators with a devastating array of ways to kill, burn, and dismember. A version of the mukradi that dwells in the Darklands is rumored to exist. It's said these variant mukradis have black scales, and all of their heads spew a black, acidic goo that animates before being reabsorbed by the mukradis. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Mukradi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3100) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 233 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +32 **Str** +9 **Dex** +0 **Con** +7 **Int** -3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 37 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +32 **Ref** +23 **Will** +26 **HP** 300 **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) 20, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 20, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 20 **Partitioned Anatomy** **Trigger** The mukradi would be [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92), or [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93); **Effect** The mukradi confines the debilitating effect to a certain portion of its nervous system, ignoring the effect but causing a maw of its choice to go dormant for the effect's duration. That maw can't be used for a Strike or to Breathe Energy during that time. This ability can't be used if all the mukradi's heads are dormant.
**Spitting Rage** **Trigger** A creature scores a critical hit on the mukradi; **Effect** The mukradi's Breathe Energy recharges. It can use Breathe Energy immediately as part of this reaction. It can't use this reaction again until it recharges Breathe Energy naturally. --- **Speed** 60 feet, burrow 60 feet, climb 60 feet **Melee** acid maw +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+17 piercing plus 3d6 acid **Melee** flame maw +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+17 piercing plus 3d6 fire **Melee** shock maw +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+17 piercing plus 3d6 electricity **Melee** leg +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+17 piercing **Melee** tail lash +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+17 slashing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46) **Breathe Energy** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The mukradi breathes a blast of energy from one of its three heads; each creature in the area must attempt a DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The mukradi can't Breathe Energy again for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Acid Maw** ([acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524)) 10-foot-wide, 60-foot line of acid dealing 16d6 acid damage.
  • **Flame Maw** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) 60-foot cone of fire dealing 16d6 fire damage.
  • **Shock Maw** ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)) 120-foot line of electricity dealing 16d6 electricity damage.
**Pull Apart** The mukradi makes two Strikes with different maws against the same target. If both hit, the target takes an extra 2d12+13 slashing damage, with a DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. On a critical failure, the creature is torn to pieces and dies. The mukradi's multiple attack penalty increases only after all the attacks are made. **Thrash** The mukradi Strikes once against each creature in its reach. It can make one of these Strikes with each of its maws, one with its tail lash, and the rest with its legs. Each attack takes a –2 circumstance penalty and counts toward the mukradi's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks are made. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Huge or smaller, leg, DC 36
From a God's Nightmares The first mukradis are rumored to have spawned in the fevered nightmares of a sleeping demigod from a dimension beyond dreams, who perished as the first mukradis hollowed out their unconscious mind and used their flesh to transition into the mortal realm. This legend is likely nothing more than fancy, but it certainly speaks to the deadly nature of these immense monsters.
name: Mukradi

markdown:
The majority of mummy guardians were created by cruel and selfish masters to serve as guardians to protect their tombs from intruders. The traditional method of creating a mummy guardian is a laborious and sadistic process that begins well before the poor soul to be transformed is dead. The victim is ritualistically starved of nourishing food and instead fed strange spices, preservative agents, and toxins intended to quicken the desiccation of the flesh. The victim remains immobile but painfully aware during the final stages, as its now-useless entrails are extracted. The victim is then shrouded in funerary wrappings and entombed within a necromantically ensorcelled sarcophagus to await instructions in the potentially distant future. While it's certainly possible to use other methods to create a mummy guardian from an already-deceased body, those who seek to create these foul undead as their guardians in the afterlife often feel that such methods result in inferior undead—the pain and agony of death by mummification being an essential step in the process. Regardless of the method of their creation, mummy guardians are more than just physical shells of flesh and bone. They retain fragmented, distorted versions of their minds, with only enough memories of their living personality remaining to fuel their undead anger and jealousy of those who yet live. This burning rage only intensifies over the centuries of waiting within a crypt for the chance to actually act, and thus, when most mummy guardians are awoken by tomb robbers or adventurers, they stop at nothing in pursuit of spiteful slaughter. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Mummy Guardian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3101) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 234 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); plus any one language they knew while alive **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** +4 **Dex** +0 **Con** +2 **Int** -2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +10 **Will** +16 **HP** 125 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Weaknesses** [alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=528) 5 (see alchemical weakness), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5 **Alchemical Weakness** The guardian's weakness to alchemical items not only applies to damage from [alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=528) items, but the guardian also takes 5 damage when splashed with non-damaging alchemical items or dosed with alchemical poisons, even if they're immune to their other effects.
**Blighted Consumption** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet eats or drinks (including an alchemical item or potion); **Effect** The food or drink burns like the caustic substances fed to the mummy before its death. If the creature fails a DC 24 Fortitude save, they become [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) after they finish the consumption and can't reduce their sickened condition while within 30 feet of any mummy. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** fist +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d10+7 bludgeoning plus Choking Pain **Choking Pain** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Requirements** The mummy's last action was a successful fist Strike; **Effect** The mummy shares the pain of its dying moments with the target of that Strike. That creature takes 3d8 void damage with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save. If the creature critically fails the saving throw, it can't speak for 1 round, including to Cast a Spell.
name: Mummy Guardian

markdown:
While mummy guardians are undead crafted from the corpses of sacrificed— usually unwilling victims—and retain only fragments of their memories, a mummy pharaoh is the result of a deliberate embrace of undeath by a sadistic and cruel ruler. The transformation from life to undeath beneath the scorching desert sand is only somewhat less awful, but as the transition is an intentional bid to escape death by a powerful personality who fully embraces the blasphemous repercussions of the choice, the mummy pharaoh retains its memories and personality intact. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Mummy Pharaoh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3102) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 235 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); plus any two languages they knew while alive **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +15, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When a mummy pharaoh is destroyed, necromantic energies rebuild its body in its tomb over 1d10 days. If the body is destroyed during that time, the process starts anew. A reforming mummy pharaoh is destroyed permanently if their tomb is [_consecrated_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=115). **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=361)_ --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +15 **Will** +20 +1 status to all saves vs. [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) **HP** 175 (sacred wrappings, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10, [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) 10 **Undead Mastery** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 100 feet. Commanded or allied [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) in the aura that have a lower level than the mummy pharaoh gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, AC, saves, and skill checks.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** The mummy pharaoh can use Reactive Strike when a creature within its reach uses a [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) action, in addition to its normal trigger. It can disrupt triggering concentrate actions, and it disrupts actions on any hit, not just a critical hit.
**Sacred Wrappings** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When a creature deals physical damage to the pharaoh or triggers one of the pharaoh's weaknesses, it must succeed at a DC 28 Will save or become [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67). Regardless of the results of the save, the creature is then immune to that mummy's sacred wrappings for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _longspear_ +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+11 piercing plus 1d6 void **Melee** fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d10+11 bludgeoning plus 1d6 void **Sandstorm Wrath** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The mummy pharaoh exhales a 60-foot cone of superheated sand that deals 5d6 fire and 5d6 slashing damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The mummy pharaoh can't use Sandstorm Wrath again for 1d4 rounds. **Veil of Sand** ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) Sand whirls around the mummy pharaoh in a 5-foot emanation until the beginning of their next turn. Creatures inside the sand are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to those outside it and any living creature ending its turn within the sand takes 4d6 slashing damage with a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. Veil of Sand ends if the mummy takes damage from their water weakness.
name: Mummy Pharaoh

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Smaranava (Clouded Naga)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3103)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Many view clouded nagas as jealous, malevolent creatures. Only those with the courage to see clearly can recognize these beings for what they truly are: tragic, wounded beings who have been trapped and corrupted by fate. The betrayal and beheading of the naga's mother goddess, [Ravithra](/Deities.aspx?ID=218), cascaded trauma onto all of her creations. Smaranavas have never recovered from this divine wound, and these cursed serpents live wretched half-lives, attempting to fulfill Ravithra's forgotten purpose by tempting and testing mortals they encounter. In this way, they separate the wicked from the righteous, the worthy from the unworthy.

 Smaranavas have dark scales that are rendered dull gray by stuck shed skin, their eyes a milky white due to opaque caps over their eyes. Many ritually scar their necks out of sorrow for their mother Ravithra. Legends claim that the wise and enlightened can free clouded naga from their fates, allowing them to shed their forms and emerge as an awakener naga.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Vicharamuni (Awakener Naga)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3104)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Awakener nagas are benevolent and wise teachers of people and places. They seek to guide and nurture the world around them, sensing mortals or locales with potential supernatural significance. Once located, these nagas provide instruction and divine trials to forge their wards toward strength and to impart a deeper understanding of right and wrong. Though considered stern and intimidating taskmasters by their students, those taught by a vicharamuni often go on to become great heroes.

 Awakener nagas are similarly attentive in guiding their young. Upon reaching adulthood, a naga is provided a final trial before being encouraged to depart the nest and seek out their own natural wonders or promising mortals. Sometimes generations of nagas might guard the same place or family line, passing the honor of such care from parent to child. In such cases, the parent nagas aim to have at least one of their children elect to stay behind and become the guardian of their ancestral ward, giving the parents peace of mind that the site they protect will continue to be guarded by their descendants.

Nightmares are flaming equine harbingers of death. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22 **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Nightmare](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3105) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 238 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +12 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Smoke** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 15 feet. The nightmare continually exhales black smoke. Creatures within the aura are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to those outside it, and creatures outside the aura are concealed to creatures within it. Nightmares and their riders can see through this smoke.
A creature that begins its turn in the area must succeed at a DC 23 Fortitude save or be [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91). It's then temporarily immune to being sickened by the smoke for 1 minute. This is an [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), and the nightmare and its rider are immune to it. --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +15 **Will** +12 **HP** 100 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 90 feet **Melee** jaws +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d10+8 piercing **Melee** hoof +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d8+8 bludgeoning plus 1d6 fire **Divine Innate Spells** DC 24 - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (self and rider only) **Flaming Gallop** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The nightmare Strides or [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312)up to triple its Speed. Its hooves burst with intense flame, dealing 3d6 fire damage with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save to each creature other than the nightmare's rider that the nightmare moves adjacent to during its gallop. Each creature can be affected only once during a single use of Flaming Gallop.
name: Nightmare

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Nilith](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3107)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The sleek, dark niliths resemble hairless, emaciated tree sloths. These creatures are intensely dangerous and fearsome, with glowing red eyes, wicked claws, and mouths full of needlelike teeth. Niliths feed off the emotions, fears, and flesh of the living, and folktales posit that those who have nightmares of these creatures are fated to one day be eaten by them. Niliths draw intense joy from tormenting sapient creatures, often focusing their predations on the pious and the just, from whom they draw forth their most basic fears and worst thoughts and revel in their victims' subsequent terror. More often than not, these despicable manipulators wish to drive their victims into madness and even to suicide. Most niliths lack the patience to spend too much time fully tearing down an individual, and when a nilith tires of its current plaything and becomes bored with its particular thoughts and fears, it murders the quarry before feeding on its flesh and moving onto the next victim.

 Niliths are actually extensions of much deadlier creatures that dwell in a distant dimension beyond dreams—in a way, niliths are little more than remote feeding machines for the unknown alien entities to which they are connected. Scholars and dimensional travelers have attempted to uncover the exact mechanisms of this mysterious connection, but they have yet to decipher the truth. Indeed, many who investigate the nature of a nilith's bond are driven to madness before getting anywhere close. The odd connection to otherworldly beings might help explain the longevity of these creatures, as it is believed that niliths can live for thousands of years.

 Thankfully for others, niliths are solitary creatures that hate the company of their own kind, likely because these harbingers of nightmares have no wish to taste the horrors they bring to others.

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 29
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)

The grabbed creature takes 6d6 mental damage with a DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save. On a critical failure, the target is also affected as though by [_never mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614), and it must attempt a second Will save against that effect.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">From the Dreamlands</title>

The nilith's association with the remote and little-understood [Dreamlands](/Planes.aspx?ID=47), also known as the Dimension of Dreams, is unusual. They draw power from a latent dreaming connection to that realm, but they are native to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26).
</aside>

markdown:
Ancient beyond imagining, norns are powerful fey women who hold in their hands the physical manifestation of fate and destiny in the form of golden thread. They watch over all life, intervening with reluctance when called upon—or with a vengeance when the strands of fate are twisted and abused by lesser beings. They cut imposing figures, standing 14 feet tall and weighing 800 pounds. Norns' relationship with the Eldest of the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35) is complex. Many among norns serve [Magdh](/Deities.aspx?ID=97) the Three, the triune Eldest who some norns believe to be the first norn triumvirate bound together into one entity, as Magdh has three bodies: a Maiden, a Mother, and a Matriarch. Magdh claims to be watching the threads of fate for some ominous prophesied cataclysm, and in addition to assisting in her divinations, Magdh expects the norns who serve her to follow her cryptic commands to help nudge the future away from the brink. However, norns are powerful beings in their own right, themselves capable of granting divine power, and many balk at serving the enigmatic demigod. These norns find the other Eldest even more alien and challenging to interact with, for they believe that while the Eldest wield great power, even these powerful beings should not be granted leave to meddle with fate as much as they desire. While even the weakest of the Eldest could destroy an unaffiliated norn with ease, they tend to obey the proclamations and judgments of norns when they are spoken. These norns, for their part, use their perceived neutrality judiciously. They know better than to issue too many demands to the Eldest, lest the capricious demigods grow frustrated. And so the balance of power remains tenuous between unaffiliated norns and the Eldest, as it has for eons. Norns know that it's merely a matter of time before the Eldest lose their respect for this tradition and start acting entirely as they please, despite norns' best efforts to rein in their most disruptive actions. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Norn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3108) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 240 **Perception** +41; [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 120 feet, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +36, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +35, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +37, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +28, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +38, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +34, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +31, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +34 **Str** +7 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +6 **Wis** +10 **Cha** +7 **Sense Fate** ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) A norn automatically rolls a 20 when she rolls initiative.
**Triumvirate** This functions as the [coven](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=58) ability, except only norns can join a triumvirate, and it functions only as long as exactly three norns are part of the triumvirate. A triumvirate grants the following spells: [_cataclysm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1460), [_foresight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537), [_manifestation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592) (once per day), [_pinpoint_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633), and [_revival_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1654). **Items** [Shears](/Weapons.aspx?ID=119) (see favored weapon) --- **AC** 46 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +30 **Will** +38 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 375 , regeneration 20 (deactivated by [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916)) **Immunities** [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Weaknesses** cold iron 20 --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 35 feet **Melee** shears +38 ([deadly 2d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 4d4+15 slashing plus 6d6 void and sever fate **Melee** hand of fate +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+15 void plus sever fate **Occult Innate Spells** DC 42 - **7th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (at will), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647) (at will), [Spellwrack](/Spells.aspx?ID=1683) (at will) - **8th** [Migration](/Spells.aspx?ID=1600), [Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644) - **9th** [Execute](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519), [Phantasmagoria](/Spells.aspx?ID=1629), [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652) - **10th** [Freeze Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=1538) - **Constant (10th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Hidden Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556), [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Rituals** DC 42 - **3rd** [Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) - **7th** [Collective Memories](/Rituals.aspx?ID=113) **Fated** When a creature is subject to a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect from a norn and a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect from any source other than a norn (or vice versa), the norn's effect automatically [counteracts](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the other effect and then takes place normally, rather than the two effects canceling each other out. If both the fortune and misfortune effect are from a norn, then the two cancel each other out as normal. At the GM's discretion, powerful entities related to fate or luck, like [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281), [Magdh](/Deities.aspx?ID=97), or [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290), can't have their effects negated by this ability. **Sever Fate** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) When a norn deals void damage with a Strike, she regains 10 Hit Points. The target must succeed at a DC 39 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) (drained 2 on a critical failure). Further void damage dealt by the norn increases the drained condition value by 1 on a failed save (or by 2 on a critical failure), to a maximum of drained 4. **Shift Fate** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature within 120 feet attempts a saving throw; **Effect** The creature rolls the saving throw twice, and then the norn decides which result applies. If the norn chooses the lower roll, this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect; if she chooses the higher roll, it's a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect; if they're the same, she decides which trait to apply. **Snip Thread** ([Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The norn produces a golden thread linked to the fate of a creature within 100 feet of her, then snips it short with her shears. The target takes 100 void damage (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). If the target is reduced to 0 Hit Points from this damage, the thread is completely severed and the creature dies immediately. A creature slain by Snip Thread can't be restored to life except by a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual or similarly powerful magic; or by divine intervention. Regardless of the outcome of their saving throw, a creature targeted by Snip Thread then becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours. The norn can't use Snip Thread again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Norn
