markdown:
This construct is a walking alchemical nightmare capable of inflicting all manner of painful wounds. The noxious needlers' ability to follow orders is granted by the otherwise mindless humanoid brain that floats in their dome-like heads, allowing them to serve as laborers and guards for their creators. In exceptionally rare cases, the brain used in their creation might retain fragments of memories or even an actual intellect, resulting in a noxious needler with a personality and agenda of its own. Unwilling creations often hunt down their creators, venting their rage on similar targets if revenge is impossible. Others blankly replicate the experiments from their last memory. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Noxious Needler](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3109) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 242 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +19 **Will** +15 **HP** 150 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) or bludgeoning), spells 10 (except [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Alchemical Chambers** A noxious needler's body contains six alchemical chambers filled with different substances. When a noxious needler's ability calls upon a randomly determined alchemical effect, roll 1d6 and consult the following (if you roll the result of a chamber that was shattered, there is no alchemical effect): 1 acid damage; 2 cold damage; 3 electricity damage; 4 fire damage; 5 poison damage; 6 sickness, with a DC 26 Fortitude save or [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) (sickened 2 on a critical failure).
**Alchemical Rupture** When a noxious needler takes physical damage from a critical hit or is affected by a [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670) spell, one glass chamber within its body shatters, spewing alchemical liquid in a 5-foot emanation. Roll on the alchemical chambers list (see above) to determine which one shatters—on a roll of 1–5, creatures in the area take 10d6 damage of the appropriate type (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex). On a roll of 6, creatures must instead save against the sickness effect. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** syringe +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+6 piercing plus alchemical injection **Ranged** bomb +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** see Generate Bomb **Alchemical Injection** When a noxious needler hits a creature with a syringe Strike, roll 1d6 on the alchemical chambers list to determine the additional effect of the attack. The syringe deals an additional 2d6 damage of the appropriate type (or exposes the target to the sickness effect, as appropriate). **Generate Bomb** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The needler fills an empty vial from one of its alchemical chambers to create a bomb and then makes a bomb Strike. Roll 1d6 on the alchemical chambers list above. On a roll of 1–5, the bomb deals 3d10 damage and 3 [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage, matching the damage type of the chamber; you can instead choose to create an alchemical bomb of 11th level or lower that deals the same damage type, such as an [acid flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2875) on a roll of 1. On a roll of 6, it creates a sickness bomb, which exposes the target and all creatures in the splash radius to the sickness effect; creatures hit by only the splash receive a +2 circumstance bonus to their Fortitude saves.
Alchemical Leftovers When a noxious needler is defeated or disabled, each of its alchemical chambers that remains intact can be salvaged. The alchemical fluids inside are 50 gp worth of ingredients for crafting alchemical items.
name: Noxious Needler

markdown:
When pollution despoils a natural waterway, it draws the dreaded nuckelavee to it from the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35). This spirit of wrath is a grisly sight to behold: a horselike monstrosity with the gnarled upper body of a humanoid growing directly from its back. Further enhancing its awfulness, not a patch of skin exists on the misshapen hybrid form, as though it survived its own flaying. When a nuckelavee rides forth from its domain, it wreaks a trail of destruction across the land surrounding its path. Nuckelavees are considered among the cruelest and most monstrous fey, seen by some as just desserts visited upon those who would befoul the waters of their homes. A nuckelavee, however, doesn't discriminate between those who pollute and those who merely have the misfortune to be in the wrong place at the wrong time. Despite their vile reputation among humanoids, nuckelavees are generally respectful of their fey counterparts. Once pollution has been cleansed and water fey like [naiads](/Monsters.aspx?ID=311) return to a body of water, nuckelavees will peacefully withdraw. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Nuckelavee](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3110) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 243 **Perception** +16; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [bastard sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=370)_ --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +16 **Will** +20 **HP** 190 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 30 feet, DC 25
**Purity Vulnerability** Unpolluted fresh water burns a nuckelavee like acid, dealing 1d6 damage to it and causing it to be [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91). A nuckelavee can't heal from damage when it's in an area that isn't polluted (subject to GM discretion).
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 40 feet, swim 40 feet **Melee** _bastard sword_ +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [two-hand d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 2d8+12 slashing plus 1d6 poison and mortasheen **Melee** jaws +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d8+12 piercing plus 1d6 poison and mortasheen **Melee** hoof +20, **Damage** 2d6+12 bludgeoning plus mortasheen **Primal Innate Spells** DC 28 - **3rd** [Aqueous Orb](/Spells.aspx?ID=1443) - **5th** [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473) **Rituals** DC 28 - **4th** [Blight](/Rituals.aspx?ID=4) **Blight Breath** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The nuckelavee breathes a 30-foot cone of foulness, dealing 8d6 void damage to living creatures in the area with a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature that fails also takes 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The nuckelavee can't use Blight Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Mortasheen** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) The target can't recover from the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) condition caused by mortasheen until the disease is cured. Mortasheen gains the [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=726) trait against [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) and [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668); **Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Stage 1** Carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) and fatigued (1 day); **Stage 3** drained 2 and fatigued (1 day); **Stage 4** dead **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Medium or smaller, hoof, DC 28
Unfortunate Victims Nuckelavees are equally delighted to murder and feed upon both hapless peasants and altruistic naturalists engaged in the process of cleaning up pollution. Indeed, those who would seek to purify such sites are often regarded as the greater threat by a nuckelavee, as without a befouled land to dwell in, the foul fey would wither away.
name: Nuckelavee

Naiads protect streams, ponds, springs, and other natural bodies of fresh water. While most naiads lead solitary lives close to their chosen home, sometimes they congregate in coven-like groups where river tributaries meet, performing great magic and blessing the waters of the land. Because naiads' bonds to their bodies of water permit more flexibility, they are the nymphs most likely to interact with humanoids—and even visit their settlements on occasion. Unlike other nymphs, naiads occasionally become adventurers, especially when dark forces seek to despoil nature or otherwise threaten the land, joining forces with others to prevent the corruption of the natural world. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Naiad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3111) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 244 **Perception** +6; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +3, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +7, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +4 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +4 **Animal Empathy** The naiad can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) skill with [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531). --- **AC** 14 **Fort** +3 **Ref** +6 **Will** +8 **HP** 20 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 3 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 3 **Water Dependent** A naiad is bonded to a spring, pond, or similar-sized water feature. If she is more than 300 feet away from it for 24 hours or more, she gains the [weak](/Rules.aspx?ID=791) adjustments until she returns. She can perform a 24-hour ritual to bond herself to a new body of water.
**Water Healing** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) For every 10 minutes a naiad spends soaking in her bonded body of water, she regains 7 Hit Points. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 25 feet **Melee** aqueous fist +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)), **Damage** 1d8 bludgeoning **Ranged** water orb +8 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)), **Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 17 - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476), [Hydraulic Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=1561), [Tidal Surge](/Spells.aspx?ID=1848) (at will)

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dryad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3112)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Dryads are fey guardians of the trees and creatures who dwell in wooded areas. They prefer using indirect methods to dissuade those who would harm their sacred groves and beloved forests, but they're not above using magic to enlist the aid of allies when evil threats cannot be dissuaded with words alone. In times of peace, dryads happily live secluded lives inside their trees, and a community at harmony with nature might not even realize a dryad lives nearby.

 Though they watch over all the woods around them, dryads are inextricably tied to a specific tree, usually an oak. Dryads who are bonded to another type of tree are fundamentally the same, but they may differ in temperament and appearance to match their chosen ward. For instance, kraneiai, or cherry-tree dryads, have beautiful pink coloration and concern themselves with the fragile beauty of life.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Dryad.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Dryad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3112)</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=599" />

</traits>

**Perception** +10; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +5, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +7, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +9, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Nature Empathy** The dryad can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) skill with [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) and [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +10 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 55

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5

**Tree Dependent** A dryad is bonded to a single great tree. If she is more than 300 feet away from it for 24 hours or more, she gains the [weak](/Rules.aspx?ID=791) adjustments until she returns. She can perform a 24-hour ritual to bond herself to a new tree.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
branch +12 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 1d12+2 bludgeoning

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 20, attack +12
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713)
- **2nd**
[Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515) (at will), [One with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) (at will)
- **4th**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (×3), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693)
- **5th**
[Nature's Pathway](/Spells.aspx?ID=1613) (×2)

**Tree Meld** <actions string="Two Actions" />  A [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) spell cast by a dryad has an unlimited duration. In addition, if the dryad merges with her bonded tree, she can choose to instead enter an extradimensional living space within the tree, and can bring up to two adjacent, willing creatures with her; the spell gains the [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=596) trait. The dryad can still be expelled from this space.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-449" />

Naiad queens rule over pristine wildernesses centered around untouched lakes, massive rivers, or other collective bodies of fresh water. Bards' songs and artists' paintings of these powerful nymphs tend to depict naiad queens in their slightly more humanoid forms, which they don when they make the rare journey into civilized lands to garner allies, gather news, or gauge threats. Most naiad queens treat those who respect their domains with kindness, but they're fierce—and quick to eliminate foes. Their blinding beauty and breadth of offensive spells make naiad queens fierce opponents if forced into a fight. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Naiad Queen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3113) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 246 **Perception** +18; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +20, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +15, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +15, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +15 **Str** +0 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +7 **Animal Empathy** The naiad queen can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) skill with [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531).
**Tied to the Land** The queen is tied to a body of water or area with a great deal of water features. --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +18 **Will** +17 **HP** 100 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10 **Nymph's Beauty** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. DC 23. A creature that fails its save is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) and becomes stunned 1 each time it starts its turn within the aura for the next 24 hours, even if it can't see the naiad queen.
**Water Healing** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) Water Healing (healing, primal, vitality) For every 10 minutes a naiad queen spends soaking in any body of water in her domain, she regains 30 Hit Points. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 25 feet **Melee** aqueous fist +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)), **Damage** 2d8+6 bludgeoning **Ranged** water orb +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)), **Damage** 4d6 bludgeoning **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 28, attack +18 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) - **1st** [Air Bubble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1438), [Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550) - **2nd** [Animal Messenger](/Spells.aspx?ID=1441), [One with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) - **3rd** [Aqueous Orb](/Spells.aspx?ID=1443), [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) - **4th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Summon Animal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1694) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 28 - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476), [Hydraulic Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=1561), [Tidal Surge](/Spells.aspx?ID=1848) (at will) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Focus Beauty** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) If a target already affected by nymph's beauty fails its save, the image of the queen sears into the creature's mind, effectively [blinding](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) the creature until its vision is restored with [_sound body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1679) or a similar effect. The naiad queen can Dismiss the effect. **Inspiration** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676))
name: Naiad Queen

markdown:
Also called a hamadryad, a dryad queen rules over an entire forest, or a portion of an incredibly large forest, leading and protecting all dryads within. Dryad queens often have strange relationships with powerful and deadly fey, working together in a dualistic way despite their differences, with the queen representing nature's wonders and the other fey representing nature's wrath. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 33 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Dryad Queen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3114) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 247 **Perception** +25; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +23, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +30, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +24, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +24 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +8 **Nature Empathy** The dryad queen can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) skill with [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) and [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668).
**Tied to the Land** The dryad queen is tied to a forest or other woodland. --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +26 **Will** +24 **HP** 220 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 **Nymph's Beauty** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) DC 30. On a failed save, the target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) in awe for 1 minute. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** branch +27 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d12+8 bludgeoning **Ranged** leaves +27 ([Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8+6 slashing **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 35, attack +25 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) - **1st** [Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550), [Pest Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1626) - **2nd** [Animal Messenger](/Spells.aspx?ID=1441), [Clear Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) - **3rd** [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752) - **4th** [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Mountain Resilience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651) - **5th** [Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559) - **6th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462), [Cursed Metamorphosis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479), [Tangling Creepers](/Spells.aspx?ID=1714) - **7th** [Regenerate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1648), [Summon Plant or Fungus](/Spells.aspx?ID=1705) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 35, attack +25 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (at will), [Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515) (at will), [Shape Wood](/Spells.aspx?ID=1668) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (at will) - **5th** [Nature's Pathway](/Spells.aspx?ID=1613) (×3), [One with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) (at will) - **8th** [Impaling Briars](/Spells.aspx?ID=1857) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Focus Beauty** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) On a failed save, if the target was already affected by the dryad queen's beauty, the target can't use [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against the dryad queen for 1 hour. **Inspiration** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Tree Meld** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) spell cast by a dryad queen has an unlimited duration. In addition, if the dryad merges with a tree in her domain, she can choose to instead enter an extradimensional living space within the tree, and can bring up to eight adjacent, willing creatures with her; the spell gains the [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=596) trait. The dryad queen can still be expelled from this space.
name: Dryad Queen

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Octopus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3115)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Giant octopuses are found in the heart of deep, dark oceans. Clever and adaptable, they hunt and devour all manner of animals. Despite growing up to 16 feet long, a giant octopus can compress its body to squeeze through small gaps as long as there's room for its beak.

 Giant octopuses favor shipwrecks, coral reefs, or underwater caverns as lairs, where they can take advantage of narrow confines for protection. Like their smaller kin, they're fond of adorning and decorating their lairs with found objects—many of which, in the giant octopus's case, are also magical weapons, shields, or works of art salvaged from sunken ships or fallen adventurers.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Giant_Octopus.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=533" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 248

**Perception** +15; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** -4

**Wis** +3

**Cha** -2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 135

</row>

**Resistances**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, swim 40 feet; compression

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
arm +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +20,
**Damage** 2d8+9 piercing plus giant octopus venom

**Compression**   A giant octopus can move through a gap at least 2 feet wide without [Squeezing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372)and can Squeeze through a gap at least 1 foot wide.

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** <actions string="Single Action" />  1d8+9 bludgeoning, DC 26

**Giant Octopus Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 26 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage, [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), and off-guard (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d6 poison damage, clumsy 2, and off-guard (1 round)

**Ink Cloud** <actions string="Single Action" />  The octopus emits a cloud of black ink in a 30-foot emanation. This cloud has no effect outside of water. Creatures inside the cloud are [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) and can't use their sense of smell. The cloud dissipates after 1 minute. The octopus can't use Ink Cloud again for 2d6 rounds.

**Jet** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The octopus moves up to 200 feet in a straight line through the water without triggering reactions.

**Writhing Arms** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The giant octopus makes up to four Strikes with different arms, each against a different target. Each attack counts separately for the octopus's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase the until the octopus has made all the attacks. If the octopus subsequently uses the [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) action, it can Grab any number of creatures it hit with Writhing Arms.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-143" />

When they first hatch from their leathery eggs, larval ofalths resemble a tendril of flesh supported by spindly legs, but they soon cocoon themselves in trash, to serve as both armor and camouflage. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Larval Ofalth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3116) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 249 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9 **Str** +5 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** -2 **Hide in Filth** A larval ofalth can hide in any pile of filth or trash that is its size or larger, allowing it to use [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) for initiative. If it rolls Stealth for initiative, on the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to it. --- **AC** 20 (22 with trash shield raised) **Fort** +11 **Ref** +9 **Will** +9 **HP** 60 **Immunities** disease, poison **[Stench](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=76)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)) 30 feet, DC 19
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)** The larval ofalth's trash shield has a hardness of 5 and 20 Hit Points. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +13, **Damage** 2d8+5 slashing plus wretched weeps **Ranged** leachate +11 ([range 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8 acid plus wretched weeps **Wretched Weeps** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** 2d4 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) every hour and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 day); **Stage 3** 2d6 persistent bleed every hour and enfeebled 2 (1 day)

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ogre Warrior](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3118)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The simplest of ogres are slabs of muscle with hateful eyes, misshapen visages, and malformed bodies. Always eager for mayhem and murder, ogre warriors are quick to turn on their kin when there's a shortage of smaller folk to torment, so those who lead ogres do their best to keep them constantly distracted with new opportunities for raids and ruin.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ogre Glutton](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3119)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ogre gluttons take the act of feeding to a horrific extreme, capable of extending their already cavernous mouths wide enough to gulp down a halfling. Stories of ogre gluttons being tricked into eating razor-edged shields or barrels filled with poisoned meat are common, but such tales are of little consolation to those who have been gobbled whole by these ravenous giants. In addition to their sadistic table manners, ogre gluttons have a disturbing knack for coming up with  violent “games” that are little more than drawn-out torments, yet those who somehow manage to beat a glutton at the rules of their own game can often enrage the ogre enough that the resulting tantrum provides an opportunity for the captives to escape.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ogre Boss](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3120)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

In ogre society, might makes more than right—it makes the rules. The strongest or most violent ogre in a family (in most cases, this is the same ogre) is invariably that family's boss. Quick to hook fallen foes on their weapons, even other ogres fear the repercussions of displeasing an ogre boss. When an ogre boss barks out commands, the other members of the family move quickly to obey.

</column>

markdown:
Mountain oni are the most common oni and often considered the least sophisticated. While other oni might have a stronger connection to the spiritual world, mountain oni have little interest in anything beyond satisfying their relentless appetites. Sloth and gluttony are common vices among mountain oni, which can allow them to coexist with nearby villages so long as they're well fed, but more ambitious mountain oni can often be found leading ogre war parties or orchestrating violent bandit raids. It's rare for mountain oni to live solitary existences, but those who do prefer to maraud the countryside or exploit villages and hoard the spoils for themselves. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Mountain Oni](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3121) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 252 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [tetsubo](/Weapons.aspx?ID=444)_ --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +15 **Will** +14 **HP** 165 **Weaknesses** bean panic, [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) 10 **Bean Panic** Oni are curiously afraid of beans, especially as the seasons begin to change. If a creature Interacts to throw a handful of beans at the oni, the oni becomes frightened 2. While frightened this way, their weakness to spirit damage is increased by 5. The oni then becomes immune to bean panic for 24 hours.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _tetsubo_ +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Razing](/Traits.aspx?ID=784), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 2d10+9 bludgeoning **Melee** jaws +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+9 piercing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 25 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The mountain oni can take on the appearance of any Medium or Large [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) creature. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Thundering Iron** The oni lifts their tetsubo and brings it down in a deafening peal. They make a tetsubo Strike. On a success, the target takes an additional 1d10 sonic damage. Each creature in a 10-foot emanation around the target, other than the oni, take this damage as well and is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 5 feet away from the target.
Tetsubo A mountain oni wields a [tetsubo](/Weapons.aspx?ID=444), which appears in _Lost Omens Tian Xia Character Guide_. This [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=721) martial weapon costs 3 gp, deals 1d10 bludgeoning damage, has 3 Bulk, and require two hands to use. Tetsubo are in the [club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5) weapon group and have the [razing](/Traits.aspx?ID=488), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694), and [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708) traits. The razing trait means that whenever the weapon damages an object, the object takes an amount of additional damage equal to double the number of weapon damage dice.
name: Mountain Oni

Snow oni maintain their seething temperaments even in their icy homes. The isolation of their environment gives them trappings of asceticism, and many snow oni pursue physical perfection, reveling in the process of turning their bodies into powerful fighting machines. They are just as hedonistic and brutal as any of their brethren, however, and eager to indulge in warm baths, strong drink, and humanoid flesh. Like many oni, snow oni possess a third eye in the center of their forehead. This eye gives snow oni a mystical sense that allows them to peer through even the thickest flurries of snow. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Snow Oni](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3122) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 253 **Perception** +26; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), snow vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +8 **Snow Vision** Snow doesn't impair the snow oni's vision; they ignore [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from snowfall. --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +25 **Will** +21 **HP** 290 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Weaknesses** bean panic, spirit 15 **Bean Panic** Oni are curiously afraid of beans, especially as the seasons begin to change. If a creature Interacts to throw a handful of beans at the oni, the oni becomes [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76). While frightened this way, their weakness to spirit damage is increased by 5. The oni then becomes immune to bean panic for 24 hours.
**Icy Deflection** **Trigger** The snow oni is targeted by a ranged Strike or spell attack roll that doesn't have the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) trait; **Effect** The snow oni creates a reflective blockade of ice, gaining a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack roll. If the attack misses, the snow oni redirects the attack to another creature within 20 feet of the snow oni. The attacker rerolls the attack roll against the new target.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** fist +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+16 bludgeoning plus 2d6 cold **Melee** jaws +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+16 piercing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** ice dart +25 ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d10+4 cold plus 1d6 spirit **Primal Innate Spells** DC 31 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The snow oni can take on the appearance of any Medium or Large [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) creature. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal **Chilling Combo** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555)) The snow oni makes two fist Strikes targeting the same creature. If they both hit, the target becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 round. **Falling Frozen Lightning** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The snow oni calls down a bolt of icy lightning, white as fallen snow. The bolt strikes a location within 60 feet, freezing the air into a cloud of snow that fills a 20-foot burst and lasts for 1 minute. All creatures within the snow become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and all creatures outside the snow become concealed to creatures within it. A creature that enters the snow or begins its turn there takes 15 cold damage, with a DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save.
name: Snow Oni

As hot-blooded as the lava that floods their homes, caldera oni have an incredible appetite for the thrill of war. While they engage in battle to conquer and control others, fire oni also love the thrill of combat, fighting for the sheer joy of it. Presenting a caldera oni with a true challenge can potentially earn their mercy and an offer to serve at their side. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Caldera Oni](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3123) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 254 **Perception** +26; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 **Items** _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [katana](/Weapons.aspx?ID=411)_, _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=47)_ --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +25 **Will** +23 **HP** 315 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Weaknesses** bean panic, [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) 15 **Bean Panic** Oni are curiously afraid of beans, especially as the seasons begin to change. If a creature Interacts to throw a handful of beans at the oni, the oni becomes [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76). While frightened this way, their weakness to spirit damage is increased by 5. The oni then becomes immune to bean panic for 24 hours.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**Stoke the Volcano** When the caldera oni is critically hit, the flames of anger grow within them. They recharge their choice of Ash Form or Dance of Burning War. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** _katana_ +30 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=718), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d6+14 slashing plus 2d6 fire **Melee** jaws +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+14 piercing plus 1d8 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 34 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The caldera oni can take on the appearance of any Medium or Large [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) creature. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Ash Form** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** The caldera oni transforms into a cloud of sparking volcanic ash and then Flies. This movement doesn't trigger reactions, and the caldera oni can move through small gaps and spaces occupied by other creatures. The caldera oni then returns to its physical form, affected by a 4th-rank [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514) spell with a duration of 1d4 rounds. **Dance of Burning War** **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** The oni's heat becomes overwhelming, causing them to breathe out superheated ash and dance across the battlefield. The caldera oni Strides, then makes a melee Strike. If the Strike hits, the oni can Stride again and Strike again, repeating this until they have either missed with a Strike or made three Strikes total. The oni then finishes the dance by calling down volcanic lightning through the cloud of ash. Each creature hit by a Strike during the dance takes 3d6 fire damage and 3d6 electricity damage with a DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.
name: Caldera Oni

Island oni are those powerful enough to claim small coastal islands, often creating makeshift armies of lesser oni who pillage surrounding lands and waters. Island oni hoard this material wealth, garbing themselves in luxurious clothing and adorning their monstrous faces with all manner of jewelry. Island oni typically scavenge sunken shipwrecks for weapons, armor, and treasure. This can also bring them into contact with undead sailors, which island oni have been known to press into service. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Island Oni](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3124) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 254 **Perception** +32; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), mist vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +32, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +29 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +2 **Wis** +9 **Cha** +6 **Mist Vision** The island oni ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from fog and mist. **Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=361)_ --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +28 **Will** +34 +1 status on all saves vs. [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) **HP** 390 **Immunities** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) **Weaknesses** bean panic, spirit 20 **Bean Panic** Oni are curiously afraid of beans, especially as the seasons begin to change. If a creature Interacts to throw a handful of beans at the oni, the oni becomes [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76). While frightened this way, their weakness to spirit damage is increased by 5. The oni then becomes immune to bean panic for 24 hours.
**Lost Oni Island** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) An island oni can claim an island of up to 1-mile radius in a process that takes 1 week, during which the oni must defeat any who come to challenge its claim. If successful, the oni can freely control the weather on its island and in a 1-mile radius from the shore, with the effect of a successful [_control weather_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=116) ritual. This altered weather surrounds the island in thick fog, seaborne mirages, or other phenomena that increase the DC of checks to locate and navigate to the island (Such as [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49)) to 40, though the oni can allow allies to pass freely. If the oni dies or leaves the island, the weather returns to normal immediately.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 40 feet, swim 50 feet; [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) **Melee** _longspear_ +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+10 piercing plus 2d6 electricity **Melee** jaws +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+10 piercing plus 2d6 [persistent electricity](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Ranged** thunderbolt +30 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d12+12 electricity plus [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round **Primal Innate Spells** DC 37 2nd invisibility (at will, self only) - **Constant (9th)** [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The island oni can take on the appearance of any Medium or Large [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) creature. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Conductive Downpour** ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The island oni fires a bolt of lightning into the air, which immediately roils with dark clouds. Rain falls in a 60-foot radius emanation, centered on the oni, for 1 minute, filling the air and pooling on the ground. Creatures in the aura gain weakness 10 to electricity, and the entire area is greater [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) for [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) creatures, and difficult terrain for creatures on the ground or [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), unless they also have a swim Speed. **Electrifying Pierce** ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Requirements** The island oni's last action was a successful longspear Strike against a Medium or smaller target; **Effect** The island oni drives the spear through the target and calls lightning to strike the spear. The target takes 6d6 electricity damage with a DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is also impaled on the spear. It's [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), and if the oni moves, they bring the grabbed creature along with them. The island oni doesn't need to use additional actions to keep the creature grabbed; the creature remains grabbed as long as it's impaled. The grabbed creature can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) as normal. The island oni can only have one creature impaled this way at a time. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** medium, 3d8+10 bludgeoning, Rupture 30 **Tripping Tide** ([Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The island oni sweeps their spear in a full circle, releasing waves of seawater. All creatures in a 20-foot emanation must succeed a DC 37 Reflex Saving throw or fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
name: Island Oni

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[String Slime](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3126)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Found underground or in dungeons, these quivering, slug-like ropes of slime continuously scour their domain for food. In addition to their shape, they are named for their ability to shoot out expanding ribbons of slime that resemble tangled strings.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 18

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />

<trait label="Ooze" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=665" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Int** -5

**AC** 10

**Will** +5 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 90

</row>

**Immunities**
[acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), bleed, critical hits, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), precision, slashing, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)

**Split** Whenever a string slime would take slashing damage (if it weren't immune) and has at least 10 HP, it splits into two identical slimes with half the original's HP. One string slime is in the same space as the original, and the other appears in an adjacent unoccupied space. If no adjacent space is unoccupied, move smaller creatures and objects out of the way to make a space or the split is canceled at the GM's discretion.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pseudopod +11,
**Damage** 1d8+4 bludgeoning plus 1d6 acid

**Tag Team** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** another string slime is within 30 feet; **Effect** The slime arcs protoplasm to the other string slime. Creatures in that line take 3d6 acid damage with a DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that fails its save is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

**Weak Acid**   A string slime's acid damages only organic material—not metal, stone, or other inorganic substances.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Tomb Jelly](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3127)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Tomb jellies are animate masses of protoplasm with a sickly combination of yellow, gray, and black hues. Their acidic bodies dissolve flesh but leave other materials, including a victim's gear and bones, intact. Some ancient cultures entombed bodies in stone sarcophagi with tomb jellies to allow the ooze to break down the flesh and clean and polish the bones.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**Speed** 15 feet, climb 10 feet

pseudopod +15,
**Damage** 1d8+6 bludgeoning plus 1d6 acid and tomb curse

**Bound in Death** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The tomb jelly splatters some of its substance on a willing [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature within its reach. The target regains 5 HP and its melee Strikes get the benefits of tomb curse until the end of its next turn.

**Flesh-dissolving Acid**   A tomb jelly's acid damages only flesh.

**Tomb Curse**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) A creature hit by a tomb jelly's pseudopod takes 1d6 [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). If the creature dies while it has this persistent damage, its corpse is affected by [_peaceful rest_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1625), except the tomb jelly can still dissolve its flesh.

</column>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Dromaar mountaineers are hardened half-orc scouts who often lead raiding or scouting parties on dangerous expeditions. Dromaar scouts who lead many successful expeditions can rise to positions of prominence within their holds, and those who command enough loyalty may even claim a hold of their own.

<column gap="tiny">

([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Paleohemoth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3133)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Paleohemoths are typically made from the fossilized bones of [dinosaurs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=391) and other ancient megafauna, though some crafters stretch the definition and instead use magically petrified bones of [dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395) or even [giants](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=420). Not bound to any specific model, paleohemoths are crafted to be as terrifying as possible instead of models of anatomic accuracy. As such, most paleohemoths are nightmarish saurian amalgams whose massive arms end with the complete skulls of apex predators like [tyrannosauruses](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2923), their bite enhanced to petrify flesh.

 Intact fossilized bones are difficult components to scrounge, much less whole skeletons. An alternate way to obtain them is to hire expert hunters or adventurers to seek out live dinosaurs, bring back the bones, and use magic to turn them to stone. Of course, some purists prefer genuine fossilized bones and are willing to pay the price for their acquisition. Either way, opportunities await enterprising souls with the proper tools and several spacious pouches.

 The magic that animates paleohemoths grants them a form of modular flexibility, letting them trade mobility for extended reach through the rapid rearrangement of their limbs. This often involves bones moving from the legs in order to extend the arms. While this does lead to a somewhat silly anatomy, it is nonetheless effective. This ability also lets them appear like a jumbled mass of bones at rest, making them likely to be mistaken for decor rather than guardians.

 The reasons for creating such constructs vary. While they're often associated with guarding evil geniuses in deep, dark dungeons, they have great uses elsewhere. More elegant versions may be used to protect the private collections of upper-class collectors. Thuvia makes great use of paleohemoths in their construct arenas. While they weren't initially permitted to fight, an exploited loophole has made them a popular addition to the arenas ever since.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 35

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30
</column>

</column>

</column>

**Fossilization**  ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631)) The first time each round a creature takes damage from the paleohemoth's jaws, the target must attempt a DC 32 Fortitude save. If it fails and has not already been [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) by this ability, it becomes slowed 1 for 1 minute. If the creature was already slowed by this ability, a failed save causes it to be [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87) permanently.

**Reassemble** <actions string="Single Action" />  The paleohemoth reorganizes its bones, increasing its [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) to 25 feet and reducing its Speed to 15 feet. It can revert to its original form by taking this action again.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons//">Dragon Bones</title>

Many crafters attempt to infuse elemental magic into paleohemoths. The greatest successes have occurred when most of the bones originated from [dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395). Specially created paleohemoths gain the following ability, with a damage type determined by the source of the bones.

 **Energy Blast**<actions string="Two Actions" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The paleohemoth blasts magical energy from one of the skulls that make up its body. Each creature in a 30-foot cone takes 10d6 damage with a DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The paleohemoth can't use Energy Blast again for 1d4 rounds.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pegasus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3134)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The pegasus is a winged horse prized for its capacity to serve as an aerial mount. Unfortunately for those who desire a saddle-trained pegasus, pegasi are wild creatures and do not readily accept even well-intentioned riders. Pegasi actively resist being mounted or controlled by evil creatures, attempting to buck an unwanted rider at every opportunity. A typical pegasus stands 6 feet high at the shoulder, weighs 1,500 pounds, and has a wingspan of 20 feet.

 Pegasi are highly intelligent beasts and have a strong sense of pride and honor. The best way to entreat a pegasus is by speaking to it with grace and offering gifts appropriate to a creature of such majesty. Prospective riders who seek the mount for a worthy cause or virtuous quest have a much easier time coaxing a pegasus into granting its favor. Regardless, a pegasus never accepts a bit or saddle, for reasons both practical (a standard horse saddle interferes with its wings) and purely out of its pride as a free and untamed creature.

 In the wild, pegasi live in small herds and establish territories on remote mountains where they are relatively safe from hunters and foal thieves. They occasionally move to lower ground during foal fledging season. They mature at the same rate as horses and can even breed with other equines, though the outcome of such unions is typically a foal with the traits of its least magical parent. On rare occasions, the interbreeding of a pegasus and a unicorn may result in a winged unicorn with characteristics of both parents and an unrivaled sense of righteousness.

 Some pegasi carry in them the blood of a mighty and heroic ancestor. These champions of pegasus-kind dedicate their long lives to the pursuit of justice. They possess powerful supernatural abilities to aid them in this fight, such as resistance to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), immunity to [petrification](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), and [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) hoof attacks.

</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Phantom Knight](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3135)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Cavaliers and knights who died for their cause make for particularly strong-willed phantoms. Though their motives vary, these phantoms often seek to continue their lifelong missions even in their purgatorial states, and their strong forces of will make them formidable fighters in spite of their incorporeality. A phantom knight might strive to find living followers of their church or order, or seek out other mortals they knew in life as a way of staying grounded and avoiding the corruption of undeath.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Phantom Beast](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3136)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The River of Souls, alongside the souls of dead sapient humanoids, also carries the spirits of monsters, animals, and other creatures. Likewise, it draws souls from across reality, and thus human and elf souls mingle with those of far stranger creatures from untold worlds. Like any others, these far-flung spirits of strange creatures sometimes wash up on the shores of the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34), becoming phantoms far removed from the humanoid forms typically associated with ghosts and other [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722).

 The phantom below is one such entity: a strange beast from a distant world, echoing their behaviors from life as they search for their postmortem fate. Their unfamiliar appearance might lead observers to assume they're a monstrous being and attack, finding the phantom beast entirely willing to fight back. More compassionate adventurers might instead seek to help the phantom find their way back to the River of Souls to complete their spiritual journey.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Ethereal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26

**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26

They can shift back to solely the Ethereal Plane by using this ability again.

**Susceptible to Death** Though phantoms aren't alive, neither are they [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), and they are uniquely vulnerable to the effects of death. A phantom whose Hit Points are reduced to 0 as a result of a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effect (such as from a spell like [_execute_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519)) is immediately whisked away to the River of Souls, where their soul resumes the usual path to the afterlife.

If they succeed, they carry the object along with them telekinetically.

markdown:
The phoenix is a primordial bird made of heat and flame that dwells in the most inhospitable regions of the desert. Though highly intelligent and usually brimming with compassion, phoenixes are best known for their iconic ability to resurrect themselves when slain, emerging reborn from the ashes of their own corpses. Phoenixes are often sought out for their knowledge of healing abilities, as they cannot abide the sight of suffering and deny their succor only to the most foul and irredeemable of creatures. Phoenixes enjoy the company of peaceful dragons, and the two can forge lifelong friendships, keeping each other updated on regional news. While most phoenixes are benevolent, they are not infallible. When a phoenix loses their way, they still retain their strong appetite for knowledge. Malevolent phoenixes are known to assault universities and libraries in their pursuit for power—not only to gain new information, but also to set fire to the texts and thus hoard that knowledge for themselves. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 39 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 [Phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3137) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 264 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +25 **Str** +6 **Dex** +7 **Con** +5 **Int** +7 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +31 **Will** +28 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 300 , regeneration 20 (deactivated by [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Weaknesses** cold 10, unholy 10 **Shroud of Flame** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 20 feet. 4d6 fire, DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. While this aura is active, any adjacent creature that hits the phoenix with a melee attack or otherwise touches them takes 2d6 fire damage. The phoenix can activate or deactivate the aura with a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait.
**Self-Resurrection** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) When a phoenix dies, they collapse into a pile of smoldering ashes before returning to life fully healed 1d4 rounds later, as if subject to a 7th-rank [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=123) ritual. Self-resurrection happens only if there are some remains to resurrect; for instance, a phoenix killed by a [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492) spell can't use this ability. A phoenix whose remains rest within an area devoted to an unholy deity by [_consecrate_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=115) can't self-resurrect until their remains are no longer in that area. A phoenix can self-resurrect only once per year. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 70 feet **Melee** beak +30 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d12+9 piercing plus 3d8 fire and 2d10 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Melee** talon +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d6+6 piercing plus 3d8 fire and 2d10 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** flame jet +30 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 6d6 fire plus 2d10 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Primal Inante Spells** DC 39 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) - **6th** [Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) (x3) - **7th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (at will) - **8th** [Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (x3), [Divine Immolation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1496), [Everlight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1518) (at will), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (x3), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748) (x3) - **Constant (6th)** [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) - **Constant (8th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) **Flaming Strafe** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The phoenix blazes with superheated flame and [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to their Speed. They deal 6d6 fire damage to each creature within 20 feet of each square they move through (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).
Servants of Sarenrae While phoenixes are not denizens of the Outer Planes, they have long been associated with the goddess [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292). Indeed, many phoenixes view the Dawnflower as their patron and subscribe to her mission of redeeming those who have fallen to evil.
name: Phoenix

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pipefox](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3138)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Pipefoxes are small, fluffy snakes with the head of a fox. They spend their days lurking in dark corners, hanging from trees, coiled around pipes, or otherwise lounging anywhere they can observe something interesting in peace and quiet. Pipefoxes are neither pests nor pets; they simply skulk around, acquiring knowledge and searching for a worthy scholar to disseminate their observations to.

 Pipefoxes are as intense as they are capricious with their fixations. One might study blacksmithing for six months before suddenly switching their entire attention to studying the slang used by a group of local thieves, or the gossip of servants behind their employer's back. Regardless of the topic, they always study from a distance lest the object of their observation alter their behaviors. If a pipefox is discovered, they usually attempt to flee, only fighting as a last resort.

 Pipefoxes view their knowledge as a form of currency that they protect at all costs. Because of this, they are secretive by nature. If they choose to reveal themselves to someone, it will be after much study and consideration. A pipefox will only approach someone they believe to be as invested in the pursuit of knowledge as they are. They are most attracted to spellcasters, alchemists, and inventors for this reason. However, while a pipefox may choose to approach and share what they know, they never do so freely. Like all currencies, knowledge must be equally exchanged.

 Many scholars believe pipefoxes to be spirits and keepers of knowledge; in the event of a massive cataclysm, pipefoxes will be able to return lost knowledge to the world, preventing great dark ages. For this reason, many institutions of knowledge are aware of the spying of pipefoxes but pretend not to notice and prevent others from discovering them. This encourages pipefoxes to stay while also providing a backup cache of important knowledge in the form of a small, fluffy snake.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +9, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +9, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +12 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 piercing

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 18, attack +8
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only Cantrips)
- **Constant (2nd)**
[Translate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723)

**Master of Tongues**   Even if the pipefox does not speak a creature's language, it can rapidly pick up on inflection, root words, and body language. If the pipefox interacts or observes a creature for at least 10 minutes and that creature can speak a language, it can communicate basic concepts to that creature.

**Rapid Erudition** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Requirements** The pipefox saw a [cantrip](/Traits.aspx?ID=550) cast within the last minute; **Effect** The pipefox can cast the cantrip it saw as an innate arcane spell for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Hoarders of Knowledge</title>

Nothing is as valuable to a pipefox as knowledge. They often fill their space with the books and tools they've secretly collected over the years. While these books are often on seemingly mundane topics, it is not uncommon to find a magic scroll or two hidden away in a pipefox's home.
</aside>

These effects last until the duskwalker uses Spirit Hunter again.

**Spirit Shot** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The duskwalker has designated a creature as their prey using Spirit Hunter; **Effect** The duskwalker makes two ranged Strikes against their prey. If both Strikes hit, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

<row gap="medium">

Mortals whose ancestry has been influenced by [archons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=365) are called lawbringers. They may have mortal ancestors who fought alongside archons against the forces of evil and entropy, or they may have been born from a union between an archon and a mortal. Many lawbringers seek adventure to bringing order to the world.

markdown:
When a creature dies, and for whatever reason its spirit is unable or unwilling to leave the site of its death, that spirit may manifest as a poltergeist: a restless, invisible spirit that is still able to manipulate physical objects. Many poltergeists perished in a way that resulted from or led to extreme emotional trauma. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 20 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Poltergeist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3142) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 268 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14 **Str** -5 **Dex** +5 **Con** +0 **Int** -1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Site Bound** A poltergeist is tied to a location and can't travel more than 120 feet from the place where it was created or formed. Some poltergeists are instead bound to a specific room, building, or similar area. --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +14 **Will** +13 **HP** 55 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83), rejuvenation) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** all damage 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Natural Invisibility** A poltergeist is naturally [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83). It becomes visible only when it uses Frighten.
**Rejuvenation** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) When a poltergeist is destroyed, it reforms, fully healed, where it was destroyed after 2d4 days. A poltergeist can be permanently destroyed only if someone determines the reason for its existence and sets right whatever prevents the spirit from resting.
**Telekinetic Defense** **Trigger** A creature approaches within 10 feet of the poltergeist; **Effect** The poltergeist makes a telekinetic object Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** fly 20 feet **Ranged** telekinetic object +13 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d12 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing (depending on object) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 23, attack +13 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **3rd** [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717) (at will) **Frighten** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Requirements** The poltergeist must be [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83); **Effect** The poltergeist becomes visible, appearing as a skeletal, ghostlike humanoid. Each creature within 30 feet must attempt a DC 21 Will save, becoming [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) on a failure. On a critical failure, it's also [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for as long as it's frightened. On a success, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 minute. At the start of its next turn, the poltergeist becomes invisible again. **Telekinetic Storm** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The poltergeist telekinetically throws numerous small objects, such as dozens of pieces of silverware or books, either spreading them out among multiple foes or directing them at one target.
  • When this effect is spread out among multiple foes, the poltergeist makes a telekinetic object Strike at a –2 penalty against each creature within 30 feet. These count as one attack for the poltergeist's multiple attack penalty, and the penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks.
  • When this effect has only one target, the poltergeist makes a telekinetic object Strike against the target, and the damage increases to 3d12. It deals 1d12 damage on a failure, and no damage on a critical failure.
Disturbed Rest One of the most common ways for a poltergeist to form is when its burial site is desecrated by the construction of a dwelling. This is usually an accident, but some creatures intentionally create poltergeists to serve as guardians.
Poltergeist Treasure A poltergeist needs items to hurl as weapons, and over the centuries of use, only durable objects survive its rampages. Silver dinnerware, hatchets, and books might all be found in a poltergeist's collection.
name: Poltergeist
