<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Poracha](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3143)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Porachas are feline beasts native to the mystical Forest of Spirits in Tian Xia. In their natural form, these graceful eight-legged creatures sport a gray tabby coat of fur streaked with lines of olive green, but they are rarely seen this way. Even the youngest porachas are capable of hiding within objects, making them incredibly elusive and rarely seen except on their own terms. Because porachas can so effortlessly blend in with the forest and emerge from its dark corners in an instant, superstitious locals believe these beasts are related to [kami](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=243)—nature spirits who embody individual trees, stones, or other elements of nature in the Forest of Spirits. The truth is, while porachas frequently associate with kami, they are their own breed of monster.

 The poracha's most iconic feature is its ability to bend reality to teleport, or jaunt, short distances. But a poracha can also rest within objects, allowing it to safely sleep inside. Given that a poracha experiences time very slowly while hiding within objects, it can sleep inside one for an extended period. Once it wakes, a poracha departs to play in the living world. Porachas especially enjoy resting inside roadside milestones, to maximize their chances of coming across travelers.

 Porachas rarely tread humanoid-occupied lands, preferring the company of the kami and their natural habitat in the Forest of Spirits. Though they enjoy occasional conversation, they're extremely sensitive to sound; they speak softly and request the same from those they speak with. Their territories span vast swaths of the wood, making them ideal allies for hunters or treasure seekers who would otherwise become lost in the dense trees. But porachas are nothing if not fickle, and typically demand inscrutable favors in return for their aid, such as the planting of a rare sapling or the clearing of an invasive monster species. Any attempt to coerce or trick a poracha into service is ill advised, for these sacred beasts have many friends among the kami as well as others of their kind. Though they generally don't travel in groups, porachas know where to find more of their kind in times of need, and an organized pack of porachas is a disorienting threat.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</row>

**Damage** 2d6+7 piercing

**Jaunt** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The poracha teleports up to 40 feet to a location it can see. It can't use Jaunt again for 1d4 rounds.

**Object Meld** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The poracha touches an adjacent object of enough volume to contain it. It merges into the object indefinitely, and while it's inside, time moves exceptionally slowly for it and it has only limited perception of the outside world. The poracha can sense what's going on outside the object only using [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82). The poracha can use only mental actions or Jaunt; if it Jaunts, it leaves the object and appears where it chooses. If a creature Strikes the object, the poracha is expelled unharmed.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">What is a Kami?</title>

The spirit creatures known collectively as [kami](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=243) were originally protectors of the natural world, especially those things unable to protect themselves. Over time, though, their remit has grown, and now kami protectors extend their services to include artificial structures and items. In theory, any animal, plant, object, or location could be served by its own kami, but not everything has yet been blessed with such protection. The classification of which objects warrant kami protection—and which don't—is confusing and complicated to all save kami themselves, who find the organizational system perfectly logical.
</aside>

markdown:
Other proteans don't consider the flying, iridescent beings known as voidworms to be part of a protean caste at all, but instead merely a shameful side effect of the [Maelstrom's](/Planes.aspx?ID=43)constantly churning energy. To call a voidworm a protean in the presence of a more powerful protean is a sure a way to instigate combat. Voidworms themselves have little interest in whether anyone sees them as proteans. They maintain a thriving ecology in the Maelstrom, frolicking in schools of up to 20 and playing in the chaos of constantly shifting realities. Elsewhere (such as in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26)), voidworms are mesmerized by the principle of object permanence; many latch onto specific features of a region (such as a hillside or pond) and flit through the air around it for months or even years as they wait for the object of their curiosity to change. Minor changes—such as a tree's change of color in the fall, a corpse's slow decay, or periodic venting of steam from a geyser—all fascinate voidworms. A voidworm is about 2 feet long and weighs 2 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Voidworm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3144) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 270 **Perception** +4; entropy sense ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +6, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7 **Str** -1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** -1 **Wis** -1 **Cha** +1 **Entropy Sense** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674)) A voidworm can anticipate the most likely presence of a creature through a supernatural insight into chaotic probabilities and chance. This grants them the ability to sense creatures within the listed range. [_Veil of privacy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739) prevents a creature from being detected via entropy sense automatically (without a counteract check). --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +5 **Ref** +9 **Will** +6 **HP** 16 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 1) **Resistances** precision 3, protean anatomy 5 **Protean Anatomy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A voidworm's vital organs shift and change shape and position constantly. Immediately after the voidworm takes acid, electricity, or sonic damage, they gain the listed amount of resistance to that damage type. This lasts for 1 hour or until the next time the protean takes damage of one of the other types (in which case their resistance changes to match that type), whichever comes first.
The voidworm is immune to [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effects unless they're a willing target. If [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) or [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), the voidworm automatically recovers at the end of their next turn as new sensory organs grow to replace the compromised ones. --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 40 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** jaws +9 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d8+1 piercing **Melee** tail +9 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d4+1 slashing plus confounding lash **Divine Innate Spells** DC 16 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455) (self only), [Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606) - **4th** [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647) - **Constant (4th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The voidworm takes on the appearance of a Tiny [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531). This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal. **Confounding Lash** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) A creature hit by the voidworm's tail Strike is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 round (stupefied 2 on a critical hit). A successful DC 16 Will save negates this effect and grants temporary immunity to confounding lash for 1 minute.

markdown:
Azuretzis are sinuous, serpentine creatures with dagger-sharp teeth covered in brilliant blue scales and mottled purple and pink highlights that shimmer in a pareidolic approximation of leering, laughing faces. The Maelstrom's chaotic forces spawn these small proteans from a variety of sources: physical mating between older azuretzis, the paradoxical promotion of bestial naunets, and possibly from mortal petitioners, though these azuretzis may just be confusing putative mortal memories with experiences gained from games of mimicry. Never expect azuretzis to operate by any rational, self-consistent rules. Azuretzis represent the humor of chaos, particularly in the form of mockery and parody via exaggerated mimicry, twisting a target's features into a laughingstock. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Azuretzi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3145) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 271 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), entropy sense (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +13 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Entropy Sense** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674)) A protean can anticipate the most likely presence of a creature through a supernatural insight into chaotic probabilities and chance. This grants them the ability to sense creatures within the listed range. [_Veil of privacy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739) prevents a creature from being detected via entropy sense automatically (without a counteract check). --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +15 **Will** +11 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 65 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 2) **Resistances** precision 5, protean anatomy 8 **Protean Anatomy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A protean's vital organs shift and change shape and position constantly. Immediately after the protean takes acid, electricity, or sonic damage, they gain the listed amount of resistance to that damage type. This lasts for 1 hour or until the next time the protean takes damage of one of the other types (in which case their resistance changes to match that type), whichever comes first.
The protean is immune to [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effects unless they're a willing target. If [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) or [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), the protean automatically recovers at the end of their next turn as new sensory organs grow to replace the compromised ones.
**Spell Pilfer** **Trigger** A creature with an active spell effect within 30 feet of the azuretzi fails to resist another azuretzi's Mocking Touch; **Effect** The azuretzi attempts a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) one spell affecting the target creature. On a success, the azuretzi transfers the spell effect to themself, keeping the same remaining duration. The target then becomes temporarily immune to Spell Pilfer for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet, swim 25 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** jaws +15 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d10+5 piercing **Melee** claw +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+5 slashing **Melee** tail +13 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+5 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 22 - **2nd** [Laughing Fit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583) - **3rd** [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **Constant (4th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 1d12+5 bludgeoning, DC 21 **Mimic Form** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) As [Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55), but an azuretzi can assume the form of a Medium or smaller creature. They can mimic a specific creature they can see, but they must succeed at a DC 25 Perception check or the attempt is disrupted. The azuretzi can transform into the same creature again without a check but can retain the details of only one specific appearance at a time. The azuretzi can Dismiss the effect as a free action to return to their natural form. **Mocking Touch** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Requirements** The azuretzi is not currently using Mocking Touch on a spell; **Effect** The azuretzi mocks a creature's magical ability with a touch. The azuretzi attempts a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check against the target's Will DC.
**Critical Success** The azuretzi learns all spells of 3rd rank or lower the target has available to cast and chooses one. The azuretzi gains that spell as a mock divine innate spell and can cast it once as an innate divine spell using their own DC and spell attack modifier. The spell is lost if unused after 24 hours. The creature can't cast the mock spell until the azuretzi casts it first or the 24 hour period passes, whichever comes first.
**Success** As critical success, but the mock spell is lost after 1 hour, and the creature touched can cast the spell normally.
**Failure** As critical success, but the mock spell is lost at the end of the azuretzi's next turn, and the creature touched can cast the spell normally.
**Critical Failure** Mocking Touch has no effect.
name: Azuretzi

markdown:
The ruling caste of the proteans, keketars orchestrate attacks against the bastions of law and adjudicate protean disputes confidently and capriciously. A keketar resembles a shimmering, serpentine creature with spines, claws, and a dragon-like head. A keketar's actual appearance is in constant flux, but they generally stay about 18 feet long with a weight of around 1,500 pounds. While their physical forms can vary, two things remain constant: first, a keketar's eyes are always a piercing shade of amber or violet. Second, the keketar's mark of office—a crown of shifting symbols that hovers above their head—never changes. A keketar cannot remove their crown but can suppress it, although most are loath to do so and consider such an act one of cowardice or shame. Keketars fill a role in protean society of a sort of priesthood, operating as intermediaries between the other proteans and the Speakers of the Depths. All other proteans defer to keketars, treating them in a way similar to how citizens of a mortal city would treat respected nobles; even more powerful proteans defer to the will of the keketars. As with many religions, dogma and theology are prone to interpretation and change, and among the proteans, the situation is even more pronounced. Whatever the nature and desires of the Speakers of the Depths may be, individual keketars often come to dramatically different conclusions as to their will and intent. To the proteans, however, this inherent dissonance is a strength rather than a weakness. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Keketar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3146) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 272 **Perception** +30; entropy sense ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet, [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +32, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +34, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +30 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** +5 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +7 **Entropy Sense** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674)) A protean can anticipate the most likely presence of a creature through a supernatural insight into chaotic probabilities and chance. This grants them the ability to sense creatures within the listed range. [_Veil of privacy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739) prevents a creature from being detected via entropy sense automatically (without a counteract check). --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +28 **Will** +34 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 260 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 10) **Resistances** precision 10, protean anatomy 25 **Protean Anatomy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A protean's vital organs shift and change shape and position constantly. Immediately after the protean takes acid, electricity, or sonic damage, they gain the listed amount of resistance to that damage type. This lasts for 1 hour or until the next time the protean takes damage of one of the other types (in which case their resistance changes to match that type), whichever comes first.
The protean is immune to [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effects unless they're a willing target. If [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) or [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), the protean automatically recovers at the end of their next turn as new sensory organs grow to replace the compromised ones.
**Spatial Riptide** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 30 feet. A creature using a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) ability within the aura or arriving in it via teleportation must succeed at a DC 38 Fortitude save or wink out of existence for 1d4 rounds before completing the teleport. The creature can't act, sense anything, or be targeted. On a successful save, the creature completes the teleport normally but is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93). Keketars are immune to this effect.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 50 feet, swim 40 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** jaws +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+16 piercing plus warpwave strike **Melee** claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+16 slashing plus warpwave strike **Melee** tail +33 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+16 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 42, attack +32 - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471) (at will), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) - **5th** [Creation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477) (at will), [Mirage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604) (×2; see reshape reality), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **6th** [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720) (at will; self only) - **7th** [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (at will), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670) (at will), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754) (×3) - **8th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Cursed Metamorphosis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479) - **9th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499), [Unfathomable Song](/Spells.aspx?ID=1731) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The keketar can take the appearance of any Huge or smaller creature. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 1d10+15 bludgeoning, DC 42 **Reshape Reality** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) When the keketar casts [_mirage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604), they infuse the illusion with quasi-real substance. Creatures that do not disbelieve the illusion treat structures and terrain created through the spell as though they were real, ascending illusory stairs, becoming trapped by illusory quicksand, and so on. **Warpwave Strike** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) A creature struck by a keketar's jaws or claw Strike must succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save or be subject to a warpwave.
name: Keketar

A nosoi resembles a whippoorwill, sparrow, or other small bird wearing a heavy leather plague doctor's mask. They are the clerks, messengers, and scribes of the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39), witnessing judgments, directing souls, and generally performing the administrative grunt work that keeps the Boneyard functioning. Most nosois are particularly chatty and eager to discuss how important they consider their individual assignments to be. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Nosoi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3147) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 274 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +8, [Library Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +8, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +6, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +6, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +2, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6 **Str** -1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +4 **Ref** +8 **Will** +6 **HP** 18 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 3, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 3 --- **Speed** 15 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** beak +6 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d4–1 piercing plus 1d6 shepherd's touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 16 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only), [Noise Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616) - **4th** [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The nosoi takes the appearance of a raven or songbird. This doesn't change its Speed or its attack and damage modifiers with its Strikes. **Haunting Melody** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The nosoi croons an entrancing song. Each living or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature within a 60-foot emanation must attempt a DC 18 Will save. The effect lasts until the end of the nosoi's next turn, but the nosoi can Sustain it. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 24 hours. Despite being a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect, this ability affects [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) undead. Psychopomps are immune to this ability.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with the nosoi.
**Critical Failure** The creature is fascinated with the nosoi and must spend each of its actions on its turn to move closer to the nosoi as expediently as possible while avoiding obvious dangers. If a fascinated creature is adjacent to the nosoi, it stays still and doesn't act. If the creature is attacked, the fascination ends. **Shepherd's Touch** A nosoi's Strikes have the benefit of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune and deal an additional 1d6 void damage to living creatures or 1d6 vitality damage to undead.
name: Nosoi

markdown:
Protectors of the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39), the stern and resolute guardians of the dead, vanths are psychopomps who resemble skeletons with raven-like wings and a mask resembling a vulture's skull. Vanths carry black scythes to fight against those who would disturb the natural progression of souls, and they consider any visitor to the Boneyard a potential troublemaker. They rarely speak and even more rarely show any emotion other than a grim adherence to duty. When the psychopomp armies go to war, vanths serve as front-line soldiers. In particular, [daemons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=386) continually stage raids on the River of Souls, requiring constant patrol. Implacable warriors, vanths fly in perfect formation. This can backfire, as they often suppress any adaptability they possess as they wage war. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Vanth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3148) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 275 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +13, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Items** _+1 [scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=394)_ --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 105 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 10, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 20 feet, DC 22
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** _scythe_ +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 1d10+8 slashing plus 2d6 shepherd's touch **Melee** jaws +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d6+8 slashing plus 2d6 shepherd's touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 22 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only) - **3rd** [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) (×3), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588) (×3) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **5th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) **Infuse Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A vanth's scythe is its symbol of office and gains a measure of its personal power. This scythe becomes a _+1 scythe_ and is treated as if it were [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) while the vanth wields it. A vanth whose scythe is taken or destroyed can infuse a new one with an hour of work. **Shepherd's Touch** A vanth's Strikes have the benefit of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune and deal an additional 2d6 void damage to living creatures or 2d6 vitality damage to undead. **Vanth's Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The vanth bestows a curse on a creature by touching it with its scythe. The creature must attempt a DC 25 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Vanth's Curse for 24 hours.
**Success** The target feels a momentary shudder of doom and is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 minute by the distracting sensation.
**Failure** The target becomes morose and glum as it accepts its own inevitable fate. For 1 hour, the target is stupefied 2. Each time the target gains the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition, the stupefied condition value increases by 1, to a maximum value of stupefied 4.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect is permanent.
Vanth Scythes Vanths' favored weapon is the [scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=394), a choice that further adds to their fearsome appearance and can lead to unfortunate associations with the Grim Reaper or [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=295) among more superstitious mortals. Vanths find such comparisons to be ignorant at best or insulting at worst. Some vanths infuse their scythes with different qualities, such as [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920), depending on the nature of their most common enemies.
name: Vanth

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Morrigna](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3149)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Bounty hunters and investigators, morrignas seek out creatures who thwart death or interfere with the natural flow of souls. Morrignas dress in flowing spider silk and wear masks reminiscent of webs, as they consider patient and watchful spiders to be their spiritual kin.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 34

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Morrigna.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 15">[Morrigna](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3149)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Monitor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=655" />
<trait label="Psychopomp" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=679" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 276

**Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680), [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +28, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +29, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +29, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +4

**Con** +4

**Int** +3

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Items**
_+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 38 

**Fort** +25 

**Ref** +27 

**Will** +29 

+1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 240

, regeneration 20 (deactivated by [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)

**Resistances**
[poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 15, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 15

**Wrappings Lash** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature within reach of the morrigna's web wrappings uses an action to Strike or attempt a skill check; **Effect** The morrigna makes a web wrappings Strike against the triggering creature. If the strike is a critical hit, the triggering action is disrupted.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_bo staff_ +31 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 2d8+14 bludgeoning plus 4d6 shepherd's touch

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
web wrappings +29 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d12+14 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) and 4d6 shepherd's touch

**Divine Spontaneous Spells** DC 35, attack +30
- **Cantrips (8th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689), [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)
- **1st**
[Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447), [Bless](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513) (4 slots)
- **2nd**
[Calm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=1674) (4 slots)
- **3rd**
[Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1453), [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478), [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503) (4 slots)
- **4th**
[Dispelling Globe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) (4 slots)
- **5th**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Scouting Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665) (4 slots)
- **6th**
[Field of Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=1526), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685) (4 slots)

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 37
- **4th**
[Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728), [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680)

**Rituals** DC 37

- **5th**
[Call Spirit](/Rituals.aspx?ID=5)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) A morrigna can take the appearance of any Small or Medium animal or humanoid. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage modifiers with their Strikes, but it might change the damage type their Strikes deal. Unless they choose to manifest their web wrappings in their new form, they cannot make web wrappings Strikes.

**Shepherd's Touch**   A morrigna's Strikes have the benefit of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune and deal an additional 4d6 void damage to living creatures or 4d6 vitality damage to undead.

**Spider Minions** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Summon](/Traits.aspx?ID=520)) The morrigna summons a [giant tarantula](/Monsters.aspx?ID=387) (page 321) or [spider swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=385). These spiders have the [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=706) trait and remain for 10 minutes or until reduced to 0 Hit Points, whichever comes first. The morrigna does not need to Sustain the Spell to direct these summoned creatures, and the morrigna can have any number of summoned spiders in existence at once. The morrigna can see through the eyes of any of their summoned spiders at any time.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-458" />
name: Morrigna

The greatest judges among the psychopomps are yamarajes, whose wisdom is legendary and whose edicts are unappealable except to ushers or [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) herself. A yamaraj resembles an immense dragon with dark, feathery scales and an emotionless, dispassionate gaze behind a feathered mask. When not serving as the senior magistrates, lords, and generals of the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39), yamarajes pursue highly individualistic hobbies, such as gardening or literature. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Yamaraj](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3150) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 277 **Perception** +37; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 240 feet, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 120 feet, [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +40, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +34, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +36, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +40, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +38, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +38, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +38 **Str** +10 **Dex** +7 **Con** +7 **Int** +10 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +33 **Ref** +31 **Will** +35 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 375 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 20, lightning drinker) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) (see lightning drinker) **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 20, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 20 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet, DC 39
**Lightning Drinker** Whenever a yamaraj would take electricity damage if not for its immunity, its fast healing increases to 40 on its next turn. During that turn, if it uses Beetle Breath, the beetles deal 2d12 additional electricity damage. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 50 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** jaws +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+18 piercing plus Improved [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) and yamaraj venom and 3d6 shepherd's touch **Melee** claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d4+18 slashing plus 3d6 shepherd's touch **Melee** tail +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+18 bludgeoning plus 3d6 shepherd's touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 44 - **5th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601) (at will) - **8th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462) (×3), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (×3), [Wall of Force](/Spells.aspx?ID=1749) - **9th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Seize Soul](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685), [Wails of the Damned](/Spells.aspx?ID=1747) - **10th** [Manifestation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592) (see final judgment), [Revival](/Spells.aspx?ID=1654) - **Constant (10th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Rituals** DC 44 - **5th** [Call Spirit](/Rituals.aspx?ID=5), [Resurrect](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) **Beetle Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The yamaraj breathes a blast of beetles in a 50-foot cone that deals 14d8 slashing damage and 4d8 [persistent slashing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to creatures in the area with a DC 42 Reflex save. It can't use Beetle Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91).
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is sickened 3. **Final Judgment** A yamaraj's [_manifestation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592) spells are used only to pronounce judgment, typically either to restore a dead or destroyed creature to life, bind a creature to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39), or banish a creature from the Boneyard. **Shepherd's Touch** A yamaraj's Strikes have the benefit of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune and deal an additional 3d6 void damage to living creatures or 3d6 vitality damage to undead. **Yamaraj Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) While a creature is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) from this poison, it is [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) with the same value; **Saving Throw** DC 42 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d8 poison damage and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) (1 round); **Stage 2** 5d8 poison damage and clumsy 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 7d8 poison damage and clumsy 3 (1 round).
name: Yamaraj

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pteranodon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3151)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Pteranodons are quick and agile reptiles with 20-foot wingspans that enable them to hover on wind currents for hours. These creatures have long beaks and equally long crests that protrude from the backs of their heads.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Quetzalcoatlus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3152)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Quetzalcoatlus are the largest flying members of the pterosaur family and are often mistaken for dragons due to their immense size and 40-foot wingspans. Using the joints of their massive wings as forelimbs, they are also quite capable of moving on land, snatching smaller prey from the ground or out of streams.

 Quetzalcoatlus are carnivorous, feeding on a variety of reptiles, mammals, large fish, amphibians, and other vertebrates. They are not inherently aggressive creatures and are happy to scavenge for food, but when presented with live prey they readily attack almost any creature smaller than themselves.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pukwudgie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3153)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Pukwudgies go by many names in many regions, but those who know of them agree that defying their mischievous nature provokes their wrath. In ancient times, pukwudgies traveled to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) from the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35), perhaps in the wake of the [gnome](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4) emigration. These proud fey are obsessed with displays of respect, and while they first attempted to befriend mortals, each attempt ended in tragedy as the pukwudgies perceived any potential slight as a grave insult. Mortals, fearing these reactions, began to view pukwudgies as dangerous nuisances. Pukwudgies, in turn, began to resent mortals and the gods that favored them.

 At their best, pukwudgies play cruel jokes on mortals they encounter. At their worst, they've been known to kidnap and even kill those who don't treat them with proper respect. Violent fey like [twigjacks](/Monsters.aspx?ID=838) and [redcaps](/Monsters.aspx?ID=349) often gather under pukwudgie leadership, much to the pukwudgies' glee.

 Pukwudgies make their villages in the oldest forests, concealed under [_mirage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604) spells. They travel freely between the Universe and the First World through tiny portals beneath hills, trees, or stones. No strangers to violence, pukwudgies rarely travel alone and often anoint their quills or weapons with a custom-brewed poison before entering hostile situations.

 Standing about 2 feet tall, a pukwudgie sports sharp quills growing from their head that extends down their back. Their skin tone varies by the region in which they live, ranging from pale gray to brown.

**Ranged**

**Damage** 1d6+10 slashing plus pukwudgie poison

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortbow +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing plus pukwudgie poison

- **2nd**

- **3rd**

In porcupine form, their size changes to Medium, they lose their weapon Strikes, and they gain a quill Strike (+18 for 2d8+6 piercing plus 1d8 [persistent poison](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)).

**Pukwudgie Poison**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage and stupefied 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 1d6 poison damage, [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), and stupefied 2 (1 round).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Trepidatious Trade</title>

Pukwudgies rely heavily on their knowledge of local plants, which they use for food, medicine, magical weapons, and their signature poison. If shown proper respect, pukwudgies trade their crafts for items they deem valuable. They have little use for coin but accept unique foods, items of beauty, and even interesting stories as payment. The slightest transgression, however, can quickly turn pukwudgie encounters hostile.
</aside>

The brutish and gluttonous augnagar live to feast—preferably on rotten flesh and, when possible, demon flesh. But to them, the greatest delicacy is the flesh of other augnagars. Augnagars have swollen, spiderlike legs with leathery membranes like a bat's wings, and three tails ending in hooked stings perfect for slicing flesh. An augnagar that gorges sufficiently, especially on other augnagars, can grow so massive it can't even move, thrashing and festering where it lies. It eventually flies into a frenzy of self-cannibalism as it rips apart its own flesh to feast on. From the ruinous remains emerges a [thulgant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=777)—a smaller but more powerful qlippoth. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Augnagar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3156) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 281 **Perception** +27; **Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature can't attempt a flat check to recover from confusion whenever it takes damage from an attack or spell. **Inhale Vitality** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The augnagar inhales sharply, drawing life force out of creatures in a 50-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 14d6 void damage with a DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is also [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73). If any creatures take damage from this activity, the augnagar becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 round, and it can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike. **Rotting Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Saving Throw** DC 32 Fortitude; **Stage 1** [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) (1 day); **Stage 2** drained 2 and the creature displays hideous, festering wounds exuding a horrific stench. When the victim takes piercing or slashing damage, creatures within 30 feet must succeed at a DC 32 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91). The victim automatically fails this save (1 day).
name: Augnagar

markdown:
Thulgants are powerful and intelligent qlippoths created from the cannibalistic feeding frenzies of augnagars. Although they spend most of their time hunting and battling [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) for control of the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45), thulgants amuse themselves with a variety of diversions, such as maintaining galleries of petrified mortals or building massive, hive-like lairs filled with enslaved minions. Each thulgant is a horrific tangle of limbs, with spiderlike legs, writhing tentacles emerging from the top of its head, and three scorpion-like stingers. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Thulgant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3157) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 283 **Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +32, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +33, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +32 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +9 --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +28 **Will** +32 **HP** 305 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 10) **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 15, physical 15 (except [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375)) --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet, fly 50 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** stinger +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d12+17 piercing plus 4d6 mental and thulgant venom **Melee** tentacle +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d8+17 bludgeoning plus 3d6 acid and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 40 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **4th** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) - **8th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Divine Decree](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632) (×3), [Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644) - **9th** [Petrify](/Spells.aspx?ID=1628) (×3), [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630) - **Constant (6th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Rituals** DC 40 - **8th** [Imprisonment](/Rituals.aspx?ID=13) **Demon Hunter** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The thulgant causes a [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573) within 30 feet to suffer the effect of its sinful vulnerability. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65)** 2d6+17 bludgeoning and 1d6 acid, DC 40 **Mind-Rending Sting** **Requirement** The thulgant hits the same enemy with two consecutive sting Strikes in the same round; **Effect** The thulgant deals 3d12+17 mental damage to the enemy. If the enemy is affected by thulgant venom, that poison gains the [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=726) trait. **Stunning Display** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The thulgant rises up on its twitching limbs and presents its numerous tentacles and stingers in a horrifying display of awfulness. Creatures in a 30-foot emanation must attempt a DC 40 Will save, after which they are temporarily immune to further Stunning Displays for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93).
**Failure** The creature is stunned 4.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 8. **Thulgant Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 40; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage and the victim gains one of the following at random: [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) (1 round); **Stage 2** 6d6 poison damage and the victim gains two of the following at random: clumsy 2, enfeebled 2, or stupefied 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 9d6 poison damage and the victim gains all three of the following: clumsy 3, enfeebled 3, and stupefied 3 (1 round).
name: Thulgant

markdown:
This big-headed beast is covered in glittering scales; its eyes are like large copper pots, and its mouth is full of sharp teeth. Despite its fish-like appearance, a quai dau to spends most of its time on land hunting along riversides or lakes. It uses its trunk to drain the nearby water before using it with deadly skill. A quai dau to can expel the water with a force strong enough to knock a knight off a horse, or gently enough to surround themself in a veil of mist. A quai dau to is extremely sensitive to sound. If they hear too much ruckus near their hunting grounds, they violently attack. Their silent movement allows them to sneak up on such distracted prey despite their massive size. Researchers have discovered, after much study, that quai dau tos don't have ears in the traditional sense. They have an organ in the upper part of their skulls that inflates to hold the water they use in their attacks. This organ is extremely sensitive to vibrations in the air and is used as a pseudo ear. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Quai Dau To](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3158) **Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 284 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), mist vision, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 120 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +8 **Int** -3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** -1 **Mist Vision** The quai dau to ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from mist and fog. --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +19 **Will** +21 **HP** 300 **Immunities** [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59), [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) **Weaknesses** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 15 **Frightful Sight** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 60 feet. This aura functions as a DC 32 [frightful presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64) aura, but a creature doesn't attempt its save until it sees the quai dau to.
**Reflective Scales** **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet casts a spell with the [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640) trait or uses an ability with the light trait; **Effect** The quai dau to adjusts its position to reflect the light off their scales in a blinding display. All creatures in a 30-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 33 Fortitude saving throw or become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round. --- **Speed** 40 feet, swim 30 feet; Inflate **Melee** jaws +27 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+14 piercing **Melee** tail +27 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning **Melee** foot +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning **Drain Water** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The quai dau to is within 10 feet of a body of water that's at least 10 feet deep and their water sac is empty; **Effect** The quai dau to sucks water through their trunk to fill their water sac, lowering the level in the body of water by 10 feet. All creatures in the water within a 30-foot emanation are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of the quai dau to's next turn. **Inflate** The quai dau to inflates their body. They become Gargantuan, gain a fly Speed of 30 feet until the end of their next turn, and then [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312). They deflate if they fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) or Dismiss this effect. **Mist Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Requirements** The quai dau to's water sac is full; **Effect** The quai dau to empties their water sac to breathe out mist in a 10-foot emanation. All creatures within the mist become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and all creatures outside the mist become concealed to creatures within it. The mist dissipates after 1 round. **Spout Water** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Requirements** The quai dau to's water sac is full; **Effect** The quai dau to empties its water sac to blast water from its trunk, dealing 9d10 bludgeoning damage to all creatures in a 90-foot line, with a DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that fails is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet (or 20 feet on a critical failure).
Impossible Flight Despite the laws of the natural world, this large headed creature can fly. The quai dau to achieves this incredible feat by inflating itself, allowing it to seemingly swim through the air like water. While inflated in this way, the quai dau to somewhat resembles a pufferfish.
name: Quai Dau To
